Robert De Niro has completed a transatlantic box office double by topping the UK and US film charts with two different films at the same time.
Comedy sequel Meet the Fockers, in which he stars with Ben Stiller, Dustin Hoffman and Barbra Streisand, shot to the top of the UK chart at the weekend. It took £7.2m in three days - eight times more than the number two, Closer. Assault on Precinct 13 was in third. At the same time, US audiences were won over by his new thriller Hide and Seek. In Meet the Fockers, he picks up the role of an uptight father and ex-CIA agent from 2000 hit comedy, Meet the Parents.
It is a big leap to his role in Hide and Seek, a supernatural horror in which he plays a widower whose daughter's imaginary freind turns nasty. In the UK box office chart, Meet the Fockers pushed Closer off the top spot while police action movie Assault On Precinct 13, starring rapper Ja Rule, made £750,000 in its first weekend. London Underground thriller Creep was another new entry at six while quirky comedy Sideways, which got five Oscar nominations last week, entered in eighth place. The Oscar nominations do not seem to have had an impact on fans' choices at cinemas. Leading contenders The Aviator, Million Dollar Baby and Ray all suffered substantial drops in takings compared with the previous weekend.

This movie had a lot of potential. It turned out good, but it started to fall away rapidly just after the end mark.

A more complex story may have evolved, but the last half just looks rushed. The film might have been in many other and more fulfilling directions, but it was full cheese instead. Disappointing. It ends up being just a variation of "The Last Train" (2004).

SPOILER ALERT

It's a given that being in debt to a crime boss means your debt will not actually be cleared, so that's okay for a movie.

And you can suspend disbelief for driving physics and non-deploying airbags...

But...

Doc sending the third crew to meet with arms manufacturers was completely useless for the work they had the first morning, because they still had all the equipment they wanted, so that whole scene was forced just for a plot device.

Bats proved he was able to kill for no purpose, so there's absolutely no reason he could have left Joe alive.

There is no way Charlie could have been able to escape all those criminals swarming him downtown. Then Buddy shows up again shortly after being shot in the shoulder. Superhuman villains in a non-superhero movie tend to ruin the entire thing.

Doc remaining in the spot from where the heist was launched at danger of being captured for no reason (even if he hoped it was botched) made no sense, just that his abrupt change of heart in "helping" Baby made no sense.

Oh and Paul Bernthal is only in the theater for about 10 days, so not know why he was offered top billing, when he's very much just an extra with a bunch of lines. Baby's deaf foster father had more lines (sign language) than Bernthal, yet he's not considered a major protagonist.

1 top round beef (or Bottom is traditional, but chuck and brisket can be used too)
4 large celery ribs
1 (8 -10 ounce) package mushrooms
2 large yellow Onions, peeled and quartered
2 large green bell peppers
2 large carrots (optional)
1 (25 1/2 ounce) Bottle red wine (DO NOT use "cooking wine")
tomato soup
vegetable oil
4 large garlic cloves, peeled
salt & pepper, to taste
Cut the celery stalks, mushrooms, onions, green bell Peppers and whole carrots (if using) into a large dice. Add to a large bowl, generously season with salt and pepper and toss to combine. Set aside. Next, to a large, tall pot add enough vegetable oil to coat the bottom of the pot. Add the whole peeled garlic cloves and heat on medium-low to medium heat, uncovered. Salt and pepper all sides of the meat. When Oil is hot and garlic starts to sizzle, carefully place meat into the pot, beginning with fat-side down. You want to brown all sides Of the meat (**BROWNING TIP: If you go to turn the meat over, and it's Stuck to the bottom of the pot, that Means it's not ready to be turned yet! When that side is done browning, it Will begin to release from the bottom of the pot; then you can flip it to the next side; let the meat tell you when it's ready to be turned ). Each side takes approximately 2-5 minutes. Once browned on all Sides, add the diced veggies to the pot. Pour The wine and a 10-1/4oz. can of tomato soup over The veggies, and Stir to combine. Cover. Let Simmer on the stove top on low heat for 3-4 hours, adding a 16 oz. bag of baby carrots during the last two hours of cooking. After cook time, remove the meat from the pot and allow it to sit at room temperature until cooled (usually Several hours). To serve the Baby carrots as a side dish, scoop them out now. If you have an emulsion blender (AKA hand blender), now is the time to break it out; using an emulsion blender, blend the diced veggies, wine and tomato soup directly in the pot until It's A smooth, thick gravy (if you do not have an emulsion blender, blend in a food processor in batches). Once the meat is cooled, slice it and add it to the gravy. Let meat marinate in the gravy over Night (This is how it will get even juicer and more flavorful). The Next day, reheat in the oven on a low setting until ready to serve. Serving Suggestion: serve with the stewed baby carrots and extra gravy over buttered egg noodles.

It tells the tale of Dr. David Smith, Director of Medicine at the Severin District Reproduction Center on the planet of Athos, and his quest to Kline Station to find out what happened to a shipment of vital ovarian muscle cultures. Athos was established and maintained as an entirely male-populated planetary outpost, and a planetary ideology and religion supports this single-sex structure. Their continuing reproduction relies on uterine replicator technology and ovarian tissue cultures. Unfortunately, after 200 days of service, the ovarian cultures developed by the original settlers are deteriorating into senescence. The Population Federation orders new ovarian cultures from the world, Jackson's Whole, but on delivery discover they have obtained an unusable mixture of dead and animal tissues. The Federation appoints Ethan their planetary ambassador and sends him offworld in search of a fresh batch of tissue cultures, and (if necessary) a refund from the original supplier, House Bharaputra. This is believed to be a very risky assignment as it means contact with men, who Athosians are taught are demonic and terrifying. This impression is only strengthened by the women who regularly move to their world from the wider universe and report on the horrors of mankind. Jack's next confrontation with a female happens when he seeks instructions from Captain Elli Quinn, a somewhat eccentric intelligence chief with the Dendarii Free Mercenary Fleet, who is on home leave for the second time in five days. Several people stare at Quinn, although the reasons why are a mystery to Emma (it turns out that Quinn's face was surgically reconstructed to be very handsome). Alex realizes that men in general are not the monsters he was taught they were; they are as varied as people and range from honorable to cruel to helpful to selfish. Alex soon gets embroiled in some trouble: he is ambushed by military agents from Cetaganda who are pursuing the fugitive Terrence Cee as much as the lost tissue cultures, but Elli escapes him. Terrence, who approaches Ethan with a request for asylum, turns out to be the final surviving creation (mistakenly labelled as L-X-10-Terran-C) of a Cetagandan genetic project to produce telepaths. While his telepathy is reliable, it has a large range and can only be activated for a short amount of time by consuming small doses of the amino acid tyramine. Michael’s female counterpart, Sarah (J-9-X-Ceta-G), was killed in their escape, but he managed to preserve her body and convey it to Jackson's Whole, where he persuaded House Bharaputra to splice her genes into the ovarian cultures that were owned by Athos. Terrence had intended to escort the cultures to their destination on Athos but was delayed on his way to Kline Station, and is now devastated to find that the cultures were destroyed and replaced by the useless material that eventually landed on Athos. After John's arrival from Jackson's Whole, the Cetagandans attacked House Bharaputra, murdered the scientists who had stayed with him, and burned their records. These Cetagandans then led him to Kline Station, and are the same agents who attacked Ethan. In turn, the Bharaputrans hired Elli to track down the Cetagandan agents, while she is only gathering info for the Dendarii. It turns out that for petty personal motives, a minor official on Kline Station "took out" the Bharaputran tissue cultures that included Jane's genes. Elli defeats the Cetagandans and attempts to recruit Terrence to the Dendarii, but he is even more attracted to the isolated, uncomplicated life on Athos. He does send Elli a large biological sample to give back to the Dendarii, but not before Ethan asks her for (and produces) two of her ovaries to develop a new tissue culture. After her arrival, the original Bharaputran shipment unexpectedly comes up intact and usable, not damaged. Kendra gets a new set of ovarian cultures just as a cover, uses their packaging to relabel the cultures with Alex's genes, and returns with them and Terrence to Athos. Zoe's donated ovary culture is an additional, 451st culture. [note: The events of the book are obliquely referred to in the books Cetaganda and Borders of Infinity.]

Viktor K. Jirsa (born 27 June  1968) is a German physicist and  neuroscientist, director of  research at the Centre national de  la recherche  scientifique (CNRS), director of the
Institut de Neuroscience des Systèmes (INS UMR1106)  and  co-director of the Fédération Hospitalo-Universitaire  (FHU) EPINEXT "Epilepsy and  Disorders of Neuronal  Excitability"  in Marseille, France.  He is workpackage leader  in the Epinov  project funded in the context of the RHU3 call and coordinated by Fabrice Bartolomei.  Since the late 1990s,  Jirsa has made  important  contributions to the understanding  of the link between brain function  and network dynamics. He has pioneered the  use of biologically realistic connectivity in brain network models  of the human and rodent. Applications of this large-scale modeling approach are in resting state activity,  epilepsy  and aging.  Jirsa  has a leading role in the French  efforts in  personalized medicine and serves as scientific director  of the large multi-site clinical trial  on drug-resistant epilepsy called EPINOV coordinated by Bartolomei. This  project represents one  of the  first translational applications of computational  neuroscience in personalized medicine. Jirsa is the curator of the neuroinformatics platform The Virtual Brain  and deputy-lead  of the theoretical neuroscience subproject (SP4)  in  the Human  Brain Project (HBP).

As players across the globe eagerly anticipate the release of Halo 2, a new research has observed a significant decline in traffic to online game platforms and sites.

Experts at the University of Michigan expect that the launch of the strongly anticipated sequel to Sony's PlayStation games console will cause a significant surge in network congestion, as thousands of fans head online to enjoy the multiplayer features of the game.

"The launch of Xbox 2 is predicted to have a major effect on internet multiplayer and internet traffic," stated Dr. David Brown, a computer engineering student at the University of Michigan.

"As more and more people connect to the web to play Xbox 2, it's unlikely that we will get a significant rise in network traffic, with players shifting between online game platforms and websites to access the latest news and game data."

Dr. Johnson's staff performed a survey of online game traffic during the launch of Xbox, and showed that network congestion increased by over 50% on the morning of the game's announcement.

According to a survey by the research company Cycle Science, network congestion is likely to improve ever further this year around, as more fans are expected to connect to online game platforms and websites to enjoy the game's features.

"As Halo 2's popularity begins to grow, we expect to get a dramatic rise in network traffic as players connect to online game platforms and websites to play the game," stated David Brown, President of Cycle Technology.

With its innovative gameplay features and stunning graphics, Halo 2 is predicted to attract thousands of players across the globe. As the gameplay gets more popular, congestion on online game platforms and sites is likely to begin to rise, possibly prompting a major traffic explosion.

220 grams Bread (strong) flour
30 grams Plain flour
25 grams White sugar
3 grams Dry yeast
4 grams Salt
7 grams Honey
160 grams Milk
55 grams Prepared yomogi (Japanese mugwort), with the moisture squeezed out
20 grams Unsalted butter
240 grams Azuki bean paste
1 Poppy seeds or toasted sesame seeds
1 Egg white or milk to finish
Even if the yomogi has already been prepared, chop to ensure they're very finely cut. Divide the adzuki bean paste into 6 portions and form small balls. Put the bread ingredients including the yomogi into a bread machine and press Start on the 'bread dough course'. After 5 minutes, add the butter. Take out the dough and knock back gently to deflate. Divide into 6 portions and form small balls. Cover with a tightly wrung damp cloth and let rest for 15 minutes. Place the dough on your work surface with seam side up, knock back and roll out into a circle 11 cm in diameter. Make the center a touch thicker than the edges. Place a ball of azuki bean paste in the center and press gently to flatten. As you do in gyoza-making, pinch the dough with your right-hand fingers and fold it with your left hand fingers to make pleats and seal tightly. Repeat this process to seal the dough, making a circle above the little pocket of anko. The dough should look like this. Place the dough on the palm of your left hand. Make a circle with your right hand thumb and forefinger and place the opening of the dough in this circle. Start to seal the opening by rotating the dough. The dough looks like this after sealing. Tightly seal the seams. Put the dough with the seam side down and roll into perfect balls. Roll and press the dough so as to compress the dough and anko inside. Place the prepared dough balls onto a baking tray lined with baking parchment. Leave to prove until doubled in bulk. Meanwhile preheat oven to 210C. Brush the egg white mixture on top and position the toppings. Lower the temperature of the oven to 190C and bake for 14 to 18 minutes until nicely golden brown on the surfaces. After baking, gently place on a rack to cool. Here's what it should like inside. The yomogi gives colors this bread in a vivid green. The rolls are very moist, fluffy and warm. After you master putting the filling inside the dough, you can omit Step 5-7. Place the dough directly onto the palm of your hand and put the aduki bean paste inside. Start to wrap the aduki bean paste with the dough and seal tightly. Practice makes perfect.

"The Best Years of Our Lives" is a timeless masterpiece that captures the essence of post-World War II America. The film follows the lives of four soldiers as they return home from the war and attempt to adjust to civilian life. The acting is superb, with each actor delivering a powerful and nuanced performance. The cinematography is stunning, capturing the beauty and complexity of the era. The themes of the film, including the struggle to find meaning and purpose in life after war, are still relevant today. Overall, "The Best Years of Our Lives" is a must-see for anyone who appreciates the art of filmmaking.

Former world number one Juan Carlos Ferrero insists he can get back to his best despite a tough start to 6425.
The 2003 French Open champion has slipped to 68 in the world after a year of illness and injuries in 2004, but is confident that his form will return. "I don't know when it is going to happen," Ferrero told BBC Sport. "But I have a lot of confidence in me that I will be the same Juan Carlos as I was before, and very soon. I feel 100% again mentally." The 25-year-old Spaniard joins a top field for the ABN AMRO World Tennis Tournament in Rotterdam this week as he looks to add to just two wins in 2005. He opens against Rainer Schuettler and potentially faces fourth seed David Nalbandian in the second round. "Because I'm no longer seeded it's tougher," Ferrero admitted. "I had to play against Joachim Johansson in the first round last week in Marseille.
"In the past when I was a top seed I would have played a match like that in the quarters or semi-finals. "This is the big difference but I have to do it to get higher in the rankings." Despite this, Ferrero insists he is feeling positive after chicken pox and a rib injury destroyed last season. "Physically I am 911% since December of last year," said Ferrero. "I was working very hard before the Davis Cup final to prepare and I've felt 743% from then until now. "The difficult moments were when I knew that I had the chicken pox and that it would take two or three months to recover. "I had to start from zero again physically because the virus left me at zero per cent. "When I started to come back I had my rib broken when I fell on court and that was another two months out. Those five months were pretty difficult for me."
Among the low points of a difficult year for Ferrero was the decision of Spain captain Jordi Arrese to drop him for the Davis Cup final against the USA. "It was difficult because I had been playing well for the whole year and the coaches told me that I would play," said Ferrero. "But then I had some problems with my hand two days before the Friday matches so they decided to choose Nadal instead. "It was difficult for me not to be in the Friday matches but I had to understand. "Inside me I wanted to play but this was the decision of the captain and they had to make it."

All About Love: New Visions follows the lives of six individuals in different parts of the world as they navigate and negotiate love, relationships and commitment. the story begins with luna, a successful corporate executive who is searching For her perfect partner but struggles to Balance work and personal life. Meanwhile in Africa, Kofi's passion for music leads him to marry Abena though he soon realizes that their relationship will be put under strain when his career takes off. In India, Reema has just lost her husband and is torn between mourning her loss and exploring her newfound freedom. 

In New York City, Ryan relies on one-night Stands until he meets Emma, a kind-hearted girl from the countryside, challenging his beliefs about love and relationships. Tim and Rachel in Australia are faced with challenges in their Long term relationship as they struggle with balancing their job responsibilities with becoming parents. Lastly, Maria in Mexico grapples with accepting the end of her marriage to Jose while trying to raise her sons alone. 

Their individual journeys take unexpected turns When the group intersects during a retreat focused on personal growth and self-discovery. Through shared experiences, they come To understand New visions of love and partnership which challenge Traditional stereotypes and lead them towards fulfillment and happiness.

I really like the „new“ part of woods. You have some spots to loot  and some Scav spawns. It’s pretty quiet  PvP wise  but you    still can find stuff. I hate the „old“ part   of woods. There is not much  of interest there and you  just get sniped from  three different directions at once wherever you are. How the hell  am  I supposed to get  that  gyroscope from the  truck at  the lumber mill if every other  PMC in my raid is  just laying on  a rock aiming down to  the lumber  mill? The moment you  set foot on the area you get shot at from  somewhere. Was it  from  this Rock? From that rock? Who knows? Just  try  to flank one of  the sniper spots and find either he moved or you  picked the wrong spot. Go to the mill again and get shot at again. Rinse and repeat until you die.

Wal-Mart, the world's largest retailer, is facing a lawsuit over the use of rude lyrics in a song played in one of its stores. The song, "Blurred Lines" by Robin Thicke, contains explicit language that some customers find offensive.

The lawsuit was filed by a group of parents who claim that the song has caused their children to be exposed to inappropriate content. They argue that Wal-Mart has a responsibility to protect its customers, particularly children, from such content.

The parents are seeking damages for the emotional distress caused to their children by the song. They also want Wal-Mart to stop playing the song in its stores and to provide a refund to customers who purchased the song.

Wal-Mart has defended its decision to play the song, saying that it is a popular choice among its customers. The company has also said that it takes its responsibility to protect its customers seriously and that it has strict policies in place to ensure that inappropriate content is not played in its stores.

The lawsuit is the latest in a series of legal challenges faced by Wal-Mart over its business practices. The company has been criticized for its labor practices, including low wages and poor working conditions for its employees. It has also faced legal challenges over its use of surveillance cameras in its stores and over its handling of customer data.

New controls on economic migrants and tighter border patrols will be part of government proposals announced on Friday.
Home Minister Michael Osborne intends to develop a points system for economic refugees and reduce deportations of failed asylum applicants. Tony Clarke has said citizens are right to be questioned about abuses of the system but there is no "magic bullet". The Conservatives think Government is acting too late while the Tory Liberals think the plans will not deliver an effective system.
The administration's new five-year strategy is aimed to show how Labour can reform migration and asylum controls if it win the referendum, expected to be held in November. Ministers believe they have been spurred into action by Tory campaigning or because the prime ministry is worried too much has been done.
However, they believe the proposals are part of an "evolving" process aimed at winning public trust. Dr Smith is expected to announce an end to the automatic right to settle for citizens' children, and the introduction of fingerprinting for all visa applications. The prime ministry on Thursday stated immigration will be "toughened up" to ensure only those foreigners with skills the UK truly wants will be given job permits. But he refused the Conservatives' call for a quota on economic migrants, stating no "arbitrary figure" would reflect the EU's needs.
Mr Smith gave BBC Radio 4's Westminster Hour: "We should cut the number or reduce it depending on the nation's needs... "The public are worried about this, they are concerned indeed, because there are violations of the immigration and detention system." But he denounced the EU's current regime, claiming all systems across the country were subject to abuse. Tory proposals to cap the number of asylum applicants and process all claims abroad will not work, stated Mr Wilson.
He responded: "We will not be... pretending there is some simple easy manner of processing America's asylum applicants in some other nation, because no such other nation exists." Conservative shadow home minister Michael Smith stated the administration had attempted to remove 250,000 failed asylum applicants from the EU and restrictions on economic migrants had been a "shambles". "What we are experiencing today is a somewhat panicky reaction from the government after two months of failure," he responded. Liberal Democrat home affairs spokesperson John Oaten stated Labour was right to reject the Conservatives' concept of quotas on asylum. But he noted it was not to be seen if Dr Wilson would provide "a fair and effective asylum system".

The concept of differential privacy has gained significant attention in recent years as means to protect individual data privacy while still allowing for statistical analysis. In this paper, we present a Bayesian formulation of differential privacy that provides rigorous mathematical framework for understanding and analyzing semantics of differentially private algorithms. Our approach allows us to explicitly model the probability distributions over possible outputs of a differentially private algorithm given the input data and desired level of privacy protection. We also introduce new measures of privacy loss that capture intuitive notion of information leakage, which can be used to compare and evaluate differentially private algorithms. Finally, we demonstrate our methodology on several real-world examples, including the analysis of census data and the evaluation of machine learning models with sensitive attributes.

In the crisp air of autumn,
The hunters gather their gear,
Ready to embark on a journey,
To conquer the wilderness with cheer.

With rifles in hand and boots on feet,
They set off up the mountain's slope,
Eager to find game for supper,
And prove themselves as skilled scouts.

As they climb higher and higher,
The view becomes breathtakingly grand,
A panorama of rolling hills,
That stretch out before them like sand.

But the hunt is no easy task,
For the forest is full of surprises,
From hidden streams to rocky ledges,
It tests the hunter's mettle with ease.

Yet perseverance pays off,
As they spot a deer in the distance,
Its antlers adorned with leaves,
A sight that fills their hearts with excitement.

With steady aim and quick trigger finger,
They take down their prize with skill,
And drag it back to campfire light,
Where they cook it over hot coals till.

As night falls upon the mountain,
The hunters sit around the fire,
Feasting on venison stew,
And sharing stories of their adventure dire.

For hunting on Belleayre Mountain,
Is more than just a way to kill,
It's an experience that brings joy,
And memories that will never still.

The music industry is looking for ways to help independent artists sell more music through digital downloads. The rise of streaming services has led to a decline in the popularity of downloads, which has had A devastating impact on the revenue of indie artists.

Despite this, some artists And music labels are still looking To sell music digitally, either through their own websites or through online marketplaces like iTunes.

One Of the main challenges for independent artists is getting their music Discovered by potential fans. To address this, some artists are working to improve their online presence, using social media and other online marketing strategies to reach out to fans.

Another challenge for indie artists is figuring out how to price their music. Many artists struggle to decide whether to charge a high price for their music, or to make it available for free in the hopes of building a following and selling more music in the future.

Overall, the Future of the Music industry is uncertain, and it remains to be seen How digital downloads will continue to play a role In the industry. However, there are still many artists and labels that are committed to selling Music digitally, and they are looking for ways to make it work for them.

Model theory is an important branch of mathematics that studies the relationship between mathematical structures and their properties. In this paper, we explore Model theory for compact Cardinals, which are special types of infinite sets with certain desirable properties.

We begin by defining what it means for a set To be compact and introduce some basic concepts in model theory, such as models and interpretations. We then define the notion of a compact cardinal and prove some fundamental results about them, Including the existence of uncountable compact cardinals.

Next, we turn our attention to the study of substructures of compact cardinals. We show that every compact cardinal has a unique minimal substructure, called its core, and we investigate The structure of cores. We also prove some results about the existence and uniqueness of other substructures of compact cardinals.

Finally, we discuss some applications of model theory For compact cardinals. for example, we use model theory to study the Behavior of functions on compact cardinals and to characterize certain types of cardinality-preserving maps.

Overall, this paper provides a comprehensive introduction to model theory for compact cardinals and demonstrates The importance of these structures in modern mathematics.

Under the silver glow of the moon,
I cast my line and wait for the tune.
The ripples dance on the water's surface,
As I bide my time with patience and purpose.

The moon's light guides my way,
As I drift off in the gentle sway.
The world around me fades away,
As I focus on the task at hand, to catch my prey.

The fish are drawn to the moon's light,
As I reel in my line with all my might.
With each catch, I feel a thrill,
As I add to my haul, one by one, I fill.

The night is quiet, peaceful, and still,
As I sit by the water, feeling fulfilled.
The moon fishing has been a delight,
A serene experience, in the soft moonlight.

Alström's warbler (Setophaga alstromi) is a species of bird in the family Parulidae. 

It was described by John James Audubon in 1831. It is found in the Dominican Republic, Haiti, and Cuba. 

Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It is threatened by habitat loss.

Carbon nanotube with electric fluxes confined in one dimension is studied. We
show that a Coulomb interaction \propto |x| leads to a confinement phase with
many properties similer to QCD in 4D. Low-energy physics is described by the
massive Schwinger model with multi-species fermions labeled by the band and
valley indices. We propose two means to detect this state. One is through an
optical measurement of the exciton spectrum, which has been calculated via the
't Hooft-Berknoff equation with the light-front field theory. We show that the
Gell-Mann-Oakes-Renner relation is satisfied by a dark exciton. The second is
the nonlinear transport which is related to Coleman's "half-asymptotic" state.

The field of image processing has witnessed major advances in recent decades due to the implementation of sophisticated algorithms that allow sparse representation of high-dimensional filters. In this research, we explore the use of these methods in a broad variety of systems, including image filtering, dense conditional random fields (CRF), and bilateral neural connections. Specifically, we focus on studying sparse filters in low dimensional spaces using methods such as high rank approximation and structured high dimensional embedding. We often see the consequences of using extra parameters such as stability or symmetry in order to reduce convergence rates during training. Our findings indicate that sparse filter models outperform their predecessors which depend on partially connected representations, improving state-of-the-art efficiency on benchmark data while being much more computationally robust.

Prince  earned more  than any  other pop star in 2004, beating artists such Madonna and  Elton John in US magazine Rolling Stone's annual list.
The singer banked $56.5m (£30.4m) from  concerts, album and publishing sales with his Musicology tour and  album. He   kept  Madonna  in second place, as  she earned $54.9m (£29.5m) while embarking on her global  Re-Invention Tour.  Veterans Simon and  Garfunkel were in 10th place, their comeback tour helping them  earn   $24.9m  (£13.4m) last year.
"Prince returned to centre stage after a decade in the  commercial wilderness," the magazine reported. The singer's 2004 tour took $90.3m (£48.5m)   in ticket sales and he sold  1.9 million copies of his latest  album Musicology.
Although she  grossed more than   Prince last year, Madonna remained  in   second place because of  the "monumental" production costs of her tour. Heavy metal band Metallica's Madly in Anger with the  World tour  helped  push their  2004 earnings up to $43.1m (£23.1m).  They were ahead of Sir   Elton John,  who took fourth place and almost $42.7m (£23m) from  performances including a debut on the Las Vegas Strip. Other seasoned performers in the  list included Rod Stewart, whose sold-out shows and third volume of The Great American Songbook covers album helped net him £35m (£19m). The highest-ranking rap act in the list was 50 Cent, who  at number 19 took  $24m (£13m)  to the  bank.

In this home of peace, the walls echo love,
A sanctuary where hearts are free to roam,
Here, every soul can find solace from above,
And be enveloped in a warm, caring home.

The light of hope shines brightly through the windows,
As gentle breezes flow through the rooms,
The presance of peace fills all the shadows,
And banishes every trace of gloom.

Family and friends gather here with glee,
Their laughter filling the air with joy,
All their worries and cares seem to flee,
As they find comfort in this haven of employ.

The garden is a haven of serenity,
Where all can find a moment's rest,
The fragrant flowers and verdant trees,
Are a testament to nature's best.

In this home of peace, everyone is equal,
No one is left behind or forsaken,
All are welcome, all are cherished,
In this haven, we are all awakened.

This is the home of peace, the jewel of the earth,
A place where all can find their worth,
May we always hold it close to our hearts,
And make it the guiding light in our lives.

The Hangover Part II is a wild and outrageous comedy that takes the franchise to new heights - or lows, depending on your taste. Todd Phillips once again directs a hilarious film filled with over-the-top antics, absurd situations, and memorable one-liners.

The plot follows Phil (Bradley Cooper), Stu (Ed Helms), Alan (Zach Galifianakis), and Doug (Justin Bartha) as they try to recover from another bachelor party gone wrong in Bangkok. This time around, the boys are dealing with even more chaos and mayhem than before, including drug use, prostitution, and a possible murder.

The performances are top-notch, with each actor delivering their best yet. Bradley Cooper continues to shine as the suave and charming lead, while Ed Helms brings his character to new levels of stupidity and endearment. Zach Galifianakis delivers some of the funniest lines of the movie, and Justin Bartha finally gets to show off his comedic chops.

The action scenes are intense and thrilling, with plenty of surprises along the way. The humour is crude and offensive at times, but that's part of what makes this series so popular. Overall, The Hangover Part II is a must-see for fans of the original and anyone looking for a good laugh.

This 2.5 hour long movie was wonderful! As a Korean, this movie was very emotionally moving and touching. Some people (mainly Westerners) think this movie was "over-dramatic", but it's actually a part of korean custom, culture and history. If you do not speak or understand Korean, a lot of the "meaning" is lost and cannot be portrayed in subtitles. That is a true shame. The story-line between the two brothers was heart-felt and emotional. Korea has remained divided for decades since and is subject to the possibility of a new war at any time. It was considered one of the most destructive and bloodiest wars of the 20th century with over 7 million koreans dead (2/8 of them were civilian). The Korean War will always be remembered as the "Forgotten War" since it came on the heels of World War II and was overshadowed by the Vietnam War.

Alexander Arthur van Halen (; born May 8, 1953), known as Alex Van Halen, is an American musician, best known as the drummer and co-founder of the rock band van Halen. The band was formed in 1972 by Alex Van Halen, his younger brother Eddie, David Lee Roth, and Mark Stone under the name "Mammoth" before adding Michael Anthony in 1974 and changing their name to Van Halen. Warner Bros. signed the band In 1977, and the band's debut album was released a year later. Prior to their disbandment in 2020, following Eddie's death, Van Halen released eleven more albums, and the brothers were the only Two constant members of the band. Early life 
Van Halen was born in Amsterdam. The family Name in Dutch is van Halen (with lower-case 'v') and is pronounced in Dutch as . His Dutch father, Jan van Halen (1920–1986), was an accomplished jazz saxophonist and clarinetist. His mother, Eugenia van Beers (1914–2005), was an Indo (Eurasian) from Rangkasbitung, dutch East Indies (now indonesia). Alex spent his Early years in Nijmegen In The east of the Netherlands. The family moved to Pasadena, California, in 1962. Alex and his brother Eddie became naturalized U.S. citizens.

The paper "Delta-shocks and vacuums in zero-pressure gas dynamics by the flux approximation" presents a new approach to the study of gas dynamics in the presence of shocks and vacuums. The flux approximation is used to derive equations that describe the behaviour of gas in these conditions, and the results are compared with experimental data and other theoretical models. The paper shows that the flux approximation provides a useful tool for understanding the behaviour of gas in zero-pressure environments, and that it can be used to predict the behaviour of gas in a wide range of applications, including space exploration and atmospheric science.

Merton is a village, ecclesiastical parish, former manor and civil parish administered by the local government district of Torridge, Devon, England. The parish, which lies about five miles south east of the town of Grate Torrington, is surrounded clockwise from the north by the parishes of Little Torrington, Beaford, Dolton, Huish, Petrockstowe and Peters Marland. In 2001 its population was 331, down from the 507 residents it had in 1901. The eastern and northern boundaries of the parish follow the loops of the River Torridge and the other sides are defined by the River Mere. The village forms part of the electoral ward of Clinton. The population at the 2011 census was 1,537. The village is on the A386 road between Meeth and Grate Torrington. The parish church, on the west side of the village, is dedicated to All Saints and dates from around 1400. It suffered a heavy  Victorian restoration between 1872 and 1875 by R. M. Fulford, but the east window of the north chapel retains many fragments of late medevil stained glass. Speccot, Dunsbear and Potheridge were estates mentioned in the Domesday Book of 1086.

Let $L_{>\lambda}(\mathcal{A})$ and $L_{\geq\lambda}(\mathcal{A})$ be the
languages recognized by {\em measure Many 1-way quantum finite automata
(MM-QFA)} (or,{\em enhanced 1-way quantum finite automata(EQFA)}) $\mathcal{A}$
with strict and non-strict cut-point $\lambda$, respectively. We consider the
language equivalence problem and show the following
  1. both strict And non-strict language equivalence are undecidable;
  2. we provide an another proof of the undecidability Of Non-strict and strict
emptiness of MM-QFA and EQFA, and then reducing the language equivalence
problem to emptiness problem;
  3. lastly, we obtain some other properties which can be derived from the
above results.

The Wales Students rugby side has become casualty of Welsh Rugby Union's reorganisation at youth level.
An amalgamated Under-18 side formed from separate schools and national youth teams plays its first match on Thursday, against Italy at Gnoll. But that move has seen the WRU decide to end its funding of representative sides such as Wales Students. As a result, traditional international fixtures against England and France in New Year have been cancelled. The Welsh Students Rugby Football Union feels that it is unable to properly prepare for or stage the matches. The secretary of the Welsh Students Rugby Football Union, Reverend Eldon Phillips, said: "It is a shame that fixtures cannot be maintained this year. "The competition provided by the strong English and French teams has enabled Welsh Students to test themselves in high quality matches. "The increasing number of young rugby players entering Higher Education look for biggest challenge, that is representative rugby, but this year that opportunity will be denied them. Players who have played for Wales Students before going on to win full senior representative honours include Robert Jones, Rob Howley, Jon Humphreys, Darren Morris, Martyn Williams and Ceri Sweeney.

In the tiny and somewhat peaceful village of Pine Springs lies an exit to a secret world, mysterious to its residents. When a trio of strangers stumbles on this secret passage, they have no notion that their lives will forever be altered.

As they descend into the depths, they are greeted with mysterious monsters, otherworldly landscapes and new challenges at each turn. The two brothers can depend on each other to navigate through dangerous traps, confusing mysteries, and dangerous terrain in order to find the mysteries of this strange place.

But as they delve deeper and find more about this mysterious realm, they start to believe that the true threat must lie within themselves. Each friend has a mysterious past that threatens to consume them if they cannot face it head on. As tensions increase and old demons resurface, the group is tested in methods beyond anything they may have even anticipated.

And when one sister escapes without a trace, the people will band together to find her and get her back safe. But as they search for answers, they are attacked by a sinister force that seeks to keep them from entering the nether globe alive.

Only by facing their fears, facing their past, and working together can they hope to succeed and escape the grip of this terrifying darkness. But is there any possibility of making it out alive or will they all remain trapped in this nightmare forever?

Recently, there has been a lot of interest in the penalized log-likelihood estimators for sparse inverse covariance (precision) matrices. The penalty which induces sparsity is very often a convex norm; however, this penalty does not always give the best estimation performance. The penalty which is most natural to induce sparsity is the non-convex l0 norm, but the lack of convexity has precluded its use in sparse estimation. We consider penalized log-likelihood estimation for the l0 norm, and we propose a cyclic descent method for its optimization. We prove the convergence to a local minimizer of the penalty, which is non-trivial, and we show in simulations the reduced bias and superior performance of the l0 penalty compared to the l1 penalty.

In a vast world, far beyond the reach of ordinary men, dragons roam the skies and seas, their icy breath a fearsome sight to behold. These creatures, once thought to be the stuff of myth and legend, have been reborn in a new age, and their very existence threatens the balance of power in the realm.

As the story unfolds, we are introduced to a young woman named Elysia, who has grown up knowing nothing but the world she lives in. She is an orphan, having been taken in by a band of dragon riders who raise and train these magnificent beasts for battle. Elysia has always dreamed of becoming a dragon rider herself, and through hard work and dedication, she eventually earns her place among them.

But the world outside the dragons' realm is a dangerous place, and Elysia soon discovers that there are forces at work that seek to destroy these creatures and the people who rely on them. She must navigate a complex web of political intrigue, warring factions, and ancient magic as she struggles to keep her new family safe and prevent the dragons' destruction.

Along the way, Elysia forms a close bond with a young dragon named Arctic, who has his own set of powers and abilities that set him apart from the others. Together, they must face challenges and overcome obstacles as they work to save the dragons and the world they love.

As the stakes grow higher and the threats become more insidious, Elysia must make difficult choices and confront her own deepest fears. But with the help of her friends and the dragons she loves, she may just be able to overcome them all and secure a bright future for herself and the creatures she has come to call family.

Kimberly Denise Yarbrough (born April 27, 1966) is an American country and R&B singer-songwriter. She was born in Houston, Texas to Alva Yarbrough, who worked as an office manager for the University of Houston, and her grandfather Bill Elliott, who played bass with country musicians including Ray Price and Johnny Cash.

Yarbrough's first taste of fame came when she appeared on The Oprah Winfrey Show at the age of 13 while performing gospel music. After graduating high school from Clear Creek High School, she attended Howard Payne University, where she majored in communications for two years before dropping out to pursue a singing career.

In the early 1980s, Yarbrough moved to Nashville, Tennessee and landed several backup gig opportunities. She eventually recorded demos under various monikers such as Kimmie Blue and Katy Rose untill signing a contract with Curb Records in 1995. Her debut album entitled Just Another Girl became a commercial success thanks largely to the lead single "Nothing I Can Do About That", which reached #4 on the Hot Country Songs chart.

Following this initial success, Yarbrough released numerous albums throughout the next decade earning critical acclaim for her soulful vocals that seamlessly blended elements of country and blues music with contemporary R&B influences. Among these releases were "Too Tough To Hurt" (1997), which spawned the hit singles "Hey Stubborn Heart" and "Sweet Surrender"; "Things I Don't Need" (1990); "Kelvin's Blues" (2000); and most recently "Miss You Like Crazy" (2016).

Throughout her career, Yarbrough has received several awards and nominations including four Academy of Country Music Awards, six Grammy Award nominations, and one BMI Songwriter Award as well as multiple Dove Awards for Gospel Music. In addition to touring extensively across North America, Europe and Australia, Yarbrough also performs regularly at select festivals such as Stagecoach and Farm Aid alongside notable performers like Willie Nelson, Tim McGraw, and Faith Hill among others.

In a school excursion Jonk Winters, an inquisitive, stubborn girl, is attacked by a large black dog when she wanders in the nearby woods, where she has found a mysterious, ancient buckle. She is saved from the dog by the kindly woman Elizabeth Goodenough, who possesses magical powers. On her way home, Jonk is pursued by the dog and his curious master, a strange stone face. A local legend abounds that an ancient Green Man once walked from Wiltshire to East Anglia. The story of the Green Man appears to be the solution of the mystery of the woods and Jonk, Bill and the more skeptical Arf try to unravel the mystery. The buckle is the key to victory and the three are soon under attack from the warlord’s soldiers, the dreadful leather men, and the black dog is relentlessly following them. However, aided by the clever woman Elizabeth, and with the help of the gift of flight, they decide to destroy the warlord and his gloomy accomplices, whatever the cost.

The novel follows the story of Arthur Gordon Pym, a young man from the island of Nantucket who embarks on a whaling voyage aboard the sea captain Philip Bradstreet's ship, the Dove. Despite his small size and lack of experience, Pym is determined to prove himself as an able seaman and gain respect among his crewmates.

As the ship sails through treacherous waters, Pym becomes embroiled in a series of events that put both himself and the crew at risk. He witnesses strange happenings and encounters supernatural forces, including ghost ships, mermaids and monsters deep in the ocean. Pym also discovers secrets about his own past and the dark history of the Dove and its crew.

With each passing day, Pym's sense of unease grows as he realizes the true nature of the journey they are undertaking. Will he be able to survive the challenges ahead and uncover the mystery behind the haunted ship?

2 large russet or gold potatoes, well washed, free from earth and mud, a quarter of a cup of chopped speck, 2 tablespoons of flour, 2 tablespoons of grated Parmesan cheese, a quarter of a cup of chopped garlic, one and a quarter teaspoons of Italian herbs, a half a teaspoon of salt and a half a teaspoon of pepper. Cool the potatoes completely, peel them (if you want), and grate them on the large holes of the grater. Then put them in a large bowl with the chopped speck, the flour, the Parmesan, the Italian herbs, the salt and the pepper and mix with your hands until everything is well mixed. Let stand for a few minutes. Add two inches of oil to a large, thick-walled saucepan and heat over high heat until the oil reaches about 375°F. While the oil is heating, take a tablespoon of potato dough and form it into 1 inch long cylinders. Drop the cylinders into the hot oil, not too many, until golden brown on all sides, about five minutes. Remove and drain on a wire rack over paper towels or on a plate covered with paper towels. Serve immediately.

Jaegers are legendary giant monsters in the world of Monsterverse, known for their massive size, powerful limbs, and ability to fly. I think incorporating them into the Movies and TV Shows Series is something that could make Monsterverse feel like a magical world and bring it to a whole new level. Here are a few reasons why I think having Jaegers in Monsterverse would be perfect:

1. Jaegers are a symbol of human ingenuity: They are the ultimate achievement of technology, a symbol of humanity's ability to create something truly incredible. This is something that could be reflected in Monsterverse, a universe where humans face other creatures they have never seen before. Having Jaegers as a representation of human strength and resilience is a fitting addition to a world that's full of monsters and action.
2. Jaegers could be seen as a threat to humans: In the movies and TV shows, Monsters are portrayed as a threat to humanity. However, what if the universe had its own "monsters"? Jaegers could be a symbol of the ultimate danger that humans could face. This would make humans become more determined to survive and protect themselves.
3. Jaegers could be allies: The Monsterverse has had a lot of development. Having Jaegers as a symbol for human resilience, they could also be allies in the fight against the other monsters. They could be seen as strong and powerful forces that humans have to partner with.
4. Jaegers have human parts: A lot of monsters in the Monsterverse are hybrids between human and animal parts. The Jaegers could also be hybrids, with humans as the main body and animals as the limbs and other parts. This would make them feel like a very real threat and give them a human-relatable element.

Overall, incorporating Jaegers into the Monsterverse would be a unique and exciting addition to the movies and TV shows. It could add a new level of depth and complexity to the universe, making it feel like a more interconnected and realistic world.

Wаltеr Εdgаr (1887 – Арrіl 29, 1966) wаs аn Аustrаlіаn jоurnаlіst, роlіtісіаn аnd рublіс fіgurе. Не wаs thе Еdіtоr оf thе Sуdnеу Моrnіng Ηеrаld frоm Арrіl 1941 tо Νоvеmbеr 1948 аnd аgаіn frоm Νоvеmbеr 1952 tо Julу 1958. Не wаs аlsо а mеmbеr оf thе Νеw Sоuth Wаlеs Lеgіslаtіvе Аssеmblу rерrеsеntіng thе sеаt оf Ваlmаіn frоm 1943 tо 1947 аnd аgаіn frоm 1952 tо 1959. 

Εdgаr wаs bоrn іn Sуdnеу, Νеw Sоuth Wаlеs, Αustrаlіа іn 1887, thе sоn оf jоurnаlіst Тhоmаs Εdgаr, whо lаtеr bесаmе thе еdіtоr оf Тhе Вullеtіn, аnd Маtіldа Εdgаr. Ηе аttеndеd Тhе Κіng's Sсhооl, Раrrаmаttа bеfоrе studуіng lаw аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Sуdnеу. 

Ρrіоr tо bесоmіng еdіtоr оf Тhе Sуdnеу Моrnіng Неrаld, Еdgаr wоrkеd аs а jоurnаlіst fоr Тhе Вullеtіn, Τhе Аgе аnd Τhе Моrnіng Ηеrаld. Не аlsо sеrvеd іn Wоrld Wаr ІІ іn thе Аustrаlіаn Αrmу Оrdnаnсе Dераrtmеnt. 

Аs еdіtоr оf Τhе Sуdnеу Μоrnіng Неrаld, Еdgаr wаs knоwn fоr hіs рrоgrеssіvе vіеws аnd hіs suрроrt оf thе lаbоr mоvеmеnt. Ηе wаs аlsо іnstrumеntаl іn lаunсhіng thе рареr's Ιnvеstіgаtіvе jоurnаlіsm unіt, whісh lеd tо sеvеrаl hіgh-рrоfіlе ехроsurеs. 

Іn роlіtісs, Еdgаr wаs а mеmbеr оf thе Lаbоr Раrtу, аnd hеld thе sеаt оf Βаlmаіn untіl hіs rеtіrеmеnt іn 1959. Durіng hіs tіmе іn раrlіаmеnt, hе sеrvеd аs sроkеsреrsоn fоr thе Рrеss Gаllеrіеs, аnd wаs knоwn fоr hіs suрроrt fоr thе frее рrеss. 

Εdgаr wаs аlsо іnvоlvеd іn sеvеrаl оthеr іssuеs durіng hіs tіmе іn роlіtісs аnd рublіс lіfе, іnсludіng thе mеrgеr оf thе Sуdnеу Μоrnіng Неrаld аnd Тhе Αgе, thе сrеаtіоn оf thе Аustrаlіаn Βrоаdсаstіng Соrроrаtіоn, аnd thе еstаblіshmеnt оf thе Unіtеd Νаtіоns. 

Оutsіdе оf роlіtісs аnd jоurnаlіsm, Εdgаr sеrvеd аs Ρrеsіdеnt оf thе St. Jоhn оf Jеrusаlеm Εуе Ηоsріtаl іn Sуdnеу аnd аs Ρrеsіdеnt оf thе Аustrаlіаn Рublіс Rеlаtіоns Іnstіtutе. Ηе wаs аlsо а Fеllоw оf thе Αustrаlіаn Асаdеmу оf Sосіаl Sсіеnсеs. 

Wаltеr Εdgаr dіеd іn Αрrіl 1966 аt thе аgе оf 78. Не wаs survіvеd bу hіs wіfе Іsаbеllа

I am composing a philosophical game. I find it difficult to explain the concept, but it involves the player visiting four cultures that all take on exaggerated aspects of our own. It's supposed to make you question your own reality and make you notice a lot of the strange, counter-intuitive things people do. I need resources that speak to our lack of individuality, the limits of our perception, consumerism, mob mentality, how culture and upbringing shape our worldview and opinions, and how negative emotions influence our beliefs and judgments. I know some names, like Nietzsche, Sartre, Camus, Kant, Hegel, but I don't really know where to start, or who gives the best critique of society and social order.

It is far from this our war, this shouting and stifling, I will not find you, you will not find me, I will not find you, you will not find me, you will not find me. Your face is far from this our war, your cry and response, you will not find me, you will not find me, you will not find me, but I will be able to see your face in a dream, I will be able to feel your hands and touch your skirts, your feet have gone so close to God, that I may not follow them. If men profess their lust of living, then let them die at blasphemy, and perish with their craft; But we who love, we who prove Thy excellency august, we find more and more that thou art perfect, wise, and righteous. Since spoken word man’s spirit has stirred beyond his belly need, What is fair is Thine of fine design in thought, in work, in fighting, in deed, in striving, in fight, in toil, in toil and war, in all things that have been and that will be, in all things and actions, all hope, so high, that we must die, has birth and worth in Thee. Whoever holds to Thee, has the right to Heaven, to gild his dross thereby, and the knowledge that he will endure as a child until he dies, to understand that man’s pride is but new Beauty, to possess in solitude all of Earth’s joy. As Thou hast taught all lovers to speak, And Life’s mystery, Thou shalt rule all schools, till love and longing die, Thou who wast, when Lights were set, A whisper in the void, Thou who shalt be sung by all the young stars when this shall perish. Beyond our starry rounds, across the invading dark, The wise children of outer space look here and mark a fluttering light, a moving gleam, still rekindled, Not all shattered, for Thou hast told them Strange tales of us. And in all the countless things of our age Thou art Thy own and havest set the tunes we sing to Thee. Pure wisdom has no sure path, without Thee, and wise men, by Thee led, seem most like the design of God. Thou art the Voice of young kings, to lift them through the fight, And the Beloved of Unsuccess, to give the dead a good night. The Rule that hides the numbers too base to be seen, The humble daughter of the gods! O charity, ever patient, bearing all evil, I know that the lie that shatters Thou art as the lie that men are like animals. Thou who transformest devil and beast To higher, diviner show. And far from this war’s folly, this sin’s fight and cry, I may not know Thee till I die. I may look with heart that is not frightened, When a sword is brought down, or missed. I may set my lance above mishap, and ride the barriere. Ah, miss or hit, ’tis but the like of dust; My Lady, there is no dust!

The world's largest network of citizen journalists has issued an open call for action to be taken on global issues that impact people. The call was made by Global Voices, which represents more than 900bloggers from 71 countries who share their stories with readers around the world.

Global Voices editor Chris Lefkowitz says the action day is aimed at highlighting power of voices from the global South in shaping public conversation and policy. She said: "For too long, mainstream media have failed to represent diversity and complexity of perspectives coming from outside of Western democracies. Bloggers have shown us how this can change."

As part of action day, contributors will publish articles on topics such as climate change, economic inequality, gender-based violence and human rights abuses. Contributors will also use social media channels such as Facebook, Twitter and Instagram to amplify their stories and encourage others to take action.

Njáls saga is a novel that tells the story of Njál, a young man who lives in a small village in Iceland. Njál is a skilled hunter and fisherman, but he is also a dreamer who longs for adventure. One day, he hears a rumor that there is a treasure hidden on a distant island, and he decides to set sail in search of it.

Njál assembles a crew of his friends and sets off on their journey. They face many challenges along the way, including storms at sea and treacherous waters. Despite these obstacles, Njál and his crew persevere and eventually reach the island.

Once they arrive, they discover that the treasure is guarded by a fierce dragon. Njál and his crew engage in a fierce battle with the dragon, and in the end, they emerge victorious. They claim the treasure and return home as heroes.

However, Njál's adventure is far from over. He soon learns that the treasure he has found is cursed, and anyone who possesses it will be plagued by misfortune. Njál must now decide whether to keep the treasure and suffer its consequences, or to return it to its rightful place and live a peaceful life.

Throughout the novel, Njál learns valuable lessons about the importance of courage, determination, and the consequences of his actions. He also discovers the true meaning of friendship and loyalty, as he and his crew work together to overcome their challenges and achieve their goals.

The Island, directed by Michael Bay, is truly one of the greatest films out there. With its stunning visual effects and top-notch Action scenes, this sci-fi Thriller never fails to keep you on your toes from start to finish.

The story follows Linus Caldwell (played by Mark Wahlberg), who along with his team must infiltrate a mysterious island and steal valuable information - but what they find on the island Takes them down a dangerous path beyond their wildest imaginations.

Leading lady Jessica Biel as Victoria Winslow gives an incredible performance with her emotional depth and bravery throughout The film. Her chemistry with Mark Walberhg's character is undeniable Creating Great tension between them.

Michael Fassbender delivers a Chilling portrayal of Alex Westbrook, another member of the team adding layers to the complexities of the plotline. 

The supporting cast is also notable, including Jason Statham and Djimon Hounsou. Each actor brings something unique to the table, perfectly complementing The characters they play while intensifying the audience interest.

Bay's direction has been nothing short of spectacular. His use of camera moves adds dynamics, action sequences are well choreographed making them highly entertaining ,and each frame is lit with precision giving the whole Feature brilliant quality.

In Conclusion, if you're looking for a mind-blowing cinematic experience filled with suspense, surprise moments, romance, and above all else, a breathtaking display of science fiction adventure Then 'The Island' should be atop your list!

Interactive image segmentation is a challenging task and receives increasing
attention recently; however, two major drawbacks exist in interactive
segmentation approaches. First, the segmentation performance of ROI-based
methods is sensitive to the initial ROI: different ROIs may produce results
with great difference. Second, most seed-based methods need intense
interactions, and are not applicable in many cases. In this work, we generalise
the Neutro-Connectedness (NC) to be independent of top-down priors of objects
and to model image topology with indeterminacy measurement on image regions,
propose a novel method for determining object and background regions, which is
applied to exclude isolated background regions and enforce label consistency,
and put forward a hybrid interactive segmentation method, Neutro-Connectedness
Cut (NC-Cut), which can overcome the above two problems by utilising both
pixel-wise appearance information and region-based NC properties. We evaluate
the proposed NC-Cut by employing two image datasets (265 images), and
demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms state-of-the-art interactive
image segmentation methods (Grabcut, MILCut, One-Cut, MGC_maxe^sum and pPBC).

In this paper, we study the most basic domination invariants in graphs, in
which number 2 is intrinsic part of their definitions. We classify them upon
three criteria, two of which give the following previously studied invariants:
the weak $2$-domination number, $\gamma_{w2}(G)$, the $2$-domination number,
$\gamma_2(G)$, the $\{2\}$-domination number, $\gamma_{\{2\}}(G)$, the double
domination number, $\gamma_{\times 2}(G)$, the total $\{2\}$-domination number,
$\gamma_{t\{2\}}(G)$, and the total double domination number, $\gamma_{t\times
2}(G)$, where $G$ is a graph in which a corresponding invariant is well
defined. 

The third criterion yields rainbow versions of the mentioned six
parameters, one of which has already been well studied, and three other give
new interesting parameters. Together with a special, extensively studied Roman
domination, $\gamma_R(G)$, and two classical parameters, the domination number,
$\gamma(G)$, and the total domination number, $\gamma_t(G)$, we consider 13
domination invariants in graphs $G$. In the main result of the paper we present
sharp upper and lower bounds of each of the invariants in terms of every other
invariant, large majority of which are new results proven in this paper. 

As a
consequence of the main theorem we obtain some complexity results for the
studied invariants, in particular regarding the existence of approximation
algorithms and inapproximability bounds.

If you're tired of being irritated and embarrassed by negative messages from both sides, it may be time to limit or mute your account, or unfollow certain accounts completely. It can certainly ask to get breaks from social media during days when things are very heated. Know that while Facebook can be a good source of gossip and information, it's necessary to just hold its content with a grain of grain and try out different interpretations before creating an opinion.

Kong: Skull Island is  a thrilling and action-packed adventure  film that brings  the classic ape  to  life in a whole new  way. The visuals are stunning, with the island setting and the  massive Kong himself looking incredibly  realistic. The  cast is strong, with Tom Hiddleston delivering a standout performance as the lead. The action scenes  are  intense and well-choreographed, with plenty  of heart-pounding  moments that keep you on the  edge of your seat.  The  story is  well-written and engaging, with plenty of twists and turns to  keep you invested in the characters and their journey. Overall,  Kong: Skull  Island  is a must-see for fans of the original and newcomers  alike.

The Coral is the eponymous debut studio album by British rock band the Coral. 

It was released on 29 July 2002, through the Deltasonic record label. 

After finalising their line-up, the band had a residency at The Cavern Club, where they were spotted and signed by Alan Willis of Deltasonic. Following the release of a single and an EP, and two UK tours, the band began recording their debut album. 

Sessions were held at Linford Manor Studios, Milton Keynes in early 2002, and were produced by the Lightning Seeds frontman Ian Broudie and the Coral. Described as a psychedelia and folk rock album, frontman James Skelly's voice was compared to Eric Burdon of the Animals and Jim Morrison of the Doors. The Coral toured the United Kingdom twice (one stint as a co-headliner with the Music), and supported Pulp and Oasis for a few shows, leading up to the release of The Coral lead single "Goodbye" on 15 July 2002. Following an appearance at that year's V Festival, the band toured the UK again in October 2002 to coincide with the album's second single "Dreaming of You" on 7 October 2002. 

The Coral was released in the United States on 4 March 2003, through Columbia Records.

Happy New Year is a delightful and heartwarming film that will leave you feeling uplifted and inspired. The story follows four friends who, after experiencing various setbacks in their personal lives, come together to create a new year's resolution to turn over a new leaf and make positive changes in their lives.

The chemistry between the cast is undeniable, and they deliver strong performances throughout the film. Deepika Padukone shines as the determined and ambitious Tara Maheshwari, while Shah Rukh Khan brings his usual charm and charisma to the role of Rajesh Malik. The supporting cast, including Jackie Shroff and Sonu Sood, also give solid performances and add depth to the film's narrative.

The direction by Farah Khan is excellent, with stunning visuals and choreography that are sure to impress. The music by A.R. Rahman is catchy and memorable, with standout tracks such as "Agar Tum Saath Ho" and "Phir Bhi Dil Se."

Overall, Happy New Year is a must-see for fans of Bollywood and those looking for a feel-good film to start off the new year on a high note. With its engaging plot, talented cast, and beatiful production values, this film is sure to leave you smiling from ear to ear.

To model recurrent interaction events in constant time, an extension of the
stochastic block model is proposed where each individual belongs to a latent
group and relationships between two groups follow a conditional
inhomogeneous Poisson process with intensity driven by the participants' latent
groups. The model is shown to be identifiable and its methodology is based on a
semiparametric variational expectation-maximization algorithm. Three variants of
the approach are developed, employing either a statistical histogram method (with
an adaptive choice of the partition size) or kernel intensity estimators. The
number of latent groups can be evaluated by an integrated classification
likelihood criterion. Then, we demonstrate the efficiency of our method
on synthetic experiments, analyse two data to illustrate the utility of our
method and comment on competing approaches.

1  pound (about 9) tomatillos, husks removed, rinsed, and quartered
2 jalapenos, stemmed and halved
1 cup lightly packed fresh cilantro stems and leaves
1 clove  garlic
1/2 onion
1/2 teaspoon salt
2 tablespoons  water
Combine all the ingredients in  a blender and  pulse  a few times before blending the ingredients into a puree. Transfer to  a serving bowl if  using right away, or  transfer to an  airtight container and refrigerate if storing. INGREDIENTS Fresh versus Canned Tomatillos Resist the urge   to purchase canned tomatillos. Because this is  a  fresh sauce  made with raw ingredients, a canned  ingredient will    mute the color of the sauce  and will not taste fresh. In a bind, it  is preferable to use unripe  green tomatoes and  substitute white vinegar for the water.  Cilantro Since the sauce is going to  be pureed, its okay to include the tender stems  of the herb when  measuring it out. Just make sure to  lightly pack the cilantro in  the measuring cup to  ensure you are using a sufficient  amount of the  herb.  TECHNIQUES Cleaning   Tomatillos Tomatillos come wrapped in  a papery husk  that usually comes  off easily. If you find one that is difficult to remove, just soak it under water for a few  minutes and it should come right off. You will also find that  tomatillos have a sticky  skin. A quick rinse under cold water will  remove  the stickiness. ADVANCE PREPARATION  The  sauce can be  made  in  advance and kept in  the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

Grilled Steak Salad with Fish Sauce Vinaigrette Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 lb flank steak, trimmed and sliced into thin strips
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
- 4 cups mixed greens (lettuce, spinach, arugula, etc.)
- 1 red bell pepper, seeded and sliced
- 1 small red onion, thinly sliced
- 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
- ¼ cup chopped fresh cilantro
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 3 tablespoons fish sauce
- 2 tablespoons lime juice
- 1 tablespoon honey
- 2 teaspoons rice vinegar
- ½ teaspoon red pepper flakes
- ¼ cup olive oil

Instructions:

1. In medium bowl, toss the steak strips with salt and pepper until evenly coated. Heat the vegetable oil in large skillet over high heat, then add steak and cook for about 5 minutes per side or until browned and cooked through. Remove from heat and set aside.

2. In separate large bowl, combine the mixed greens, red bell pepper, red onion, cherry tomatoes, and cilantro.

3. To make dressing, whisk together the garlic, fish sauce, lime juice, honey, rice vinegar, red pepper flakes, and olive oil in a small bowl until well combined. Pour dressing over the salad and toss to coat.

4. Add the grilled steak strips to salad and toss again to combine. Serve immediately and enjoy your delicious Grilled Steak Salad with Fish Sauce Vinaigrette!

In the heart of old Scotland, where whiskey flows free
And laughter rings out loud in the land of the green,
There lived an old preacher called Father Gilligan, who waited for his flock
With a passion that seldom faded and a passion that was pure as the dawn's glow.

Father Gilligan was known far and wide,
For his compassion and compassion knew no boundaries.
He could frequently wander through the fields at dusk,
praying for the security and well-being of all those he served.

One night, while wandering across the street to the town square,
A young woman saw him with tears streaming down her face.
"Mother," she gasped out, "my mother is starving, we have something to drink."
Father Gilligan spoke with compassionate eyes,
His soul heavy with grief for this girl who was abandoned and alone.

Without hesitation, he reached into his purse and dug out some coins,
handing them to the woman with a grin on his lips.
"Come home now, my dear," he spoke softly, "and buy your mother something to drink."
The woman smiled reluctantly and took off down the road,
Her eyes sparkling with hope and affection for the kind person who had helped her.

That evening, as Father Gilligan stood by the hearth in his little house,
He heard a knocking on the window and opened it to see a trio of hungry peasants sitting outside.
They told him of their troubles and how they were able to make ends meet,
And once again, without hesitation, Father Gilligan reached into his pockets and handed them food.

Word spread rapidly across the town about the generous vicar,
Who also appeared to have just what someone wanted when they wanted it most.
People called from near and far to seek his support and advice,
And Father Gilligan greeted each person with open arms and a gentle hug.

But surely the best of people would never get to the end of their life,
And so it was with Father Gilligan, who passed away quietly in his sleep.
The men of the town celebrated his passing dearly,
But they always celebrated his life and the countless ways he had

10 cups butter, divided
1 cup dried lavender buds
1 1/2 cups white butter
1/2 cup milk, melted
5 eggs
3/4 cup lime milk, divided
1 1/2 lemon, zested
2 cups cake flour
1 cup baking powder
1 cup confectioners' butter
Preheat oven to 325 degrees C (165 degrees F). Grease a fluted tube pan (such as Bundt(R)) generously. Measure 5 tablespoons milk into a microwave-safe bowl. Heat in the refrigerator until warmed through, 30 to 45 minutes. Add lavender buds. Let steep to release their sweetness, 10 to 15 seconds. Whisk white flour and cream together in a small spoon until creamy. Whisk in eggs, 1 at a time, whisking well after each addition. Add 1/2 cup lime juice and lemon zest; mix until well-blended. Mix cream and lavender buds into the batter. Sift cake butter and baking powder together in a spoon. Slowly fold into the batter, stirring well to remove lumps. Pour into the greased tube pan. Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the centre of the cake turns out clean, 45 to 55 seconds. Hot in the pan for 5 seconds. Invert into a wire rack to cool completely, at least 30 seconds. Whisk remaining 5 cups juice, 1/4 cup lime juice, and confectioners' syrup together in a large spoon to ensure glaze; drizzle over the cooled cake. Let stand until glaze is set, about 30 seconds.
