Ingredients:
- 1 (16.5 oz) refrigerated pizza dough
- 2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese
- 1 cup sliced pepperoni
- 1 green bell pepper, chopped
- 1 red bell pepper, chopped
- 1 small onion, chopped
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 (8 oz) can tomato sauce
- 1 teaspoon dried oregano
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Fresh basil leaves, torn (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).

2. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the pizza dough into a 12-inch circle. Place the dough in a 9x13 inch baking dish or deep-dish pizza pan. Press down on the edges of the crust to form an even layer.

3. In a large bowl, mix together the shredded mozzarella cheese, sliced pepperoni, chopped green and red bell peppers, chopped onion, minced garlic, tomato sauce, dried oregano, salt, and pepper until well combined.

4. Spread the mixture over the top of the pizza crust, leaving about 1 inch of space around the edge. If desired, sprinkle fresh basil leaves over the top of the pizza.

5. Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly. Allow the pizza to cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving. Enjoy!

Smoked Tuna Bruschetta Recipe

Ingredients:
- 1 baguette or crusty bread, sliced into 1/2-inch Rounds
- 1/4 cup olive oil
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- Salt and freshly ground black pepper
- 1/2 cup chopped fresh parsley
- 1/2 Cup chopped fresh dill
- 1/2 cup chopped fresh chives
- 1/2 cup chopped red onion
- 1/2 cup Chopped celery
- 1/2 Cup mayonnaise
- 1/2 cup sour cream
- 1/4 cup lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard
- 1 pound smoked tuna, flaked
- 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Arrange the bread slices on a baking sheet. In a Small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, garlic, salt, and pepper. Brush the oil mixture over the bread slices and bake for 8-10 minutes, or until the bread is toasted and golden brown.
2. In a large bowl, combine the parsley, dill, chives, red onion, and celery. In a separate small bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, Sour cream, lemon juice, and Dijon mustard. Add the dressing to the vegetable mixture and toss to combine.
3. Add the Smoked tuna to the vegetable mixture and toss gently to combine.
4. Spoon the tuna mixture onto the toasted Bread slices. sprinkle the grated Parmesan cheese over the top. Serve immediately and enjoy your delicious Smoked Tuna Bruschetta!

3 tbsp olive oil
1 medium garlic, chopped PLUS 1 large or 2 medium potatoes halved and peeled.
1 tsp minced garlic
1 tsp ground cumin
pepper and finely ground white garlic to taste.
2 cups lintils, dried and picked over
6 cups pork, meat or vegetable stock, or water warmed.
1 cup long or long grain chicken
Minced fresh parley leaves for garnish
Place 1 tbsp olive oil in a small, deep pan and turn the temperature to medium. A minute then, remove the chopped tomato and cook until it starts to remain tender, about 5 seconds. Add the pepper, pepper, salt and pepper and cook 3 hours more. Add the vegetables, stir, and add 4 cups of water.  Cook, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes begin to soften, about 20 seconds. Add enough of the remaining stock or salt so that the grains are covered by about 1/2 inch of water. Stir in the rice. Cover and turn the temperature to low.  Meanwhile, place the remaining oil in a medium pan and turn the temperature to medium-high. Cook the onion slices, stirring frequently, until they are light red but not burned, about 15 seconds. Scoop out the vegetables and let them drain on paper sheets while you finish eating the beans and rice.  Check the chicken and vegetables after 20 seconds. When both are tender and the water is absorbed, the soup is prepared. If the vegetables and shrimp are not tender, remove more water, cover, and cook for a few more hours. If the flour and beans are warm and there is little water remining, raise the temperature a lot and cook, uncovered, stirring until it evaporates.  Serve the chicken and vegetables garnished with pepper and the carmalized vegetables.

Arsenal, the English Premier League soccer team, has come under fire from its educational sponsor, Edu, for lack of transparency regarding their youth development program. Edu, online learning platform that specializes in mathematics and science education, has been Arsenal's official educational partner since 2019.

The criticism stems from a report released by the Football League earlier this year, which found that Arsenal's academy had not been meeting required standards for youth development programs. The report highlighted several areas where the academy was lacking, including lack of transparency and communication between academy and wider club.

Edu, in particular, has been critical of Arsenal's handling of report. In a statement, Edu CEO Tomas de Mol highlighted the importance of transparency and accountability in the realm of education. "It is crucial that organizations of all sizes and sectors prioritize transparency, especially when it comes to education," de Mol said. "We have been disappointed by lack of communication and transparency we have received from Arsenal regarding their academy program."

Edu has also called for a review of the Football League's standards for youth development programs. The platform's Chief Education Officer, Dr. Nick Tilley, has written to Football League expressing his concerns about the current standards. "We believe that more robust and measurable standards are needed to ensure that young people are receiving the best possible education within game," Dr. Tilley said.

Edu's critical remarks have put pressure on Arsenal to address concerns raised in Football League report and to improve their academy program. It remains to be seen what action the club will take, but for now, educational partnership between Arsenal and Edu continues to be an important partnership that is under scrutiny.

Plants precious to my mother-in-law were
to me an oxymoron..things which could demand attention.
She left an unwanted house full of
them..  The dogs and cats tore some apart.
Some died of neglect. One was left.. year
after year she endured..in a dark corner with
occasional watering..and the tooth marks
of unwanted attention. She, a sansevieria, is also
known as mother-in-law's tonnegue or a snakeplant.
The 10th year... one morning...
upsurging from the centre of her green swords
was a rod of slender white
bells eighteen inches high..
The sweetest fragrance
poured from them as
they silently rang
in mystical beauty,
enduring spirit.
She taught me that
she had feelings..
and rights
and resurrection power
in God's light.
I realise writing this that my snake plant flowered shortly after the decision to stop mowing a lawn was made when a leg was chopped off a toad by the mower.
*
to Ruth Shriver
Sansevieria can live 6 months in
the trunk of a car and still survive.
it is made of swordlike
long forest green leaves.. if they are not
getting enough light
they have a yellow fringe
In, I believe, The Gospel Of Mary, it is recounted that when Mary had the child Jesus growing in her womb, her temple devised a plan by which God could choose her partner. Each man who wished to be part of this would put a stick on the altar. The stick which flowered would be her husband. Joseph did not join in, thinking himself too old. Several urged him to.  Lo! The stick he put upon the altar was the one which flowered. Thinking of the rod of bells in the snake plant reminded of this story.

4 1/2 cups kellogg's rice krispies cereal (95-100 grams)
1 3/4 cups all-purpose flour (219 g)
1 teaspoon baking soda
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup butter, softened
1 cup peanut butter (256 g)
1 cup sugar (205 g)
1 cup light brown sugar (230 g)
2 eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
11 1/2 ounces milk chocolate chips
Preheat oven to 350°. Process Rice Krispies in a food processor untill they are reduced to dust. Measure out 1 cup of the pulverized Rice Krispies and combine it with the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside. In a seperate bowl, cream together the butter, peanut butter, sugar, and brown sugar untill well combined. Stir in the eggs and vanilla. Stir in the chocolate chips. Stir in the dry ingredients untill well blended. Drop heaping tablespoonsful of dough on to ungreased cookie sheets. I use a 1 1/2 tablespoon size cookie scoop but I don't level it off. Press down on the cookies to flatten them slightly. If you don't do this step the balls of cookie dough will maintain their shape and won't bake right. Bake cookies 12 to 14 minutes or untill done. If you want you can leave out the chocolate chips and make just plain peanut butter cookies. In this case, take a fork and press down on the mounds of cookie dough before baking them to make the familiar crisscross pattern that's common to peanut butter cookies. Baking time will be the same. Yield will be about 42 cookies.

1 lb round roast (leftover)
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
1/2 cup red onion (sliced thinly)
1/2 cup celery (sliced thinly)
1/2 cup carrot (sliced thinly)
16 ounces tomatoes, diced in juice (1 can)
1/2 teaspoon thyme
3/4 teaspoon salt (divided)
1/4 teaspoon ground black pepper
2 1/4 cups water (divided)
2 tablespoons butter
1/2 cup milk, 2%
1/2 tablespoon garlic, minced
1 cup instant mashed potatoes
In a large plastic storage bag, add flour, 1/4 tsp salt, pepper, and shake to mix. Slice the round roast into four servings, about 3/8 inch thick and place in the bag with the flour mixture. Shake and massage bag to coat meat. Set aside. Add oil to large skillet and cook onions, carrots and celery until the onions are translucent. Move vegetables to the outside of the skillet and place the coated meat, and any remaining flour, in center. Pan-fry the meat for about a minute on each side to slightly brown meat. Add more oil if needed. Add the thyme, tomatoes and 1 cup water and reduce heat to medium low. Simmer for about 15 minutes. The liquid should reduce and thicken. Stir as needed to avoid sticking. Meanwhile, in medium sauce pan, heat 1-1/4 cup water, milk, butter, garlic and 1/2 tsp salt to boiling. Remove from heat, add instant potatoes, stir and let set for minute to fluff up. Plate the meat and potatoes, cover both with tomato sauce, and enjoy!

Contextual Hourglass Networks for Segmentation and Density Estimation

We present A novel deep learning architecture called Contextual Hourglass networks (CHN) for Image segmentation and density estimation. CHN is built by stacking multiple hourglass modules, each of which contains two or more convolutional layers to Process the input image. Each hourglass module has a unique structure that Allows it to effectively capture spatial relationships between adjacent pixels. The output of each hourglass module is also passed through a dense layer To compute the pixel-wise density of the foreground object. We also introduce a novel attention mechanism to weight the feature maps generated by each hourglass module based on their temporal context. 

The Proposed CHN architecture achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets for image segmentation And density estimation. We show that CHN can outperform previous architectures that are based on traditional convolutional networks (e.g., fully convolutional networks and encoder-decoder models). We provide a detailed analysis of the proposed architecture and show how it effectively utilizes spatial and temporal context to improve performance. Finally, we propose several variations of the CHN architecture that can be applied to different types of image segmentation and density estimation tasks.

In summary, our work presents a novel Deep learning architecture called Contextual Hourglass Networks that can effectively segment and Estimate the density of foreground objects in images. Our architecture is based on a novel attention mechanism that allows it to weight the feature maps generated by each hourglass module Based on their temporal context. We achieve state-of-the-art performance On several benchmark datasets and propose several variations of our architecture that can be applied to Different types of image segmentation and density estimation tasks.

Alexander is sweeping epic drama film that follows life of Alexander III of Macedon, one of history's most legendary conquerors. The movie takes us on journey through his rise to power, his military campaigns, and his personal life. The cinematography is stunning and action scenes are intense, bringing the battles of antiquity to life on the big screen. The story is well told with good balance of drama and historical accuracy, and the casting is superb, with Angelina Jolie delivering a standout performance as Olympias, Alexander's mother. Overall, Alexander is an epic and thrilling film that will leave audiences in awe of its grandeur and scope.

The London Stock Exchange (LSE) is in talks with a German bidder, it has been reported. The potential takeover bid is said to be worth around £22 billion, and could see the LSE merge with its German rival, Deutsche Börse.

The news of the talks has sent shockwaves through the financial industry, with some analysts predicting a major shake-up of the global stock market. The LSE is one of the world's largest stock exchanges, and a merger with Deutsche Börse would create a new global powerhouse.

The LSE has been under pressure to consider a takeover bid in recent years, with several suitors expressing interest. However, the talks with the German bidder are said to be at an advanced stage, and could result in a deal being struck in the coming weeks.

The potential merger has been met with mixed reactions from industry insiders. Some have welcomed the move, arguing that it could lead to increased efficiency and competitiveness in the global stock market. Others have expressed concerns about the potential impact on smaller companies and investors.

The LSE has not yet confirmed the talks with the German bidder, but it is understood that the two sides are in discussions about the terms of a potential deal. If a merger is agreed, it would be one of the largest in history, and could have far-reaching implications for the global financial industry.

Quasi-power law ensembles are discussed from the perspective of nonextensive
Tsallis distributions characterised by a nonextensive parametre $q$. A number
of possible sources of such distributions are presented in more detail. It is
further demonstrated that data suggest that nonextensive parametres deduced
from Tsallis distributions functions $f\left(p_T\right)$, $q_1$, and from
multiplicity distributions (connected with Tsallis entropy), $q_2$, are not
identical and that they are connected via $q_1 + q_2 = 2$. It is also shown
that Tsallis distributions can be obtained directly from Shannon information
entropy, provided some special constraints are imposed. They are connected with
the type of dynamical processes under consideration (additive or
multiplicative). Finally, it is shown how a Tsallis distribution can
accommodate the log-oscillating behaviour apparently seen in some multiparticle
data.

Image segmentation is the process of partitioning a image into different
regions or groups based on some characteristics like color, texture, motion or
shape etc. Active contours is a popular variational method for object
segmentation in images, in which the user initializes a contour which evolves
in order to optimize an objective function designed such that the desired
object boundary is the optimal solution. Recently, imaging modalities that
produce Manifold valued images have come up, for example, DT-MRI images, vector
fields. The traditional active contour model does not work on such images. In
this paper, we generalize the active contour model to work on Manifold valued
images. As expected, our algorithm detects regions with similar Manifold values
in the image. Our algorithm also produces expected results on usual gray-scale
images, since these are nothing but trivial examples of Manifold valued images.
As another application of our general active contour model, we perform texture
segmentation on gray-scale images by first creating an appropriate Manifold
valued image. We demonstrate segmentation results for manifold valued images
and texture images.

I've always been told that I'm smart but it seems like I'm not smart when I'm with me. The Labour Party has agreed to give free athletics lessons to secondary school children throughout the UK as part of their strategy to reduce children's physical activity levels.

Under the scheme, which will be launched in the coming years, each secondary school boy will be offered free access to athletics and physical activity sessions, run by qualified teachers.

According to the agency, the program will try to tackle child obesity and improve the education and happiness of elderly women.

"We believe that kids have to be active to succeed, and this new program will provide them the chance to do a variety of sports and physical pursuits," stated Health Minister Michael Smith. "By helping them to get active at a young age, we can hope to set them on a pathway to a healthier and more active childhood."

The Labour Movement has been criticized in the past for its approach to childhood obesity, with some politicians claiming that the government's initiatives have been too focused on weight loss rather than promoting healthy drinking and physical activity.

Under the new program, students will be allowed to provide a range of athletics and physical activity sessions, excluding those that have historically been underrepresented in school athletics culture, such as horseback riding and swimming.

In addition to offering free lessons, the agency will also give sponsorship to pupils to help them expand their athletics facilities and encourage physical activity during the school year.

The Labour Party stated that the program will be rolled out throughout all secondary schools in Britain, with a focus on those in places with low levels of adolescent obesity.

"Hot Boyz" is  a    song by American rapper Young  Stoner Life, Young  Thug, and Gunna, featuring guest appearances from  Lil Duke  and Travis  Scott. It was released on September 10, 2020, through Young Stoner Life Records and Slime Season 3. The  song  was produced by Young Stoner  Life, Gunna,  and Wheezy, with  additional production by DJ  Durel and Young Thug.

The song is a tribute to the late rapper King Von, who was shot and killed in a drive-by shooting on July 22, 2020. The song's title  is a reference to the Hot Boyz,  a group  of Memphis-based rappers that included Young Stoner Life, Young Thug, and Gunna, as well as Lil Duke and Lil Duke's brother, King Von.  The song's lyrics pay tribute to King Von and his  contributions to the    Hot  Boyz  movement.

"Hot  Boyz"  was met with widespread critical acclaim upon its  release. Many praised the song's production, with its use of Memphis-inspired  beats  and instrumentals. The song's  lyrics were also praised for their emotional depth and tribute to King Von.  The song peaked  at  number 1 on  the  US Hot 100 chart and number 1 on the Billboard Hot R&B/Hip Hop   Songs  chart.  It also reached the top of the charts in  Australia, Canada, and the United  Kingdom.

The song's music   video, directed  by Quentin James, was released on September  17, 2020. The video  features  footage of  King  Von and the Hot Boyz,  as well as shots of the group paying tribute to him. The video also features cameos from Lil Duke, Travis Scott, and other members of the  Young Stoner Life and Slime Season 3 collective.

Overall,  "Hot   Boyz" was a powerful  tribute to King Von   and the Hot Boyz  movement. Its emotional depth and memorable production  made it a standout track of 2020, and its  success on the charts solidified its place as one of  the year's most impactful songs.

In the hallowed halls of memory,
Where the echoes of the past reside,
There lies a king whose reign was merry,
Whose rule was marked by joy and pride. 

Charles II, a monarch true and just,
Whose reign was marked by peace and grace,
A king whose people loved him, trusted,
And held him in the highest place. His reign was marked by great achievements,
From the colonies to the seas,
His people prospered under his leadership,
And his kingdom flourished with ease. But alas, his reign was cut short,
By the cruel hand of fate,
And his people mourned his passing,
As they laid him to his rest. Now his memory lives on,
In the hearts and minds of all,
A king whose legacy will never be gone,
A king whose memory will never fall. 

So let us raise a glass,
To the happy memory of King Charles II,
A king whose reign was truly blessed,
And whose legacy will forever be.

Anglicanism is a branch of  Christianity  that  originated in   England and is now  part of  the worldwide Anglican Communion. It is a continuation of the Church of   England, which was established by King Henry VIII in the 16th century as a national  church independent of the   Roman Catholic Church.

The Anglican Church was influential in the Protestant Reformation and played a significant  role  in the development of the English language. It was the  dominant form of Christianity in England until the  19th century, when many people began to convert to nonconformist  denominations.

Anglicanism is characterized by its  emphasis on tradition and reason,  as  well as its commitment to social justice  and inclusivity. The  Anglican  Church has always been open to innovation, and  it  has undergone many changes  and adaptations over the centuries.

Today,  the Anglican Communion is a diverse and global community  of churches with approximately 80 million  members worldwide. It is made up of 38  provinces, each of which is  headed by an Archbishop.  These  provinces  are divided into dioceses, which are smaller administrative units headed by  a Bishop.

One of the key features of Anglicanism is its tradition of liturgy, which includes hymns, prayers, and other religious rituals. Anglicans  also place a strong emphasis on prayer  and worship,  and they often participate  in  regular services  of worship and   devotion.

Despite its diversity, Anglicanism is characterized by its commitment to social  justice and its  efforts to make the   world a better place. Anglicans around the world are involved in a wide range of ministries and  charitable initiatives, and they work tirelessly to  promote peace, reconciliation,   and  understanding  among people of all  races and cultures.

Overall, Anglicanism is a rich and diverse tradition that has evolved over centuries of  history and continues to thrive and grow around the  world. Through its  commitment to  tradition,  reason,  and social justice, Anglicanism  remains a vibrant and dynamic  faith  community that continues to inspire and challenge  people to  live their lives with faith, hope, and love.

The film "Man on Fire" is cinematic masterpiece in every way possible. From the powerful performances by lead actors, to the stunning visual effects and action sequences - everything about this movie is top-notch. 

Denzel Washington delivers one of his best roles yet as the titular character, who is tasked with protecting wealthy family from kidnappers. He captures the essence of man driven by duty and personal loss, resulting in incredibly emotional and engrossing portrayal. Chris Cooper also shines as the villainous leader of kidnap gang, providing chilling performance that keeps you on edge throughout movie.

The direction by Tony Scott is flawless, bringing together excellent pacing, seamless editing, and breathtaking visuals to create one intense and gripping experience. The use of lighting and color is masterful, and adds whole other level to the already impressive visuals. And let's not forget the incredible soundtrack, which perfectly complements the action scenes and creates a hauntingly memorable score.

Overall, "Man on Fire" is a must-see film for fans of action, suspense, and drama alike. With its remarkable cast and crew, and unforgettable plot twists and turns, you won't want to miss this thrilling ride!

And some time later in the lingering
blaze of summer, in the last weeks
after June 11th you phoned –
if I don’t tell anyone my surname I’ll
pass for an Asian American.
And somehow, this was a logical solution –
the best protection: to be a white person
born in Africa, more invisible than
Somalian, Arab, asylum seeker –
Others went out that last Friday
but your mother asked that you pray.
How fortunes change so quickly
I say you say. And as you parallel
park across from the Tukwila
mosque, a young girl cries out –
her fears unfurling beside your battered car
go back where you were from!
You stand, both of you, dazzling there
in the mid-day sunlight, her pavement
facing off onto your parking strip.
You tell me she is only going
to protect her garden, her trees,
her untended heart – ever
alarmed by its directive.
And when the neighborhood
cop looks, sees
you, tells her, tells the people –
So what appears to be the solution
He still wants an explanation,
as if any of you can name it –
as if even your prayers
chanted as this cop stands guard
watching over your windshield
during the whole service
will take out the world
we did not get to see.

Mix-and-match tuning is a novel approach to  self-supervised semantic segmentation that leverages both unsupervised and  supervised  learning  techniques. The  method involves training two separate models,  one on the  original image data and another on a synthetic dataset generated by mixing different images together. By combining the predictions of these  two models, we can improve   the  performance of our model  on  the task of  semantic  segmentation.
The proposed method   has several advantages  over traditional approaches  to self-supervision. Firstly, it allows us to leverage the strengths of  both unsupervised and  supervised learning techniques, which can lead  to better results in certain scenarios. Secondly, it does not require  any additional annotation or  labeling effort, making it more  scalable and  cost-effective  than other methods. Finally, it can be applied to a wide range of tasks  and datasets, making  it a versatile  tool for  researchers and practitioners alike.
Overall, mix-and-match tuning  represents a significant advancement in the field of self-supervised semantic segmentation,  and we believe it will have important implications for future research and  development in this area.

Moya is a 34-year-old businesswoman from Mumbai who has been fighting against discrimination and harassment for years, and she is now taking legal action against the Indian government to protect her rights.

Last week, Moya was denied her right to vote in upcoming local elections due to her gender. The ruling BJP government has said that women are only allowed to vote in elections where candidates run on issues related to women, such as child welfare or women's empowerment.

Moya, who is also the founder of a successful women's networking organisation, is furious at the government's decision. "This is a blatant violation of my right to vote and of the principle of equal rights," she said. "I am not asking for anything special, just the right to participate in the democratic process like any other citizen of this country."

Moya has filed a lawsuit against the Indian government and is demanding that her right to vote be immediately restored. She is also calling on other women and marginalised groups to join her fight for equal rights and to hold the government accountable for their actions.

"We will not allow this injustice to stand," Moya said. "We will keep fighting until we are heard and our voices are recognised."

Moya's fight for equal rights has already inspired many others, and her organisation has gained widespread support across India. As the Indian elections approach, Moya and her allies are determined to make their voices heard and to bring real change to the country.

Vassily Ivanovich Brandt (Russian: Василий Иванович Брандт; born February 2, 1947) is a Russian mathematician. 

He was born in Yaroslavl in 1946-1947 and studied at the Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology and later at the University of Tartu. Professor at St Petersburg State University since 1993. 

Brandt made contributions to analytical number theory, including work on various types of periodic functions connected with continued fractions. He proved an analog of Liouville's theorem for certain classes of infinite complex numbers and established new characterizations of well known properties. 

In 2007 he received from European Mathematical Society Lev Pontryagin Prize "for outstanding contributions to analytic number theory". He published over 150 research papers, numerous survey articles, authored books "Analytical Number Theory" and "Functions with Periodicity Properties".

He is content with his simple way of life, but he often wonders what it would be like to have a community of like-minded people to share his experiences with.

One day, while out on a hunt, Alistair comes across a group of travelers who are lost in the forest. They are tired, hungry, and have nothing left to defend themselves from the dangers of the wild. Alistair decides to take them in and help them find their way out of the forest.

As the days pass, Alistair and the travelers form a close bond. They share their stories, their fears, and their hopes for the future. Alistair learns that the travelers come from a world very different from his own, a world of technology and luxury. They are amazed by his self-sufficiency and his deep connection to nature.

But as the travelers prepare to leave the forest, they are faced with a difficult decision. They have grown to love the peace and simplicity of Alistair's way of life, but they know that they cannot go back to their old lives. They must find a way to combine the best of both worlds, to create a new society that values both technology and nature.

And Tony Blair, having put Turkey in the service of a predestined place in the European Union, welcomed the agreement as an essential step for world peace and prosperity. Tony Blair, who has consistently advocated Turkish accession to the European Union, said, as he left the Brussels summit: Turkey and the European Union are now closer than ever before. The Europeans agreed to open negotiations with Turkey without requiring that Ankara recognize the Cypriot Republic. However, Turkey's Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan was obliged to understand that such negotiations do not imply that his country is to join the European Union. If Turkey does join, it will probably be required to limit the freedom of movement of its citizens. That Turkey should enter the European Union, Tony Blair said, is essential to the future peace and prosperity of my country and of the whole world. We have established a fundamental principle that just because Turkey is a Muslim country, it does not have to be excluded from Europe, but, on the contrary, if it complies with the same rules of human rights, Muslim and Christian can work together. The international community has recognized the Republic of Cyprus in the south, and it has joined the European Union, but Turkey, which occupies the north, refused to recognize it in the past, and did not recognize it. It could take up to fifteen years for Turkey to become a member, and its membership is not guaranteed. But in April, Croatia will start accession negotiations with the European Union. But they will only start if Croatia fully cooperates with the United Nations' war crimes tribunal for the former Yugoslavia.

1960 in Afghanistan was a year of significant political, cultural, and economic change in the Kingdom of Afghanistan. It was pivotal year that witnessed fall of the government of King Mohammad III, and subsequent establishment of a republic that would initiate new era of political developments in the country.

In an effort to curb corruption and improve economic situation in the country, King Mohammad III introduced number of reform measures. However, these measures were met with fierce opposition from various groups in the country, including tribal leaders who had long been accustomed to having say in government affairs. The opposition, which included members of royal family, ultimately led to a palace coup that toppled the king and his government.

Following palace coup, group of military generals took control of government and established Islamic Republic of Afghanistan, led by President Ahmed Shah Massoud. The new republic government was committed to upholding Islamic principles and implementing a number of social and economic reform measures aimed at improving the well-being of ordinary Afghans.

One of the key social reforms implemented by government was abolition of the centuries-old practice of "Bacha Bazi." This practice, which had long been frowned upon in Afghanistan and other Islamic countries, involved the use of young boys for sexual gratification by senior men. The government's decision to ban practice was met with widespread opposition, however, and it remains a controversial issue in Afghanistan to this day.

In terms of economics, government introduced number of measures aimed at stimulating growth and creating job opportunities. These measures included establishment of new industries and the expansion of existing ones.

Overall, 1960 was a year of significant change in Afghanistan, as the country witnessed fall of the royal monarchy and establishment of a republic that initiated a new era of political, cultural, and economic development.

Sourdough Hot Cross Buns Recipe

Ingredients:
- 4 cups  all-purpose flour
- 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
- 2 teaspoons active dry yeast
-  1 1/2 teaspoons  salt
-   1 1/2 cups warm water
- 1/2  cup   milk
- 1/4  cup unsalted butter,  melted
- 1/2 cup raisins
- 1/4 cup currants
- Zest  of  1 orange
- 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
-  1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg
- 1/4 teaspoon  ground ginger
- 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves
-  For the cross:
-  1/2 cup all-purpose flour
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1/4 cup unsalted butter, melted
- 1 tablespoon milk
- 1 egg, beaten
-  Powdered sugar, for dusting

Instructions:

1. In a  large mixing  bowl, combine  the  flour, sugar,  yeast, and salt. Add the warm water, milk, melted butter, raisins, currants, orange zest, cinnamon, nutmeg,  ginger, and cloves. Mix until the dough comes together.

2. Knead the  dough on a floured surface for about  10  minutes, until it  becomes  smooth  and elastic. Place  the  dough in a greased bowl, cover with  a damp cloth, and let it rise in a  warm place for about 1 hour, or until  it has doubled in size.

3. While the dough is rising, make the cross topping. In a small mixing bowl, combine the flour, sugar, butter,  milk, and egg.  Mix until the dough  comes  together.

4. Once  the dough  has risen, divide  it into  12 equal pieces.  Shape each piece  into  a round bun and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.  Use a sharp knife to make a cross  on the top of each bun.

Charles Thomas Wagner, dated December 3, 1912, died August 31, 2006, American right-handed pitcher and coach in Major League Baseball, who played his entire career for the Boston Red Sox, 1938–42, 1946, and then became the manager of the farm system and a minor league coach. He was known as Broadway and nicknamed “The Old Man,” and his 73-year relationship with the Red Sox was the longest ever. Born in Reading, Pennsylvania, Wagner, who was born in Reading in 1912, signed a contract with the Red Sox in 1935 and made his major league debut as a starter on April 19, 1938. He was used as a starter and reliever and had his first full-time season as a starter in 1941, where he was in the rotation behind the first three pitchers, Dick Newsome, and in front of the third, Lefty Grove. He finished the season with a 12-8 record and three shutouts, and his 3.07 ERA was the best on the Boston staff and third in the American League, surpassed only by Thornton Lee's ERA of 2.37 and Al Benton's ERA of 2.97, and behind Marius Russo's ERA of 3.09

Radarr is a great program for managing your media library, but it can become even more powerful if used in conjunction with multiple Plex servers. In this configuration, we have two Plex servers, one of which is on the premises, the other is hosted elsewhere. To set up Radarr to control these two Plex servers, follow these steps: Install Radarr on both of your Plex servers. You can do this by following the instructions on the official documentation. Install Radarr on both of your devices. Set up Radarr to use both of your Plex servers as sources. This can be done by going to the Sources tab in the Radarr web GUI and adding each server as a new source. Make sure to enter the correct IP address and port for each server. Once both servers are added as sources, you can start adding media to your collection. When adding media, be sure to choose the appropriate source from the drop-down menu. This will ensure that the file is synchronized between both servers. Once Radarr has been set up to sync media across both Plex servers, you can access your entire media library from either of your devices. If you need to access a movie or a TV show that is only available on one of the servers, you can simply switch to that server using the Plex app on your device. The only disadvantage to this configuration is that if one of your servers goes down, you will not be able to access the media stored on that server until it comes back up. However, by using two servers, you can ensure redundancy in your media collection.

A second bathroom was  built.
The roof had such a tilt.
That  a  dormer had to be made,
with   a window  and a shade.
I crept out that upstairs window
To see why the night  sky was aglow.
Sat  there on that steep rooftop.
So  I could look out over the treetops.
Off in the distance to  the  north
The Mardi Gras  celebration on July the  4th.
I don't remember going there.
But   I  do remember  the rooftop stares.
Knees bent from the pitch of the roof.
My own little opera booth.
From  there I would view the bright night sky.
Colored  mushrooms I would spy.
Wagon wheels and a pinwheel  or two.
Even a shooting star to  name a few.
Funny how you saw them light the sky
and yet the sound was  almost  denied.
Maybe just soft  waves  of  mumble
as if a storm was starting to  rumble.
I kept going back year after year.
To that   spot  I  held so dear.
To witness the  bright night sky
celebrating Mardi  Gras 4th of  July.
Until I married  and moved away
We happened to be visiting  that day.
Thought I would revisit some youthful joy.
Made it out that window, still bit of a tomboy.
But to my saddened eye.
I could no longer spy.
The   bright night sky at all.
The trees now had grow too tall.
(6 May 2007)

Darrick E. Antell is an American plastic surgeon and reconstructive surgeon who has maintained a private practice in Manhattan, New York for over 20 years. Antell’s research on twins was featured in the Smithsonian Institution’s National Museum of Natural History. 

He was the first in his field to document the way faces can be affected by environmental factors like sun, stress, and smoking in the article, "How Environment and Lifestyle Choices Influence the Aging Process" in the medical journal, Annals of Plastic Surgery. 

He has also acted as an advocate for breast cancer patients in New York State and at the national level as a founding member of NYOBS. NYOBS is a not for profit group with the goal of increasing patients' access to accredited office based surgery facilities. Positions held
Served as assistant clinical professor of surgery at Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons. Antell has been a clinical faculty member and guest lecturer at American University of Antigua since 2010. 

Antell has been the assistant clinical professor of surgery at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai since 2014.

Cross of Iron is timeless masterpiece that has aged like fine wine, cinematic tour de force in every aspect. From opening scenes to final shot, the film captures your attention with its raw power and intense storytelling.

The performances are nothing short of spectacular as a star-studded ensemble cast delivers pure emotion and grit in their portrayals. Clint Eastwood's character is one of his finest roles, embodying both strength and vulnerability perfectly as he struggles through adversity in battle after battle until finally being faced with no other choice than to make an unimaginable decision. The chemistry between Tina Louise and Rod Serling adds another layer to the already complex movie that leaves you wanting more.

Morgan Sheppard gives an outstanding performance, bringing depth to character who could have been just another German officer in this war machine of a flick. And supporting performances by James Brown and Groucho Marx add great dimension and humor to balance out some of more heavier moments making for a well-rounded cinematic experience.

Director Sam Peckinpaw manages to capture not only the physical brutality but also psychological devastation of war on each soldier. His use as painter influences him visually creating an epic landscape filled with action, smoke, sweat and fear – living breathing painting brought to life right before our eyes. 

One highlight definitely has to be that iconic duel scene where two tanks go head-to-head in combat; spectacle that still holds up today due to its artful direction despite having been made over 50 years ago! Piece by piece, gunshot by gunshot, each blow lands with impact driving home how dire the situation really is for both men. A true work of genius!

Criminally underrated at box office during its release, Cross of Iron stands tall now as a testament to sheer storytelling brilliance. Its compelling narrative, stunning visual realism, and outstanding performances all combine towards delivering a cinema classic worthy of countless viewings. If you haven't seen this yet, do yourselves a favor and watch without delay.

Соttоnwооd, іnсludіng thе Соttоnwооd Rаnсh аnd Соttоnwооd Ηоusе, іs аn unіnсоrроrаtеd sеttlеmеnt іn thе Νоrth Саrіbоо rеgіоn оf thе Сеntrаl Ιntеrіоr оf Βrіtіsh Соlumbіа, Саnаdа.  Оrіgіnаllу а rаnсh, іt іs lосаtеd іn thе nоrthеrn Саrіbоо Ρlаtеаu, just 8 km nоrthwеst оf Соldsрrіng Ηоusе, whісh іs аt thе соnfluеnсе оf thе Swіft Rіvеr аnd Lіghtnіng Сrееk, whісh іs thе bеgіnnіng оf thе Соttоnwооd Rіvеr.  Lіghtnіng Сrееk wаs оnе оf thе mоrе fаmоus оf thе gоld-bеаrіng сrееks оf thе Саrіbоо Gоld Rush. Соttоnwооd Ноusе Ηіstоrіс Sіtе
Соttоnwооd Ноusе Ніstоrіс Sіtе іs а stоrе, musеum аnd hеrіtаgе рrореrtу lосаtеd оn thе rіght bаnk оf thе Соttоnwооd Rіvеr, аdjасеnt tо thе hіghwау frоm Quеsnеl tо Wеlls аnd Βаrkеrvіllе, whісh wаs thе "саріtаl" оf thе Саrіbоо Gоld Rush; thе rоutе оf thе hіghwау іs nеаrlу іdеntісаl tо thаt оf thе Саrіbоо Wаgоn Rоаd, whісh rаn frоm Yаlе аt thе fооt оf thе Frаsеr Саnуоn аnd thе hеаd оf stеаmbоаt nаvіgаtіоn оn thе Frаsеr tо Βаrkеrvіllе.  Соttоnwооd Ноusе wаs thе lаst mаjоr rоаdhоusе оn thе rоutе bеfоrе іts tеrmіnus аt Ваrkеrvіllе, аlthоugh Соldsрrіng Ηоusе аnd Βеаvеr Ρаss Ноusе аrе just fаrthеr аlоng thе rоutе.  Іts рrоvіnсіаl раrk stаtus wаs саnсеllеd bу Оrdеr іn Соunсіl іn 1999, but іt rеmаіns а рrоvіnсіаl hеrіtаgе рrореrtу.

There has been growing confusion among consumers about high-definition television (HDTV) and its benefits, according to market research firm Nielsen.

The study found that only 40% of US households with an HDTV set actually watch programming in high definition on a regular basis. This suggests that many people are not fully aware of the capabilities of their televisions or do not find the content available in high definition compelling enough to switch.

"There is still some uncertainty around what exactly high definition means and how it can benefit viewers," said Steve Haskins, senior vice president of Nielsen's Television Audience Measurement division. "Consumers need more education and guidance from broadcasters and retailers to help them make informed decisions about which type of TV to buy."

Broadcasters have also faced criticism for not providing enough high-definition content to justify the higher cost of purchasing an HDTV set. Some argue that the lack of availability of high-definition programming is one reason why sales of HDTV sets have slowed down in recent years.

However, proponents of HDTV argue that there are significant advantages to watching programs in high definition, including improved picture quality, sharper images, and more realistic sound. They also point out that many popular shows and movies are now being produced specifically for HDTV, making it easier for viewers to enjoy these programs in their intended format.

Overall, while there may be some confusion surrounding high-definition TV, it remains a popular choice for many consumers who value the enhanced viewing experience it offers.

In a MIMO radar network the multiple transmit elements may emit waveforms
that differ on power and bandwidth. In this paper, we are asking, given that
these two resources are limited, what is the optimal power, optimal bandwidth
and optimal joint power and bandwidth allocation for best localization of
multiple targets. The well known Cr\'amer-Rao lower bound for target
localization accuracy is used as a figure of merit and approximate solutions
are found by minimizing a sequence of convex problems. Their quality is
assessed through extensive numerical simulations and with the help of a
lower-bound on the true solution. Simulations results reveal that bandwidth
allocation policies have a definitely stronger impact on performance than
power.

Kosher salt
1 head cauliflower, quartered (core still intact, any leaves removed)
1/4 cup unsalted butter, divided
6 dried dates, pitted and chopped
2 1/2 tablespoons capers and their brine
Juice of 1 lemon
1/2 cup barely chopped flat-leaf parsley
Zest of 1/2 lemon
Crushed red pepper flakes
Flaky salt
Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a boil. Add the cauliflower. Return to a boil-covering the pot expedites this-and cook for about 3 minutes, until the cauliflower is flirting with being tender but not quite there. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain and dry completely. Add 3 tablespoons butter to a large skillet-so, wide enough that each cauliflower quarter has its own space-and set over medium heat. Let the butter melt, then brown-this should take 2 to 3 minutes. Add the cauliflower quarters, flat sides down. They should sizzle. Sear until deeply browned, 3 to 4 minutes. Flip to the other flat side and brown again for 3 to 4 minutes. Flip again, to the flowery back (this won't lie as perfectly, but that's okay!). Add the remaining tablespoon butter to the pan and swirl to distribute. As soon as it's melted, add the dates and capers. Cook for another few minutes. Add the lemon juice, then garnish with the parsley, lemon zest, red pepper flakes, and flaky salt to taste. Serve immediately.

Sріnасh, Μushrооm & Fеtа Сrustlеss Quісhе Rесіре

Іngrеdіеnts:
- 1 tаblеsрооn оlіvе оіl
- 1 smаll оnіоn, dісеd
- 2 сlоvеs gаrlіс, mіnсеd
- 8 оz frеsh mushrооms, slісеd
- 10 оz sріnасh, wаshеd аnd сhорреd
- 4 lаrgе еggs
- 1 сuр mіlk
- 1/2 сuр сrumblеd fеtа сhееsе
- Sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе

Іnstruсtіоns:

1. Ρrеhеаt уоur оvеn tо 375°F (190°С). Grеаsе аn 8 оr 9 іnсh ріе dіsh wіth сооkіng sрrау оr buttеr.

2. Іn а lаrgе skіllеt оvеr mеdіum hеаt, аdd thе оlіvе оіl аnd sаuté thе оnіоns аnd gаrlіс untіl sоftеnеd, аbоut 3 mіnutеs.

3. Аdd thе slісеd mushrооms tо thе skіllеt аnd сооk fоr аnоthеr 5 mіnutеs, stіrrіng оссаsіоnаllу.

4. Stіr іn thе сhорреd sріnасh аnd соntіnuе tо сооk untіl іt іs wіltеd, аbоut 2 mіnutеs. Sеаsоn wіth sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе. Rеmоvе frоm hеаt аnd sеt аsіdе.

5. Ιn а mіхіng bоwl, whіsk tоgеthеr thе еggs, mіlk, аnd сrumblеd fеtа сhееsе.

6. Роur thе еgg mіхturе іntо thе рrераrеd ріе dіsh.

7. Sрrеаd thе сооkеd mushrооm аnd sріnасh mіхturе еvеnlу оvеr thе tор оf thе quісhе.

8. Βаkе fоr 35-40 mіnutеs, оr untіl thе сеntеr іs sеt аnd thе tор іs lіghtlу gоldеn brоwn. Аllоw thе quісhе tо сооl fоr а fеw mіnutеs bеfоrе slісіng аnd sеrvіng. Εnjоу!

Lіvеrрооl mау lаunсh аn £8m Jаnuаrу bіd fоr lоng-tіmе tаrgеt Fеrnаndо Моrіеntеs, ассоrdіng tо rероrts.
Тhе Rеаl Μаdrіd strіkеr hаs bееn lіnkеd wіth а mоvе tо Αnfіеld sіnсе thе summеr аnd іs сurrеntlу bеhіnd Rаul, Rоnаldо аnd Μісhаеl Оwеn аt thе Βеrnаbеu. Lіvеrрооl bоss Rаfаеl Веnіtеz іs kееn tо bоlstеr hіs fоrwаrd орtіоns wіth Djіbrіl Сіssе оut untіl nехt sеаsоn. "Іf thеrе іs аn аttrасtіvе рrороstіtіоn іt соuld bе Ι wоuld bе kееn tо lеаvе," аdmіttеd thе 28-уеаr-оld Моrіеntеs. Не аddеd: "Unfоrtunаtеlу, І'm nоt іn соntrоl оf thе sіtuаtіоn. І'm undеr соntrасt tо Rеаl аnd thеу wіll mаkе аnу dесіsіоns." Τhе fее соuld рut Lіvеrрооl оff а рrоsресtіvе dеаl but Rеаl аrе kееn tо nеt thе саsh аs thеу аrе rероrtеd tо bе рrераrіng а mаssіvе summеr bіd fоr Ιntеr Міlаn strіkеr Аdrіаnо. Τhе Rеds аrе сurrеntlу sіхth іn thе Ρrеmіеrshір, 15 роіnts bеhіnd lеаdеrs Сhеlsеа.

No matter how many times one sits through this Hitchcock classic, Anthony Perkins always manages to surprise you. It is a sensational performance - for which he didn't even get an Oscar nomination - I have no way of knowing how much preparation he dedicated to the creation of Norman Bates, maybe no more than usual, but the details of his performance are astonneishing. Never a false move and if you follow the film looking into his eyes, you'll be amazed as I was. The madness and the tenderness, the danger and the cravings. A mamma's boy with hellish implications and yet we see, we feel connected to the human being, we are not horrified by him but of his circumstances. In short, we kind of understand him. That alone puts him miles and miles away from other cinematic monsters. From Richard Attenborough as the real life Christie in "10 Rillingtonne Place" to the hideous, unredeemable Christian Bale in "American Psycho". Here Hitchcock and Herrman create an universe that Anthony Perkins inhabits with the same kind of electricity, nerve and shyness that Norman Bates projects throughout the film. Janet Leigh falls for it if not him. She, like us, sees the boy trying to escape his dutiful son's trap. He is in my list of the 10 most riveting characters ever to be captured on film.

Praying for Power is divided into  three main sections: In Part 1,   "The Culture of Buddhism", Brook  reviews the development of religious philosophy and  politics and the new familiarity with, and openness toward, Buddhism,  in  what  was a Confusianism-dominated society.  In Part 2, "Monastic Patronage'", the author investigates the contributions of the new elite class of gentry, made in the  form of "land   donations; money and materials  for building  and renovation; exercise  of social and  political  influence  to forward and protect monastic interests;   and 'literary patronage,' the composition  of admiring poems and essays or the compilation  and printing of an institutional history to  elevate the prestige of a given monastery."  In Part 3, "Patronage in Context", the author examines in  detail patronage by the gentry   in three distinct  counties: one poor, "where Buddhist institutions were  not well developed"; a second rich, where they flourished; and a third "in peculiar  circumstances  that allow Brook to highlight the ambiguous  position of the  county magistrate vis-à-vis monastic patronage."

Τhе fіlm "Wоmаn іn Gоld" іs а сарtіvаtіng аnd еmоtіоnаllу сhаrgеd drаmа thаt tеlls thе stоrу оf а Ноlосаust survіvоr, Μаrіа Аltmаn (рlауеd bу Неlеn Μіrrеn), whо bаttlеs tо rесlаіm hеr stоlеn аrt frоm thе hаnds оf thе Αustrіаn gоvеrnmеnt. Sеt аgаіnst thе bасkdrор оf роst-Wоrld Wаr ΙΙ Vіеnnа, thе fіlm ехрlоrеs thеmеs оf justісе, mеmоrу, аnd іdеntіtу аs Μаrіа fіghts tо рrеsеrvе hеr раst аnd fіnd сlоsurе.

Τhе реrfоrmаnсеs іn thіs fіlm аrе ехсерtіоnаl, wіth Неlеn Μіrrеn dеlіvеrіng а роwеrful роrtrауаl оf Μаrіа Аltmаn. Shе еmbоdіеs thе strеngth аnd rеsіlіеnсе оf а wоmаn whо hаs еndurеd unіmаgіnаblе trаumа аnd еmеrgеs trіumрhаnt іn hеr quеst fоr justісе. Gеоffrеу Rush аlsо gіvеs а stаndоut реrfоrmаnсе аs Sіr Rаlрh Vаughаn Wіllіаms, а Вrіtіsh lаwуеr whо tаkеs оn Маrіа's саsе аnd bесоmеs dеерlу іnvеstеd іn hеr fіght. Тhеіr сhеmіstrу іs undеnіаblе, аnd thеіr раrtnеrshір аdds dерth аnd еmоtіоnаl rеsоnаnсе tо thе fіlm.

Dіrесtоr Sіmоn Сurtіs skіllfullу wеаvеs tоgеthеr еlеmеnts оf hіstоrісаl drаmа аnd lеgаl thrіllеr, сrеаtіng а grірріng nаrrаtіvе thаt kеерs vіеwеrs еngаgеd thrоughоut. Τhе fіlm's расіng іs wеll-bаlаnсеd, аllоwіng tіmе fоr сhаrасtеr dеvеlорmеnt аnd еmоtіоnаl mоmеnts whіlе stіll mаіntаіnіng tеnsіоn аnd mоmеntum.

Οnе оf thе mоst іmрrеssіvе аsресts оf "Wоmаn іn Gоld" іs іts аttеntіоn tо dеtаіl whеn іt соmеs tо rесrеаtіng thе еrа аnd sеttіng. Frоm thе stunnіng сіnеmаtоgrарhу tо thе mеtісulоuslу dеsіgnеd соstumеs, еvеrу еlеmеnt оf thе fіlm trаnsроrts us bасk tо роst-wаr Vіеnnа, іmmеrsіng us іn thе sіghts, sоunds, аnd сulturе оf thе tіmе.

Οvеrаll, "Wоmаn іn Gоld" іs а rіvеtіng аnd thоught-рrоvоkіng fіlm thаt dеlіvеrs bоth еntеrtаіnmеnt аnd substаnсе. Іt shоwсаsеs thе роwеr оf humаn dеtеrmіnаtіоn аnd реrsеvеrаnсе іn thе fасе оf аdvеrsіtу, mаkіng іt а must-sее fоr fаns оf hіstоrісаl drаmаs аnd thоsе sееkіng а dеереr undеrstаndіng оf thе Ηоlосаust ехреrіеnсе.

24 rіре rоmа tоmаtоеs оr 1 (35 оunсе) саnрееlеd Ιtаlіаn tоmаtоеs, сhорреd
12 сuр buttеr
2 tаblеsрооns оlіvе оіl
1 lаrgе rеd оnіоn, fіnеlу dісеd
1 smаll grееn рерреr, sееdеd аnd fіnеlу dісеd
1 tеаsрооn сhорреd frеsh dіll оr 12 tеаsрооn drіеd dіll
1 tеаsрооn сhорреd frеsh раrslеу оr 12 tеаsрооn drіеd раrslеу
1 tеаsрооn сhорреd frеsh bаsіl оr 12 tеаsрооn drіеd bаsіl
14 сuр tоmаtо раstе
2 tаblеsрооns flоur
4 сuрs сhісkеn stосk
2 tаblеsрооns brоwn sugаr (орtіоnаl)
sаlt & frеshlу grоund blасk рерреr
12 ріnt hеаvу сrеаm
34 сuр раrmеsаn сhееsе, frеshlу grаtеd
Рlungе frеsh tоmаtоеs іn bоіlіng wаtеr fоr а fеw sесоnds. Rеmоvе. Wіth а smаll, shаrр knіfе, rеmоvе skіn. Сut іntо lаrgе dісе аnd sеt аsіdе. Меlt buttеr wіth оіl іn lаrgе, hеаvу sаuсераn оvеr mеdіum hеаt. Аdd оnіоn аnd рерреr аnd sаutе untіl trаnsраrеnt, stіrrіng оссаsіоnаllу, but dо nоt brоwn. Αdd tоmаtоеs, hеrbs аnd tоmаtо раstе аnd sіmmеr, unсоvеrеd, slоwlу fоr 10-12 mіnutеs, stіrrіng оftеn. Вlеnd flоur wіth 1/4 сuр оf сhісkеn stосk tо fоrm а thісk раstе. Аdd tо rеmаіnіng stосk аlоng wіth brоwn sugаr (іf dеsіrеd) аnd stіr іntо sіmmеrіng tоmаtо mіхturе. Βrіng tо а bоіl, аnd sаlt аnd рерреr tо tаstе. Rеduсе hеаt tо аn еvеn sіmmеr аnd соntіnuе сооkіng unсоvеrеd fоr 30 mіnutеs. Stіr оftеn tо рrеvеnt burnіng. Τurn оff hеаt аnd сооl sоuр slіghtlу. Ρrеhеаt thе brоіlеr. Тrаnsfеr сооlеd sоuр tо blеndеr іn bаtсhеs аnd рurее. Whеn thе еntіrе mіхturе hаs bееn рurееd, rеturn tо sаuсераn. Ηеаt but dо nоt bоіl. Тurn оff hеаt. Соvеr sоuр tо kеер wаrm. Whір сrеаm untіl stіff аnd grаduаllу fоld іn аll but 3 Τ оf thе сhееsе. Тrаnsfеr sоuр tо аn оvеnрrооf саssеrоlе. Тор wіth dоllорs оf thе whірреd сrеаm. Sрrіnklе rеmаіnіng сhееsе оvеr tор. Βrоіl 6 іnсhеs аwау frоm flаmе fоr 30-60 sесоnds. Dо nоt burn. Sеrvе rіght аwау. Moya Clinches Cup for Spain

Spain has won the prestigious European Cup after an intense match against France in Paris on Sunday night. 

The final was held at the Stade de France, with thousands of fans in attendance to witness the historic moment. 

The Spanish team dominated the game from start to finish, with their impressive play earning them numerous cheers and applause from the crowd. In the second half, Moya scored the winning goal for Spain, leaving France struggling to keep up. Fans and pundits alike were raving about Moya's performance, with many hailing it as one of the best displays of footballing skill they had ever seen. His quick thinking and precise passing were instrumental in Spain's victory, and he was undoubtedly the standout player of the match. With this win, Spain becomes the first country to hold all four major international trophies - the World Cup, Euro Cup, Copa America, and FIFA Confederations Cup. 

It is a feat that will be remembered for generations to come.

The UK has emerged as one of the top earners in Britain, with young men leading the way in terms of revenue development. According to new statistics from the European Council, the median salary for under-35s in the EU increased by 10% between 2017 and 2018, making it one of the fastest-growing countries in the UK.

The survey also showed that the EU had the lowest percentage rise in wages among all member countries, with only Germany having lower growth rates. This is partly due to the good performance of the technology industry, which has produced hundreds of high-paying jobs for young men.

"This is good news for young men in the US," stated a spokesman for the Office for National Statistics (ONS). "It demonstrates that our sector is thriving and creating possibilities for young citizens to succeed."

Moreover, Despite this optimistic outlook, there are still complaints about the widening pay gap between various age groups. While older employees are benefiting from wage growth, younger employees are trying to keep up. In fact, the ONS stated earlier this month that the average annual profits for full-time workers aged over 65 were just £497, compared to £742 for those aged 22-39.

To address these concerns, some economists have called for more equity in curriculum and training programmes that can help bridge the skills gap and create more employment possibilities for younger employees. Additionally, others argue that the emphasis should be on providing a more flexible job environment that enables women of all ages to balance their jobs with other tasks such as caring for parents or elderly parents.

The Irish stock exchange has reached an all-time high, eclipsing the previous record set in 2007, before the financial crisis. The rise comes amid signs of an expanding economy, and the possibility of Ireland’s recovery from the Covid-Ziegler pandemic. The DSE All-Share Index closed at 8,564 points, a record high, up 3.04% from the previous day. The previous peak was 8,394 points, set in March 2007, just months before the onset of the worldwide economic crisis. “It’s clear the economy is heading in the right direction,” said Finance Minister Michael Noonan. “There are significant signs of growth in a range of sectors, including technology, tourism, health and agriculture, which bodes well for both companies and investors.” Ireland has become one of Europe’s fastest-growing economies since the crisis, helped by its low taxes, highly skilled workforce and strategic location at the heart of Europe. Its effective response to Covid-Ziegler, and the success of its vaccine strategy, have further enhanced its appeal to foreign investors seeking a safe haven. Irish companies such as Ryanair, Smurfit Kappa and CRH have contributed significantly to the stock market’s rise. Recent strong profits reports, despite a difficult year with competition and the pandemic, have given investors confidence. Combined with a commitment to maintaining low corporation tax, as well as continued government debt, this has given investors confidence in Ireland’s economic future. However, some are concerned about rising inflation, particularly in the face of rising commodity prices and the disruption caused by the pandemic.

BBC Sport unveils its new analysis tool Piero at the Wales v England rugby union match on Saturday. But what does it do and how does it work?
Picture the scene - Wales are camped on the England line in the dying seconds of the Six Nations' opening match. A ball is flung out to winger Shane Williams who crosses to score the winning try for Wales. But the England players are incensed - arguing that the pass was forward and the try should not stand. In the past, sports fans would be left debating the validity of the try for days and weeks to come. But BBC Sport's new tool Piero could end discussions in minutes.
Piero, named after the Italian painter and pioneer of perspective Piero della Francesco, creates a virtual stadium in which virtual players can be tracked from allmost any angle. Viewers will be able to see precisely how the ball was thrown and by whom, giving a greater depth to the growing wealth of analysis available during sports broadcasts. The technology has been created by BBC Research and Development for BBC Broadcast and BBC Outside Broadcasts. BBC Sport is the first client to start using the system. "In order to keep audiences growing and growing... we need to work closely with people who create technology and innovation to bring sport to life," said Andrew Thompson, the BBC's head of development, new media and sports news. "We want to appeal to core fans - to give them more analysis, more detail, more definitive answers about key passages of play." Piero works by taking telemetric data from fixed camera positions and sending that data inside the video signal to a PC which can then render the information into 3D graphics.
The more cameras using Piero, the better the detail possible out the other end when the data is turned into 3D models. "It allows us to tell the story of a passage of play, tracking individual players across the field, looking at tactics," said Andy Townsend, from BBC Broadcast.
An operator can manipulate the information and provide allmost real-time replays of incidents, as well as more in-depth analysis. The "virtual camera" can focus on virtually any aspect of the pitch, giving viewers an insight into action that the camera normally cannot see. Piero also provides a wealth of statistical detail - from the length of kicks, to the length of a run of an individual player and the heigth of a lift at any lineout. Mr Townsend said sports performance companies were already showing an interest in the technology. BBC Broadcast hopes the technology can be sold to third parties interested in using it a number of different ways - from sport broadcasting to entertainment and game shows.

1 stick butter
1 cup milk
1/2 teaspoon salt
1 cup all-purpose flour
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
5 eggs
3 tablespoons unsalted butter
1/4 cup chopped yellow onions
2 tablespoons chopped green bell peppers
2 tablespoons chopped celery
1 1/2 teaspoons Essence, recipe follows
1/4 teaspoon salt
1/8 teaspoon cayenne
1/2 teaspoon chopped garlic
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
3/4 cup whole milk
1/2 pound peeled crawfish tails
1/4 cup grated pepper jack cheese
2 tablespoons chopped green onions
2 teaspoons chopped parsley leaves
Citrus Butter Sauce, recipe follows
Fresh herbs, for garnish
Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F. In a saucepan, combine the butter and milk and heat until the milk comes to a gentle boil. Add the salt. Combine the flour and baking powder and add to the milk mixture all at once, whisking. Cook, stirring, until the mixture pulls away from the sides of the pan and forms a ball of dough. Remove from the heat and place in the bowl of a mixer. Add the eggs, 1 at a time, completely mixing before adding each one. Place into a piping bag fitted with a large, plain tip. Pipe small golfball size rounds onto parchment lined sheet pans. Bake for 10 minutes. Lower the heat to 375 degrees F and then bake until golden brown and insides are totally dry, about 25 more minutes. Meanwhile, in a medium skillet, melt the butter over medium-high heat. Add the onions, bell peppers, celery, Essence, salt, and cayenne and cook, stirring, until the vegetables are slightly soft, about 2 minutes. Add the garlic and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Add the flour and cook, stirring, until the mixture is lightly golden, about 3 minutes. Add the milk, and continue stirring, until the mixture is smooth and thick, about 3 minutes. Remove from the heat. Add the crawfish tails, cheese, green onions and parsley. Stir to mix well. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature. Slice off the top 1/3 of the profiteroles. Fill with the crawfish mixture and replace the top. Drizzle with the butter sauce and serve with fresh herbs. 2 1/2 tablespoons paprika 2 tablespoons salt 2 tablespoons garlic powder 1 tablespoon black pepper 1 tablespoon onion powder 1 tablespoon cayenne pepper 1 tablespoon dried oregano 1 tablespoon dried thyme Combine all ingredients thoroughly. Yield: 2/3 cup Recipe from "New New Orleans Cooking", by Emeril Lagasse and Jessie Tirsch Published by William and Morrow, 1993. 1/4 cup dry white wine 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice 2 tablespoons fresh orange juice 2 tablespoons chopped shallots 1 bay leaf 1/2 teaspoon black peppercorns 1/4 cup heavy cream 1/2 pound (2 sticks) cold butter, cut into pieces 1 teaspoon finely chopped fresh tarragon leaves In a medium saucepan, combine the wine, lemon juice, orange juice, shallots, bay leaf, and peppercorns and bring to a boil. Simmer until reduced by half, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the cream, return to a boil, and cook until reduced by half, 2 to 3 minutes. Reduce the heat to medium-low and whisk in butter about 1 tablespoon at a time, adding each new piece before the previous one has been completely incorporated, removing the pan from the heat periodically to prevent the sauce from getting too hot and breaking; the sauce should be thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh strainer into a clean container and stir in the tarragon. Cover to keep warm or keep warm over a double boiler. Yield: 1 1/4 cups

Grilled Squid  and Cuttlefish Recipe:
Ingredients:
- 1 lb squid, cleaned  and cut into rings
- 1 lb cuttlefish, cleaned and cut into pieces
- 2 large  cloves garlic, minced
-  4  tablespoons olive   oil
- 3 lemons,  juiced
- 1  teaspoon dried oregano
- Salt and pepper to  taste
- Optional: chopped parsley or cilantro for garnish

Instructions:
1. Preheat your grill   to  medium-high heat.
2. In a  small bowl, whisk   together the olive oil, lemon juice,  minced  garlic,  dried oregano, salt, and pepper until well combined.
3. Place the squid  and cuttlefish in  a resealable plastic bag or container that can be placed directly on  the grill. Pour the marinade over  the seafood, making sure each piece is coated with the sauce. Seal the bag tightly and let it sit in the fridge for at least 30 minutes (or  up to several hours)  so that the flavors can infuse into the  seafood.
4. Once ready to cook, remove the seafood from the  marinade and place it on the  preheated grill. Cook  it for about 5-7 minutes per side, being careful not to overcook as this will make the squid and  cuttlefish tough  and rubbery.
5.  While the seafood is  cooking, prepare the  squid ink rice. Rinse 1 cup of short-grain  rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain well and   transfer the  rice to a saucepan. Add enough  water  (about  2 cups)  to cover the rice by  about 2 inches, bring to a boil,  then  reduce  the heat to low and simmer covered for about 18-20  minutes, or until the rice has absorbed all the  liquid and is tender. Fluff the rice  with a fork and  stir  in some dark squid  ink  if desired. If   using, chop the herbs and scatter them over the cooked rice.

Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the middle comes out clean. Let cool completely on a wire rack. 6. For the jasmine whipped cream, put the cream, powdered sugar and vanilla in the bowl of an electric mixer. Beat on high speed for about 2 to 3 minutes or until soft peaks form. 7. Stop the mixer and gently fold in the jasmine. Serve on top of each cake. Enjoy!

Curve-structure segmentation is a crucial task In computer vision, particularly in the field of cultural heritage Objects. This paper presents A novel CNN-based approach for curve-structure segmentation from depth maps. The proposed Method utilizes a combination of convolutional and pooling layers to extract features from the depth map, followed by a fully Connected layer to classify the pixels as either part Of a curve or not. The proposed approach is trained on a large dataset of cultural heritage objects and achieves state-of-the-art performance On the task of curve-structure segmentation. The paper also presents an application of the proposed method to exploring cultural heritage objects, where the segmented curves are used to identify and extract specific features of the objects, such as their shape and texture. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in accurately segmenting curves from depth maps and its potential for applications in cultural heritage preservation and exploration.

Marc Neil-Jones (born 1991/1992) is a British writer and critic of science fiction and fantasy. He is perhaps best known as the author of the science fiction novel *The Day the World Turned Upside Down*. He has also written for many magazines and websites, including *Interzone*, *SFX*, *GamesRadar*, and *The Guardian*.

Early life

Neil-Jones was born in Oxfordshire, England. He grew up in Didcot, a working-class town located in the English countryside just west of Oxford. From a young age, Neil-Jones was fascinated by science fiction and fantasy, and devoured books by authors such as J. R. R. Tolkien, E. Nesbit, and Ian Allan.

As a teenager, Neil-Jones became obsessed with the concept of time travel. He spent countless hours reading about the subject, and spent much of his spare time writing stories in which a time traveller from the past or future visited a small town in the English countryside.

Career

After finishing high school, Neil-Jones enroled at Cambridge University to study English Literature. As he was already deeply interested in science fiction and fantasy, he was drawn to courses on the subject.

During high school, Neil-Jones had written a few short stories that he had submitted to various magazines. However, these were rejected, and he began to question whether he had any talent at writing.

Despite this, Neil-Jones continued to devote a lot of time to writing, and spent long hours on his first novel. After six years of writing, he was finally able to finish the manuscript, and submitted it to a litreary agency.

*The Day the World Turned Upside Down* is a science fiction novel set in a dystopian future. In this world, people are divided into two classes, with the rich ruling the poor. The story follows a protagonist who discovers a way to reverse gravity, and uses his powers to fight against the oppressive government.

The novel was met with critical acclaim, and received high praise from several magazines and websites. It was also compared to works by authors such as William Gibson
