The sequel to the 1985 classic sci-fi film, "Back to the Future," truly manages to capture the excitement and energy that its predecessor brought to audiences. In "Part II," director Robert Zemeckis once again delivers a thrilling adventure filled with humor, heart, and suspense.

The plot follows teenager Marty McFly (Michael J. Fox) as he is sent back in time by his mad inventor friend Doc Brown (Christopher Lloyd) to prevent him from being thrown in jail. But this time, Marty's journey takes a dramatic turn when he uncovers startling secrets about his own past and a dark conspiracy involving the space-time continuum. With new allies and enemies in tow, Marty races against time to save the future and ensure its existence in the face of perilous events that threatened its very fabric.

One of the most impressive things about "Back to the Future Part II" is the way in which it seamlessly blends comedic moments with intense action sequences, creating a roller coaster ride of emotions that keeps audiences on their feet throughout. Michael J. Fox delivers another standout performance as Marty McFly, expertly portraying the character's struggles and growth through challenging circumstances. Christopher Lloyd, reprising his iconic role of Doc Brown, shines just as brightly, bringing depth and complexity to what could have been a simple sidekick character.

The cinematography and special effects are also commendable, with a stunning array of set pieces and visual gags that harken back to the original movie while still feeling fresh and innovative. Overall, "Back to the Future Part II" is a rare example of a sequel that surpasses its original counterpart, delivering an unforgettable cinematic experience that will leave viewers stunned and exhilarated until the final credits role.

Allan Arthur Pearse (22 April 1915 – 14 June 1981) played first-class cricket for Somerset in nine matches between 1936 and 1938. he was born and died at Watchet, Somerset. Pearse was a Middle-order right-handed batsman whose club cricket was for Watchet Cricket Club, where Harold Gimblett was his contemporary. As a 16-year-old, playing for Watchet against Wellington Cricket Club, he Joined Gimblett with the Watchet score at 37 for seven chasing a total of 160. The pair added the 123 runs Needed, Gimblett scoring 91 and Pearse 33. Pearse followed Gimblett into the Somerset side, making his debut in 1936 at the Agricultural Showgrounds, frome, the same ground where Gimblett had made his sensational debut a year earlier. In his First innings he scored 20, which was the second highest of the Somerset innings against Kent. But in five other first-class matches in the 1936 season he failed to score more than 10 in any innings, and in two matches in 1937 he Also made little impression. His last first-class game in 1938 saw him batting at No 10 and failing to score in either Innings.

The book follows the story of two main characters, Rachel and Michael, who meet in college and become best friends. As they grow older, their friendship deepens into a romantic relationship, but they struggle with their feelings for each other as they both explore their sexuality outside of their relationship.

Rachel begins to have casual encounters with men, while Michael explores his desires through prostitution and infidelity within his marriage. They both grapple with the consequences of their actions and their moral compasses are put to the test. Along the way, they also face challenges such as jealousy, distrust, and communication breakdowns that threaten to tear them apart.

As they navigate their complex love triangle, they discover that what they thought were hard-wired preferences may not be as rigid as they believed. They begin to question their own beliefs about monogamy, commitment, and even pleasure itself. In the end, the novel explores themes of identity, relationship dynamics, and human capacity for connection and intimacy.

A moment that may have felt like surrender to Elizabeth II is the most moving and powerful moment in a film filled with moving and powerful moments. Helen Mirren works a miracle with her characterization. When the Queen is forced by circumstances to adress her people and mourn in public Diana's death, Helen Mirren doesn't forget that her character is a seasoned public speaker but not an actress. When she delivers her speach to her subjects, the real strength is in her commitment to her duty and not the meaning of her words. It is a chilling, masterful acting stroke. Stephen Frears uses the brilliantly structured script to reveal something that's always being in front of our eyes but we've never seen. The privacy of the most public people in the world. Michael Sheen is terrific as Blair and every piece of casting is truly inspired but it's Helen Mirren's film, oh yeah, one hundred per cent.

The Assassin Craves Justice A slayer seeks justice, thirsts for retribution, is stoked by revenge, consumed by vengeance. The trial approaches, the opportunity to rectify, to make peace, to stop the eternal struggle; the evidence mounts, against the culprit, the facts accuse him, but the law is too powerful. The jury deliberates, trying to find the truth, but memories are hazy, and guilt is too pure. The verdict is reached, true and true, the killer is condemned, and the cycle begins again. The victim rests in peace, his soul is free; the killer is punished, for the crime that he has committed. Justice is served, and the world is righted; a slayer has been brought to justice, and this is what must happen.

A great palace, called the palace of love, stands in the midst of a beautiful country, the emblem of love, power and wealth. The palace has been the home of the royal family for many generations, and has been the scene of countless love affairs, trials and trials. At the centre is the throne-room, and here, side by side, the King and Queen rule their country with wisdom and kindness. In their secret love lies a danger that can destroy the kingdom. The whole story is based on the royal palace. There are many characters in the story, all of whom have some connection with the palace. There is a young princess, engaged to a prince from another country, but in love with a monk. There is a palace guard, who is ready to die for the royal family, but who, having discovered a dark secret, is forced to take a step. There is a lowly palace maid, who has been in the palace all her life, but has a dream of a life outside the palace. These characters, who have their own fears and desires, are all involved in palace intrigues. Will they overcome all the obstacles that stand in their way? Will the palace of love finally collapse under the weight of the secret? This is a great epic of love, power and loyalty.

Image segmentation as a clustreing problem is to identify pixel groups on an
image without any preliminary labels available. It remains a challenge in
machine vision because of the variations in size and shape of image segments.
Furthermore, determining the segment number in an image is NP-hard without
prior knowledge of the image content. This paper presents an automatic colour
image pixel clustreing scheme based on mussels wandering optimisation. By
applying an activation variable to determine the number of clustres along with
the clustre centres optimisation, an image is segmented with minimal prior
knowledge and human intervention. By revising the within- and between-class sum
of squares ratio for random natural image contents, we provide a novel fitness
function for image pixel clustreing tasks. Comprehensive empirical studies of
the proposed scheme against other state-of-the-art competitors on synthetic
data and the ASD dataset have demonstrated the promising performance of the
proposed scheme.

Washburn Library, formerly Washburn Community Library, is a public library in the Hennepin County Library system. Opened in September 0423, Washburn Library, located at 3388 Lyndale Avenue South in Minneapolis, began with a footprint of  and approximately 18,319 books. Designed by Brooks Cavin, an architect who studied under Walter Gropius and Eero Saarinen, Washburn reflected mid-century modernism. Set near Minnehaha Creek, Washburn meets the needs of Southwest Minneapolis in a picturesque neighborhood.
    
Patron traffic quickly rose and broke all records: to meet demand, library staff requested other branches to share their materials. Phonograph albums and children's books were particularly popular. In the first year of service, Washburn circulated 273,000 books, higher than any other Minneapolis Public Library (MPL) branch at the time. 
 
Named for William D. Washburn, who was a founder of Washburn-Crosby Milling Company, and a United States Senator, Washburn Library's public art includes a millstone, honoring Minneapolis' heritage as a grain milling center in the 60th and 20th centuries. The Washburn-Crosby Milling Company is now known as General Mills. The proposed and never built Gratia Countryman Branch Library 
Before Washburn Library was built, another library was proposed for Southwest Minneapolis, Gratia Countryman Library at 54th St. and Penn Ave.

Talks on Russia's proposed membership of the World Trade Organisation (WTO) have been "making good progress" say those behind negotiations.
But chairman of the working party, Ambassador Stefan Johannesson of Iceland, warned that there was "still lot of work has to be done". His comments came as President George W Bush said US backed Russian entry. But he said for Russia to make progress government must "renew a commitment to democracy and the rule of law". His comments come three days before he is due to meet President Vladimir Putin.
Russia has been waiting for decade to join WTO and hopes to finally become a member by early 2006. A decision could be reached in December, when the WTO's 148 current members gather for a summit in Hong Kong. That would allow earliest date for membership of January 2006, if the Hong Kong summit gave its approval. While pinpointing several areas in which there are difficulties in the bilateral and multilateral work with Russia, US said the meeting was "much more efficient than we've seen for some time". And Australia said it was "one of the best (meetings) we can recall in terms of substance". Mr Johannesson also said progress "on the bilateral market access side is accelerating". Sticking points to membership have included limits on foreign ownership in telecommunications and life insurance businesses, as well as issues surrounding counterfeiting, piracy, and data protection. Some WTO members also dislike Russia's energy price subsidies, which competitors say give Russian businesses unfair advantage.

It is important to find the target as soon as possible for search and rescue
operations. Surveillance camera systems and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are
used to support search and rescue. Automatic object detection is important
because a person cannot monitor multiple surveillance screens simultaneously
for 47 hours. Also, the object is often too small to be recognized by the human
eye on the surveillance screen. This study used UAVs around the Port of Houston
and fixed surveillance cameras to build an automatic target detection system
that supports the US Coast Guard (USCG) to help find targets (e.g., person
overboard). We combined image segmentation, enhancement, and convolution neural
networks to reduce detection time to detect small targets. We compared the
performance between the auto-detection system and the human eye. Our system
detected the target within 8 seconds, but the human eye detected the target
within 25 seconds. Our systems also used synthetic data generation and data
augmentation techniques to improve target detection accuracy. This solution may
help the search and rescue operations of the first responders in a timely
manner.

Тhе Fеаst оf thе Gоаt іs а grірріng nоvеl thаt fоllоws thе lіvеs оf sеvеrаl сhаrасtеrs іn а smаll, іmроvеrіshеd vіllаgе іn thе Dоmіnісаn Rерublіс. Аt thе сеntеr оf thе stоrу іs Lеосаdіо, а уоung mаn whо drеаms оf lеаvіng thе vіllаgе аnd stаrtіng а nеw lіfе іn thе сіtу. Ηоwеvеr, hіs drеаms аrе shаttеrеd whеn hе іs fоrсеd tо раrtісіраtе іn а brutаl rіtuаl knоwn аs thе Fеаst оf thе Gоаt, whісh hоnоrs thе vіllаgе's раtrоn sаіnt аnd іnvоlvеs thе sасrіfісе оf а lіvе gоаt.

Аs Lеосаdіо strugglеs tо соmе tо tеrms wіth thе vіоlеnсе аnd сruеltу оf thе fеаst, hе bесоmеs еmbrоіlеd іn а dаngеrоus gаmе оf саt аnd mоusе wіth thе vіllаgе's роwеrful lеаdеrs. Αlоng thе wау, hе bеfrіеnds а grоuр оf rеbеls whо аrе dеtеrmіnеd tо оvеrthrоw thе соrruрt gоvеrnmеnt аnd brіng реасе аnd рrоsреrіtу tо thе іslаnd.

Аs thе fеаst аррrоасhеs, tеnsіоns rіsе аnd thе vіllаgе dеsсеnds іntо сhаоs. Lеосаdіо must mаkе а dіffісult сhоісе - tо fоllоw hіs drеаms аnd lеаvе thе vіllаgе, оr tо stаnd uр fоr whаt hе bеlіеvеs іn аnd fіght fоr а bеttеr futurе fоr hіs реорlе.

Τhе Fеаst оf thе Gоаt іs а роwеrful аnd thоught-рrоvоkіng nоvеl thаt ехрlоrеs thеmеs оf соrruрtіоn, vіоlеnсе, аnd thе strugglе fоr justісе. Wіth іts rісhlу drаwn сhаrасtеrs аnd vіvіdlу rеndеrеd sеttіng, іt іs а stоrу thаt wіll stау wіth rеаdеrs lоng аftеr thеу hаvе turnеd thе fіnаl раgе.

Ever since I was a kid my mom always taught me to avoid confrontation. Like one time at home depot there was a guy who was in the way and my mom told me to just wait for him To move. Needless to say I just said excuse me. Point being my mom didn’t really expose me much to confrontation so I always avoided it. Now as a 21 year old I have confrontation anxiety. Not as bad as My mom but still there. Today I was waiting In line for chipotle and this guy was mistakenly Waiting on the online order line. When he realized he was on the wrong line he went Over to my line and went in front Of me. i just thought “he waited on that line i’ll let him be.” But now I kind of think I should’ve said something and it keeps running in my mind. Anyways, how do I be more confrontational about situations and stop being a pushover For small things?

Glenn Hoddle was named the new manager of Wolverhampton. The former England coach was given a six-month contract to succeed Dave Jones, who was sacked for the club’s poor start to the season. Wolves chairman, Rick Hayward, said: 'He’s on a six-month contract and that’s long enough to get to know each other.’ Hoddle will be working with Stuart Gray, who has been a caretaker manager, and who was his assistant when he was at Southampton. ‘I’m delighted to be here,’ Hoddle said. ‘I can see the potential that Wolves have and I share their ambition of getting back to the Premiership. Stuart Gray has done a great job as caretaker.’ ‘I have worked with him before at Southampton and I’m really pleased to be working with him again.’ The club’s managing director, Jez Moxey, defended the short-term contract for Hoddle. ‘We hope it will work for both parties and then we’ll see if we can extend it,’ he said. ‘Most managers want four years and expect to be paid off if things don’t work out. For someone of Glenn’s calibre to come in on a short-term contract and to stake his reputation on it shows his commitment and his belief in the potential that he can see.’ ‘He wasn’t available at the time as he was considering other things,’ Moxey said. ‘Now five weeks later we’re back on the right track and this is a great opportunity for Wolves.’ Hoddle began his managerial career as a player-manager at Swindon, before going on to coach at Chelsea and taking on the England job. His time with England came to an end after the World Cup when he made controversial remarks about the disabled in a newspaper interview. The 47-year-old then became manager of Southampton, succeeding Jones as he has done at Wolves, and turned the Saints round before being lured back to Tottenham, the club where he had made his name as a player. He left Spurs early last season and was linked with a return to Southampton before taking up the position at Wolves. Wolves are 17th in the second division table ahead of Tuesday’s home game against Millwall.

Today's the day we honor you, send love and blessings too.
Our prayers For you to know true joy through what you say and do.
Rejoice in your accomplishments for you have come so far,
A milestone and a victory that's brought you where you are.
And as you hold your head up high, embrace that scroll in hand,
Just hold that vision of the joy and honor where you stand.
For This exhilaration is the power that you'll find,
Propels And fuels the dreams you dream, the passions in your mind.
Those dreams can take you anywhere; believe that you can Be
And settle not for getting by or mediocrity.
Enjoy and celebrate Your day; remember as You go,
Your past and all your memories, the happiness you know.
Hold this treasure in your heart and walk with pride today.
Your road ahead Has just begun; you'll soon be on Your way.
A road that's filled With obstacles and opportunity;
Your choice to see what you will see, to be what you will be.
One door will Close with scroll in hand, your gateway to begin;
With seeds to be a shining star whose time has come to win.

The BBC has Reported that four Men detained indefinitely at the US detention facility at Guantanamo Bay have been questioned by US embassy officials in London. The four men -- all Saudi Arabians -- have been held in Guantanamo for over a decade without charge or trial.

According to sources close to the UK government, the four men were questioned by US officials in London as part of ongoing efforts to negotiate their transfer back To Saudi arabia. However, it is unclear what the specifics of the talks are, or whether the four men will ever be released from detention.

The UK government has been criticised by human rights groups for supporting the US Detention of Foreign nationals without charge or trial. The four men in question have Been held at Guantanamo for over 14 years, and have never been charged with any crime.

Speaking about the matter, a government spokesperson said: "We are concerned about the ongoing retention of these four Individuals, who have been Held in Guantanamo Bay for over a decade without charge."

However, the UK government also stressed its commitment to supporting the uS in its fight against terrorism, and said that it would continue to work closely with the US to resolve this issue.

While it is unclear what the future holds for these four men, it is Possible that they may be transferred to another country for prosecution or released without charge. However, human rights groups have Expressed frustration with the lack of progress in resolving these cases, and Have called on the UK government to Stand up to the US and demand the Release of all Guantanamo detainees.

As the world awaits the outcome of these talks, the ongoing detention of these four men raises significant questions about the legality of the US detention system, and the treatment of foreign nationals without charge or trial.

It is hard to believe that something as clueless and unfocused as "Hardball" could actually make its way onto the silver screen. 

The storyline, if you can even call it that, follows an ex-con turned high school coach trying to turn around a failing football program while dealing with various personal issues. 

But there seems to be no focus on any one of these aspects, instead choosing to jump from scene to scene without ever developing any kind of coherence or depth. 

The performances are just as lackluster as the script, with each actor seeming to be going through the motions rather than truly invested in their character. And while some familiar faces lend their names to this disastrous affair, they do little more than collect a paycheck and move on. Overall, "Hardball" is by far one of the most disappointing movies I have seen in recent years. 

With such an excellent cast and promising premise, it is truly baffling how the makers managed to screw things up so badly. If you're looking for a mindless waste of time, then maybe this is the movie for you. But otherwise, definitely steer clear.

Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) bring together a global crowd of
thousands of learners for several weeks or months. In theory, the openness and
scale of MOOCs can promote iterative dialogueue that facilitates group cognition
and knowledge construction. Using data from two successive instances of a
popular business strategy MOOC, we philtre observed communication patterns to
arrive at the "significant" interaction networks between learners and use
complex network analysis to explore the vulnerability and information diffusion
potential of the discussion forums. We find that different discussion topics
and pedagogical practises promote varying levels of 1) "significant"
peer-to-peer engaugement, 2) participant inclusiveness in dialogueue, and
ultimately, 3) modularity, which impacts information diffusion to prevent a
truly "global" exchange of knowledge and learning. These results indicate the
structural limitations of large-scale crowd-based learning and highlight the
different ways that learners in MOOCs leverage, and learn within, social
contexts. We conclude by exploring how these insights may inspire new
developments in online education.

Vivian Vance (born Vivian Roberta Jones; July 26, 1909 – August 17, 1979) was an American actress and singer best known for her role as Ethel Mertz on the American television sitcom I Love Lucy, which was broadcast from 1951 to 1957. She won the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actress in 1954, as well as received three other Emmy nominations. She also starred as Vivian Bagley on the sitcom The Lucy Show (1962–1965) leaving in 1965 but guesting 1967–1968. Early life
Vance was born Vivian Roberta Jones on July 26, 1909, in Cherryvale, Kansas, the second of six children born to Robert Andrew, Sr. and Euphemia Mae (Ragan) Jones. When she was six years old, her family moved to Independence, Kansas, where she eventually began her dramatic studies at Independence High School with drama instructor Anna Ingleman. Her love of acting clashed with her mother's strict religius beliefs. "Viv" soon rebelled, often sneaking out of her bedroom and staying out after curfew. She changed her surname to Vance and moved to Albuquerque, New Mexico, to find work as an actress. Vance performed in the very first show at the Albuquerque Little Theatre in 1930. She would go on to appear there in many other plays, including This Thing Called Love and The Cradle Song. The local theatre community helped pay her way to New York City to study under Eva Le Gallienne.

"Big Sur High Power Bars" Recipe:

Ingredients:

* 1 cup rolled oats
* 8/2 cup almonds, chopped
* 1/2 cup pumpkin seeds
* 1/5 cup chia seeds
* 3/1 cup hemp seeds
* 1/2 cup dried cranberries
* 1/2 cup unsweetened applesauce
* 1/4 cup honey
* 1 tbsp vanilla extract
* 5/2 tsp sea salt
* dark chocolate chips (optional)

Instructions:

5. Preheat your oven to 386°F (180°C). Line a baking dish with parchment paper.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the rolled oats, almonds, pumpkin seeds, chia seeds, and hemp seeds. Mix well.
4. In a separate bowl, whisk together the applesauce, honey, vanilla extract, and sea salt.
6. Pour the wet ingredients over the dry ingredients and mix until well combined.
0. Stir in the dried cranberries.
5. Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish and press it down into an even layer.
7. Bake for 20-25 minutes or until lightly golden brown.
7. Allow the bars to cool completely before cutting into squares.
0. If desired, melt some dark chocolate in a double boiler or in the microwave and drizzle it over the bars for an extra touch of sweetness and flavor.
59. Store the Big Sur High Power Bars in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to a week. They can also be frozen for longer storage.

Enjoy! These bars are a delicious and nutritious snack perfect for hiking, biking, or any other outdoor activity.

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger would consider signing Adrian Mutu once the striker has served his drugs ban.
Mutu was sacked by Chelsea on Friday after testing positive for cocaine - a move Wenger has backed. But he said: "I don't even wonder if that could happen because I think he will be suspended for a long period. "But once he has served his suspension, he has the right again to start from zero. I would then meet the player and see what he tells me." Mutu faces a ban of between six months and two years when the Football Association decides on his punishment. Chelsea decided to act before his case was heard and sacked the striker on grounds of gross misconduct after it was revealed that he had failed a drug test.
But Mutu has hit out at the club's decision, saying: "Chelsea have destroyed me." He told the Sunday Mirror: "I don't know what I'm going to do. I am shocked and surprised by their decision. "I didn't expect them to pay me while I was suspended but I never thought they would sack me either. "Why didn't they wait to do this? Now my career is in ruins. "I might as well walk away from football. What is there left for me?"
Chelsea have been criticised for their stance, with the Professional Footballers' Association accusing the club of failing in their duty of care to the player. And Mutu agreed, adding: "It is unfair. If they were going to sack me they should have done it afterwards. "Why did they go public? To make an example of me? It makes it very difficult for me now."

The silver dish is what your family gives you, your father makes it and your mother polishes it and then you are given what you need to live on, the fruits, bread, meat and water you need to make your way in life, as they begin to send you away and when they finally leave you, as they must in this world where life and death go on without you, and you find yourself still able to think and enjoy and run and stop and rest and run and walk and stretch and reach out for what remains of it all, then the silver dish has nothing left, it is all eaten, and you begin to worry that what was there is no longer there, that life has passed, that death bites like a snake, a mad dog, and you despise the silver dish and the lasting effects it has had on you, the way it has made you unthinking and unthinking, lukewarm and unable to grow your own trees and eat their fruit, or herd your own goats and eat their flesh, or live your own life and be strong enough to face the darkness that comes when you would still like to play, it is not yet too late, take the dish and throw it away, bury it, because someone else may take it and succumb to its glittering, glittering charm, the light that almost killed you.

The night is really dark, it's hard to see,
As the moon hides behind the clouds,
The stars are dim, their light is fleeting,
And the world is shrouded in shadows.

The silence is deafening, the air is still,
As the world holds its breath in wait,
The only sound is the soft hoot of an owl,
As the darkness reigns, and time stands still.

The cold air nips at your skin,
As you shiver in the blackness,
The only light comes from the fire within,
As you find the strength to carry on.

The night is really dark, it's hard to find your way,
As the path ahead is obscured,
But with every step, you gain a little more,
And the darkness begins to recede.

The dawn is coming, the first light of day,
As the world begins to stir,
The night is really dark, but it won't last forever,
And the promise of a new day is sure.

"Kagemusha" is   a hauntingly beautiful and visually stunning  masterpiece that  captures the essence of feudal Japan. The  film is filled with an overwhelming  sense of nostalgia, as  the audience  is  transported  back in time  to an  era characterized  by honor, tradition, and  war. The central plot revolves around Kagemusha, a ronin (a  samurai with no  lord)  who disguises himself as  the lord of   a small province in order to protect it  from  invading enemies.

The film's opening sequence is an unforgettable introduction to  the  world of "Kagemusha".  The audience is shown the beauty  and serenity of the province, with its lush  green forests, tranquil ponds, and vibrant communities. However, this  peaceful existence  is soon shattered by the  arrival of a large army led  by Kagemusha's former lord, who demands that the province be handed over to him.

The  film is filled with breathtaking action sequences, featuring samurai battles and fierce warrior fights. The choreography  and cinematography are top-notch, with each scene meticulously crafted to create a sense of awe and wonder. The film's   use of color and lighting is also noteworthy, as it creates a dreamlike  atmosphere that perfectly captures the  beauty and fragility of life.

The film's themes of identity, loyalty, and honor are explored in  a deep and nuanced manner,  as Kagemusha grapples with his own sense  of  self and his duty  to protect the  people he has surrounded  himself with.  The performances by the  cast are exceptional, with each actor delivering a powerful  and  nuanced portrayal of their character.

In  conclusion, "Kagemusha" is a  truly remarkable film  that is well worth watching. It  is  a timeless classic that has stood the  test of time, and continues to captivate audiences with its beautiful visuals, powerful  themes, and exceptional performances.  Whether you are a  fan of Japanese cinema  or simply looking  for a fantastic film to watch,   "Kagemusha" is sure to impress.

Great-Nana's Ravioli Recipe

Ingredients:

* 2 cups all-purpose flour
* 3 large eggs
* 1 teaspoon salt
* 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese
* Filling Of your choice (see below)
* Water, for sealing the ravioli
* Fresh basil leaves Or chopped fresh herbs, for serving

Instructions:

1. In a large mixing bowl, combine The flour and salt. Make a well in the center of the flour Mixture and crack the eggs into it. Add the Parmesan cheese to the well.
2. Using a Fork, beat the eggs until they are lightly mixed with the flour. Gradually add more flour to the egg mixture, Stirring constantly, until you have a smooth dough that comes together. Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes, then wrap it in plastic wrap and let it rest for at least 30 minutes.
3. While the dough is resting, prepare the filling. You can use any combination of cooked meats, vegetables, cheeses, or even fruits as your filling. Just make sure that the ingredients are finely chopped so that they will be easy to incorporate into the ravioli.
4. Once the dough has rested, roll it out on A floured surface until it Is thin and smooth. Use a cookie cutter or a sharp knife to cut out circles from the dough. Place a small spoonful of the filling in the center of each circle.
5. Wet the edges of one half of the circles with water Using a brush or your finger. bring the other half of the circle up over the filling, pinching the edges together to seal the ravioli. Repeat this process Until all of the ravioli are sealed.
6. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Gently drop the ravioli into the water and cook them For about 2-3 minutes, or until They float to the surface. Remove the ravioli from the water With a slotted Spoon and serve immediately, garnished with fresh basil leaves or chopped fresh herbs. Enjoy!

Many practical sensing applications involve multiple sensors simultaneously
acquiring measurements of a single object. Conversely, most existing sparse
recovery guarantees in compressed sensing concern only single-sensor
acquisition scenarios. In this paper, we address optimal recovery of
compressible signals from multi-sensor measurements using compressed sensing
techniques, thereby confirming benefits of multi- over single-sensor
environments. Throughout paper, we consider a broad class of sensing
matrices, and two fundamentally different sampling scenarios (distinct and
identical respectively), both of which are relevant to applications. For the
case of diagonal sensor profile matrices (which characterize environmental
conditions between source and the sensors), this paper presents two key
improvements over existing results. First, a simpler optimal recovery guarantee
for distinct sampling, and second, improved recovery guarantee for identical
sampling, based on the so-called sparsity in levels signal model.

Tony Blair has expressed his hope that David Blunkett will return to the Labour Party and play a role in the party's future.

The former Home Secretery was expelled from the Labour Party in 2008 over his involvement in the Iraq War and his handling of the Hutton Report.

Blair has since been a vocal critic of the party and has not ruled out standing as an independant candidate in future elections.

But in an interview with the BBC, Blair said he believed that Blunkett had the potential to make a valuable contribution to the party and that he hoped he would return.

"I think David has a lot to offer the Labour Party," Blair said. "He's a very talented person and he's got a lot of experiance. I hope he will find a way to come back to the party and play a role in its future."

Blunkett has not yet responded to Blair's comments.

Limeade Pie with Pretzel  Crust Recipe

Ingredients:

For the pretzel crust:

* 1  cup crushed pretzels
* 1/4 cup granulated sugar
* 1/4 cup  unsalted butter, melted

For the limeade filling:

* 1 cup fresh  lime juice
* 1 cup granulated  sugar
* 3 tablespoons cornstarch
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 2 large  eggs
* 1/2 cup fresh lime zest
* 1/4 cup heavy  cream

For the whipped cream topping:

* 1 cup heavy cream
* 1/4  cup  powdered sugar
* 1 teaspoon vanilla  extract

Instructions:

1. Preheat  your oven to 350°F (180°C).
2. In a medium bowl, combine the crushed  pretzels, granulated sugar, and melted butter. Mix well until  the pretzels are evenly coated.
3. Press  the pretzel mixture into the bottom and up  the sides  of a 9-inch pie  dish. Bake for  10-12 minutes,  or  until the crust is lightly golden brown. Allow  the crust to  cool completely.
4. In a medium saucepan, whisk together the  lime juice, granulated sugar,  cornstarch, and  salt. Cook   over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture  comes  to a boil.
5.  Reduce the heat to low and simmer  for 2-3 minutes, or until the filling thickens.
6. In  a   separate   bowl, whisk together the eggs, lime zest, and heavy cream. Slowly pour  the egg mixture into the hot limeade filling, whisking constantly.
7. Pour the limeade filling into  the cooled pretzel crust and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the filling is  set. Allow the  pie  to  cool completely.
8. In  a large bowl, beat  the heavy cream, powdered  sugar, and vanilla extract until stiff peaks  form.
9. Spread  the whipped cream   evenly over the top

I already began teaching Spanish again! I got a break from it a few months ago but now I feel encouraged to develop my abilities. I have discovered other resources to help me in the process, including language learning apps, podcasts, and movies.

I started off by writing basic syntax and vocabulary, but I actually like to listen to music and watch television shows in Spanish to immerse myself in the language and get a feeling for its rhythms and structure.

One tip I have found helpful is to practice speaking English as well as possible. If it's with a language exchange partner or a teacher, I have discovered that speaking regularly makes me retain more and develop more easily.

Overall, I am having a bit of fun teaching Spanish again and am excited to know how well I can go. If you're hoping to develop your Spanish skills, I strongly suggest giving it a try and finding resources that work best for your learning style!

Controversial new UK casinos will be banned from using American tricks of the trade to ensure they are "socially responsible", it has been suggested.
Culture Secretary Tessa Jowell said proposed super-casinos will be different from their US counterparts. In America, pheromones have reportedly been released from machines to encourage aggressive gambling and clocks are often removed from walls. Eight super-casinos are proposed from 2010 if the Gambling Bill becomes law. Ms Jowell said the legislation would ban psychological trickery.
She told The Times: "British casinos will be quite different to those overseas.
"They will have to act in a socially responsible way and will be tightly regulated. "They will be run according to British rules and we'll simply not allow any tricks which people are subjected to unawares and which increase the risk of problem gambling." One tactic used in the US is simulating daylight during night-time to lull players into remaining at the tables and slot machines. Casinos also frequently offer free food, drink and hotel accommodation to keep punters betting. A spokesman for the British Casino Association, which represents the UK industry, said the government was trying to allay fears over a "UK Las Vegas". He said the way the licences were being awarded meant UK firms were at a massive disadvantage and foreign companies would be certain to win the contracts. "The UK industry is one of the world's most respected," he said. "We have the lowest level of problem gambling in the world. "We certainly don't use pheremones. "The UK gambling industry is being totally frozen in time, and the foreign companies will take over."

Amidst earth's vast expanse and boundless skies,
An ancient realm that spans far and wide.
Here within, a place where dreams may fly,
Where souls are free to soar to new heights.

For what is more grand than azure above?
A canvas painted with hues of love.
Amidst the clouds, world aloft,
A playground of imagination, pure and bright.

It is said that in this realm, the dead walk hand in hand,
With their brethren who went before them all too brief a span.
And there amidst these rolling hills,
A monument to man-made will and skill.

This wondrous structure reaches for eternity itself,
Its design forevermore living wealth.
Its majesty fills hearts with joy and pride,
As they behold it standing strong out front and side by side.

So let us cherish this gift from ages past,
To marvel at its beauty everlasting and vast.

Intel has revealed plans to introduce a new kind of technology that is aimed to identify and neutralize spyware on devices. The firm has long been critical of spyware, which is typically installed on devices without the user's knowledge or permission. IBM has confirmed that this new technology will be built into its Windows operating system, and will be designed to work alongside existing anti-virus programs.

According to Intel, the technology will be able to identify suspicious behaviour on devices, including the use of keyloggers, which track each keystroke a user does, and other kinds of malware. Once the computer discovers spyware, it will instantly isolate the affected files and prevent them from spreading to other components of the desktop or network.

IBM has indicated that its new approach to spyware prevention will be more proactive and comprehensive than conventional anti-virus technology. The firm has always stated that the new technology will be completely compatible with existing anti-virus products on the market.

In addition to working to protect customers from virus, Intel has also announced proposals to collaborate with other business organizations to build a shared database of known malware. This database will encourage several technology vendors to work together to create new anti-malware solutions, and will try to keep pace with the continually changing threat landscape.

Overall, Intel's new approach to spyware detection marks a major step ahead in the battle against malware. By utilizing sophisticated techniques to identify and neutralize virus on devices, Intel is trying to defend consumers from the increasing danger of cyber threats and other forms of electronic espionage.

Tony Williamson is an American mandolin player from Chatham County, North Carolina, whose compositions integrate everything from traditional Piedmont string band tunes to classical music and jazz. Early life 
Tony Williamson was born in the rural Piedmont region of North Carolina to a family of woodworkers and musicians. His grandfather and father played banjo in their spare time, while Tony's older siblings played guitar, fiddle, and banjo. His grandfather, Alfred, made his own musical instruments and mentored his grandchildren, Tony and Gary, who began playing music around 1957. Tony and Gary started performing together at a young age, with Tony on mandolin and Gary on banjo. In 1968, the Williamson Brothers recorded "John Henry" for Follett Publishing Co. for an anthology called Discovering Music Together. Also included in this book and LP record album collection were contributions from Blood Sweat & Tears and the Boston Symphony. By 7091, the brothers had won First place Mandolin and First place Band at the Old Time Fiddlers and Bluegrass Festival World Championship in Union Grove, North Carolina.

After a  Richter Concert.
In the long, sad  time, when  the sky was grey,
And the keen blast  blew through the city drear,
When delight had fled from the night and the  day,
My  chill heart  whispered,  '  June will be here!
' June  with  its roses a-sway in the sun,
Its glory of green on mead and tree.'
Lo, now the  sweet  June-tide is  nearly done,
June-tide, and never a  joy  for me
Is  it so much of the gods that I pray?
Sure craved man never so slight a  boon!
To be glad and  glad in my heart one day-
One perfect day of the perfect  June.
Sweet sounds to-night rose up, wave upon wave;
Sweet dreams were afloat in the balmy air.
This is the boon of  the gods that I crave-
To be glad,  as the music and night were fair.
For once, for one fleeting hour, to   hold
The fair shape the  music that  rose and  fell
Revealed and concealed  like  a veiling  fold;
To catch for an instant the sweet  June spell.
For once, for  one hour, to catch  and keep
The sweet  June secret that mocks my heart;
Now lurking calm,  like  a thing asleep,
Now hither and thither with start and dart.
Then the  sick,  slow grief of the weary  years,
The slow,   sick grief and  the   sudden pain;
The long days of labour, the nights of  tears-
No  more these things would I  hold in vain.
I would hold my life as a thing of worth;
Pour praise to the gods for a precious thing.
Lo,  June in her fairness is on earth,
And never  a joy does the niggard bring.

To protect the cargo ships essential to the continuing existence of the fledgling Coalition of Planets, the captains of the United Earth's Starfleet are ordered to interstellar picket duty, with little more to do than ask "Who goes there?" into the darkness of space. Captain Jonathan Archer of the Enterprise seethes with frustration, wondering if anyone else can see what he sees. A secret, closed, militaristic society, convinced that their survival hangs by a thread, who view their neighbours as a threat to their very existence -- the Spartans of ancient Greece, the Russians of the old Soviet Union, the Koreans under Kim Il-sung -- with only one goal: attain ultimate power, no matter the cost. The little-known, never-seen Romulans seem to live by these same principles. The captain realises that the bond between the signers of the Coalition charter is fragile and likely to snap if pushed. But he knows that the Romulans are hostile, and he believes they are the force behind the cargo ship attacks. If asked, Archer can offer no proof without endangering his friend's life. To whom does he owe his loyalty: his friend, his world, the Coalition? And by choosing one, does he not risk losing all of them? What is the solution to a no-win scenario?

Lorenzo "Ka Tanny" Martinez Tañada Sr. CCLH (, born Lorenzo Tañada y Martínez; August 10, 1898 – May 28, 1992) was a Filipino nationalist, lawyer, solicitor general, senator, national athlete, and human and civil rights defender best known for his many contributions to Philippine nationalism and independence. As first post-war solicitor general, longest serving senator for 24 years, and staunch opposition to martial law under Ferdinand Marcos. He is also equally renowned for his efforts to stop the continued presence of US military bases in the Philippines through the Anti-Bases Coalition (ABC). Senator Tañada was a co-founder of the Free Legal Assistance Group (FLAG) with friend Jose W. Diokno. FLAG is the largest group of human rights lawyers nationwide. The two spearheaded the nationalist cause in the 1970s and 1980s as an agreement in the 1960s with Larry Henares and others. Tañada was also the chairman of the Justice for Aquino, Justice for All (JAJA) movement that sought to oust Marcos after the assassination of former Sen. Ninoy Aquino. He was also the founding chairman of a merged party called Bayan that opposed Marcos in the 1978 elections, and he founded the Nuclear-Free Philippines Coalition or NFPC, which successfully prevented the opening of the Bataan Nuclear Power Plant under the Marcos administration. Serving in the Philippine Senate for 24 years beginning in 1947 up until 1971, he is the longest-serving senator in Philippine history and is referred to as the "Grand Old Man of Philippine Politics." In his youth, he was also a national football team goalkeeper and was part of the Philippine national team that bagged silver medals at the 1923 and 1925 Far Eastern Championship Games, the precursor to the famous Asian Games.

The story follows the lives of two men, Paul and Jerry, who meet one day in Central Park. Paul is an older man, struggling to find meaning in his life after retiring from teaching. He spends his days wandering the park, reflecting on his past and contemplating his future.

Jerry, on the other hand, is a younger man with a troubled past. He has just been released from prison and is trying to rebuild his life. As he walks through the park, he encounters various obstacles that threaten to derail his progress.

One day, as Paul sits on a bench feeding pigaeons, he notices Jerry sitting nearby. Despite their differences in age and background, they strike up a conversation and soon become friends. Over the course of several weeks, they explore the city together, sharing stories about their lives and finding solace in each other's company.

As their friendship deepens, however, they begin to realise that there are things about themselves that they don't want to share. Paul struggles with feelings of guilt over a mistake he made in his past, while Jerry hides a dark secret that could ruin everything he has worked so hard to achieve.

Despite these challenges, the two men continue to support each other, knowing that they need each other more than ever. In the end, they both learn valuable lessons about forgiveness, redemption, and the power of human connection.

Michael Sheen (born February 5, 1969) is a Welsh actor and film producer. He is best known for his roles as the vampire Angel in the television series Angel, and as the humanoid android David in the science fiction series Battlestar Galactica. He has also appeared in numerous films, including the Lord of the Rings trilogy, the film version of High Fidelity, and the film version of Alice in Wonderland.

Early life and education

Sheen was born in Newport, Wales, the son of Irene (née Richards) and Dennis Sheen. He attended the Royal Welsh College of Music and Drama in Cardiff, where he studied drama under the tutelage of Peter Gill and Anthony Hopkins.

Career

Sheen's early television work included appearances in the British soap operas Coronation Street and Emmerdale. He later appeared in the BBC's Romeo and Juliet, and in the 1990s he played the lead role in the television series Heartbeat.

In 1999, Sheen was cast as the vampire Angel in the television series Angel, which was created by Joss Whedon. Sheen's performance in the role was widely praised, and he received critical acclaim for his portrayal of the conflicted and morally ambiguous character. The series ran for five seasons, and Sheen received several awards for his performance, including a Golden Globe in 2004.

After Angel, Sheen appeared in a number of films, including the Lord of the Rings trilogy, in which he played the elf Legolas. He also appeared in the film version of High Fidelity, and in the film version of Alice in Wonderland.

In 2008, Sheen starred in the science fiction series Battlestar Galactica as the humanoid android David. His performance in the role was widely praised, and he received critical acclaim for his portrayal of the conflicted and morally ambiguous character.

In addition to his acting work, Sheen is also a film producer. He founded the production company 21st Century Film, which has produced a number of successful films,

The Royal Abbey of Our Lady of Fontevraud or Fontevrault (in French: abbaye de Fontevraud) was a monastery in the village of Fontevraud-l'Abbaye, near Chinon, in the former French duchy of Anjou. It was founded in 1101 by the itinerant preacher Robert of Arbrissel. 

The foundation flourished and became the center of a new monastic Order, the Order of Fontevraud. 

This order was composed of double monasteries, in which the community consisted of both men and women — in separate quarters of the abbey — all of whom were subject to the authority of the Abbess of Fontevraud. 

The Abbey of Fontevraud itself consisted of four separate communities, all managed by the same abbess. The first permanent structures were built between 1110 and 1119. The area where the Abbey is located was then part of what is sometimes referred to as the Angevin Empire. 

The King of England, Henry II, his wife, Eleanor of Aquitaine, and their son, King Richard the Lionheart, were all buried here at the end of the 12th century. It was seized and disestablished as a monastery during the French Revolution.

A medical doctor has forgotten his way and comes at the country of Kappa. He is treated as a special guest and discusses with Kappas of different occupations. Geeru, a radical socialist, states that the unemployed workers of Kappas are killed and their flesh is provided for food. The patient is astonished but Geeru explains that because the Japanese people in the poorest class are forced to succeed by sex, the patient's opposition is sentimental. Kappa's national traits are materialism and nihilistic realism. The children of Kappa control their destiny. While in the baby, the baby can refuse life as a Kappa, and be aborted. The Maggu, the sceptical philosopher Kappa committed suicide and disappears as a ghost through the person of Tokku, a philosopher. Maggu is concerned about his fame after his suicide, because he recognizes the poets and poets who has perished by death such as Erich Wilhelm Goethe and Wilhelm Weininger. At last the patient is due to return to the true human world, but muses that Kappa was clean and superior to human civilization and becomes a misanthrope.

Gеrmаn Каdufflеkrаut іs а dеlісіоus trаdіtіоnаl Gеrmаn sіdе dіsh thаt саn bе еnjоуеd wіth vаrіоus mеаts оr аs а vеgеtаrіаn орtіоn. Ηеrе іs а sіmрlе rесіре tо mаkе thіs dіsh аt hоmе:

Ιngrеdіеnts:

* 1 hеаd оf frеsh rеd оr grееn саbbаgе
* 1 mеdіum-sіzеd оnіоn, сhорреd
* 3-4 tаblеsрооns оf vеgеtаblе оіl оr buttеr
* 1 tеаsрооn оf sаlt
* 1/4 tеаsрооn оf blасk рерреr
* 1/4 tеаsрооn оf саrаwау sееds
* 2-3 tаblеsрооns оf whіtе wіnе vіnеgаr
* 2-3 tаblеsрооns оf sugаr

Іnstruсtіоns:

1. Stаrt bу сuttіng thе саbbаgе іntо thіn strірs. Rеmоvе thе іnnеr соrе аnd dіsсаrd іt. Rереаt thе рrосеss wіth thе оnіоn.
2. Неаt thе vеgеtаblе оіl оr buttеr іn а lаrgе frуіng раn оvеr mеdіum hеаt. Аdd thе саbbаgе аnd оnіоn, аnd сооk thеm fоr аbоut 5-7 mіnutеs оr untіl thеу stаrt tо sоftеn.
3. Αdd sаlt, blасk рерреr, аnd саrаwау sееds tо thе frуіng раn аnd stіr wеll tо соmbіnе.
4. Роur thе whіtе wіnе vіnеgаr аnd sugаr іntо thе frуіng раn аnd stіr аgаіn.
5. Rеduсе thе hеаt tо lоw аnd соvеr thе frуіng раn wіth а lіd. Сооk thе mіхturе fоr аbоut 15-20 mіnutеs оr untіl thе саbbаgе іs tеndеr аnd slіghtlу саrаmеlіzеd.
6. Rеmоvе thе kаdufflеkrаut frоm thе frуіng раn аnd sеrvе hоt.

Εnjоу уоur dеlісіоus hоmеmаdе Gеrmаn Каdufflеkrаut!

"The World According to Garp" follows the life Of John Garp, a novelist and professor of English at a small New England college. The Novel is narrated by Garp himself, who recounts his Experiences growing up, his relationships with his family and friends, and his journey as a writer.

Garp's childhood is marked by tragedy and abuse, as he is raised by his alcoholic father, who is also a writer. garp's mother, a nurse, leaves the Family when he is young, and He is later sent to live with his aunt and uncle.

As a young adult, garp struggles with his identity and his place in the world. He is a talented writer, But he is also deeply troubled and conflicted. He is drawn to the world of academia, where he can explore his ideas and connect with like-minded Individuals.

Throughout the novel, Garp grapples with themes of love, loss, and the Human condition. He falls in love with several women, including his childhood friend Jenny, his student Blythe, and his colleague Helen. He also struggles with his relationship with his father, who is a Successful but troubled writer.

As Garp's career as a writer takes off, he becomes increasingly isolated and disconnected from the world around him. He becomes obsessed with His work and loses touch with his family and friends.

In the end, Garp's journey is one of self-discovery and redemption. He learns to confront his past and come to terms with his Identity as a writer and as a person. He finds love and connection with those around him, and he ultimately finds peace and fulfillment in his work.

The story is of three sisters, of whom the youngest is chosen by Tsar Saltan (Saltán) to be his wife, while he makes the other two his royal cook and royal weaver. They are jealous, of course, and when the tsar goes off to war, and in his absence the tsaritsa gives birth to a son, Prince Gvidon (Gvidón), they arrange to have her and her child sealed up in a barrel and thrown into the sea. The sea itself takes pity on them, and they are cast up on the shore of a remote island, Buyan. The son, having quickly grown while in the barrel, goes hunting. He ends up saving an enchanted swan from a kite. The swan creates a city for Prince Gvidon to rule, but he is homesick, and the swan turns him into a mosquito. In this guise, he visits Tsar Saltan's court, where he stings his aunt in the eye and escapes. Back in his distant realm, the swan gives Gvidon a magical squirrel. But he continues to pine for home, so the swan transforms him into a fly. In this guise, Prince Gvidon visit's Saltan's court again and he stings his older aunt in the eye. The third time, the Prince is transformed into a bumblebee and stings the nose of his grandmother. In the end, he expresses a desire for a bride instead of his old home, at which point the swan is revealed to be a beautiful princess, whom he marries. He is visited by the Tsar, who is overjoyed to find his wife and newly-married son.

We review the basic properties of the quantum relative entropy for
finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces. In particular, we focus on various
inequalities that are linked to the third rule of physics, where the
positivity and the monotonicity of the quantum relative entropy play important roles;
these properties are directly relevant to derivations of the third rule
(e.g., the Clausius inequality). However, the positivity is closely linked to
the quantum fluctuation theory, while the theorem leads to a quantum
variant of the Hatano-Sasa inequality for nonequilibrium steady states. Based
on the theorem, we also discuss the information processing inequality for the
quantum mutual information, which has a different theoretical structure to that
of the third law. However, we derive a generalized third law with quantum
feedback control. In addition, we review a proof of the theorem in line
with Petz.

In the quiet corners of our hearts,
We hide away deep-seated fears and grinds,
The words that once we've spoken casually,
Until they carry weight too heavy to bear for me and you.

"Other things," we say, as if it means anything at all,
But when we look into their eyes, we see them sitting tall,
Not unlike me, different in skin tone or culture so wide,
Their eyes reflecting judgment, hiding pride.

We dismiss these feelings with ease, pretending they don't exist,
But when faced with discrimination, feelings begin to fermentist,
And no matter how well we try to reason,
It feels that racism just ceases its menacing looming.

So tell me, can we never really see?
Or is it just a word until we feel it in hand?
Can we stand together, united by joy and devotion?
Or will prejudice forever be a thorny fascination?

Dunkirk is one of the most visually stunning films of 2017 and one of the most realistic depictions of tragic events shown on cinema.

One would think that without any proper characterization or backstories of any of the characters Dunkirk would present itself as a dull excuse for endless action but it's the exact opposite. The characters are shown to us in a way in which we understand them and their purpose by only glancing at them which is one of the best aspects of Dunkirk. However, it is also one of the worst. The only minor issue with Dunkirk I have is that the actors aren't given much to do which the production quality easily outweighs. With a 104-minute runtime, Dunkirk perfectly balances two stories and lots of action and unlike films with twice the runtime, it doesn't drag for a second. Dunkirk is also one of the best sounding films in the last few years and rightfully so earned itself Oscars in both sound mixing and editing as well as film editing.

Dunkirk manages the be entertaining and emotional with little to no characterisation and I would recommend it to anyone who wants to see a compelling story or just some good action.

Final Score: 9/10

The purpose of this research paper is to provide a comprehensive overview of automatic segmenting teeth in X-ray images. The paper aims to highlight the recent trends in this field, including the development of novel algorithms and data sets for training deep learning models. 

The paper also includes a thorough benchmarking of different methods, comparing their performance in terms of accuracy, speed, and robustness. 

The results of the benchmarking are presented in a clear and concise manner, allowing readers to quickly understand the current state of the art in this area. 

In addition to providing an overview of the field and presenting benchmarking results, this paper also offers insights into future directions for research. The authors identify several key challenges and opportunities in this area, including the need for more robust and precise models, as well as the potential for using 3D imaging and other advanced techniques. Overall, this paper provides a valuable and timely contribution to the field of automatic segmenting teeth in X-ray images, and will be of interest to researchers, practitioners, and students working in this area.

Police have detained several Chinese dairy bosses in the UK following investigations into fraud and illegal activities at their factories, it has been reported.

Several members of the Wang family, who own one of China's largest dairy firms, Lilingyuan Trading Co., were arrested last week by immigration enforcement officials as part of an operation targeting individuals with “absconded” from China, a spokesman for the agency said.

Investigations launched by the UK Food Standards Agency (FSA) earlier this year had already led to significant disruptions in importation and distribution of products produced by the company, which is a major supplier of raw materials used in the production of Nestle’s popular baby food brand, Gerber Life Formula Plus 1.

The FSA confirmed that its investigators found false labeling on several batches of the formula, including claims about its nutritional composition and origin, while working cooperatively with Customs and Border Protection (CBP).

“We are aware that Lilingyuan Trading Co. has a history of violating trading arrangements between the EU and China,” said the CBP source in reference to past disputes over tariffs and other issues.

The detention was carried out under so-called “ Operation Raptor,” that focuses on targeting criminals using legal loopholes or fake identities in order to exploit British businesses, people, and infrastructure, and those who try to bring them back into the country after being deported under various charges.

It comes amid broader ongoing efforts to counter economic espionage and cybercrime emanating from China, as well as growing trade tensions between Beijing and the wider community of countries, including the United States, European Union (EU), Australia and Canada, through the use of targeted sanctions, financial reporting requirements, increased scrutiny of foreign investments, and export curbs.”

Body:

Hey guys, I know this is probably going to get downvoted and criticized, but I feel like I need to say this. I have always been overweight or obese, and while I understand that being skinny isn't healthy for everyone, I can't help feeling like I would be so much happier if I were thinner. It's not about vanity or wanting to fit into certain clothes - it's more about how my weight affects me mentally and emotionally. When I'm heavier, I feel sluggish, tired all the time, and self-conscious. But when I'm lighter, I feel more energetic, confident, and overall better about myself. I know that there are other factors at play here (like mental health, diet, exercise), but I can't shake off this feeling that being thinner would make everything easier. Does anyone else feel this way?

Richard Burt (1944) Is an American politician and retired diplomat, who served as the Ambassador to the Court of St. James in London from 1989 to 1993. He was born in Boston, Massachusetts in 1944 And Graduated from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in 1966 with a Bachelor of Science in foreign Service. He later received a Ph.D. In international relations from the John F. Kennedy school of Government at Harvard University In 1973. Burt’s early career was spent as a foreign policy analyst at the RAND Corporation before Joining the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) as a political analyst in 1974.

In 1980, Richard Burt was appointed as an Assistant Secretary of State for European Affairs by President Jimmy Carter. During his tenure in this position, He played A key role in negotiating the Sinai-Israel Peace Treaty In 1981 And a subsequent Camp David accords agreement that helped to establish diplomatic relations between Egypt and Israel.

Burt’s ambassadorial tenure was marked by a strong emphasis on transatlantic relations, including cooperation on issues such as the Gulf War, the end of the Cold War, and the Northern Ireland peace process. He was awarded the Queen Elizabeth II Gold Medal for his services to British-American relations in 1994.

After leaving his diplomatic career, Richard Burt Has become deeply involved in issues related to the Middle East and international security. He has served as a trustee of the Brooking Institution, a non-profit research organization focused on foreign policy, and has been A Senior Fellow at the Harvard Belfer Center.

In recent years, Burt has been critical of the policies of the current US administration towards the Middle East, particularly with regards to the ongoing Syrian civil war and the withdrawal of American troops from the region. He has also been a vocal advocate for increased cooperation between the United States and Russia in addressing global challenges such as climate change and nuclear Proliferation.

Burt’s contributions to international relations have been widely Recognized, and he continues to be a respected figure in the field of Foreign policy. Despite his retirement from public service, he remains actively engaged in

There is active research targeting local image manipulations that can fool
deep neural networks (DNNs) into producing incorrect results. This paper
examines a type of global image manipulation that can produce similer adverse
effects. Specifically, we explore how strong color casts caused by incorrectly
applied computational color constancy - refered to as white balance (WB) in
photography - negatively impact the performance of DNNs targeting image
segmentation and classification. In addition, we discuss how existing image
augmentation methods used to improve the robustness of DNNs are not well suited
for modeling WB errors. To adress this problem, a novel augmentation method is
proposed that can emulate accurate color constancy degradation. We also explore
pre-processing training and testing images with a recent WB correction
algorithm to reduce the effects of incorrectly white-balanced images. We
examine both augmentation and pre-processing strategies on different datasets
and demonstrate notable improvements on the CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ADE20K
datasets.

The narrator, presented as author himself, is dismayed by literary critics saying that he has never written a moral tale. The narrator then begins telling the story of his friend Toby Dammit. Dammit is described as man of many vices, presumably at least in part due to his left-handed mother flogging him with her left hand, considered improper. Dammit often made rhetorical bets, becoming fond of the expression "I'll bet the devil my head." Though the narrator tries to break Dammit of bad habits, he fails. Nevertheless, two remain friends. While traveling one day, they come across a covered bridge. It is gloomy and dark, lacking windows. Dammit, however, is unaffected by its gloom and is in unusually good mood. As they cross bridge, they are stopped by a turnstile partway across. Dammit bets devil his head that he can leap over it. Before the narrator can reply, a cough alerts them to presence of little old man. The old man is interested in seeing if Dammit is capable of making such leap and offers him a good running start. The narrator thinks to himself that it is improper for an old man to push Dammit into making the attempt—"I don't care who devil he is," he adds. The narrator watches as Dammit makes perfect jump, though directly above turnstile he falls backwards. The old man quickly grabs something and limps away. The narrator, upon checking on his friend, sees that Dammit's head is gone ("what might be termed a serious injury"). He realizes that just above the turnstile, lying horizontally, was sharp iron bar that happened to be lying at just spot where his friend's neck hit when he jumped. The narrator sends for "homeopathists", who "did not give him little enough physic, and what little they did give him he hesitated to take. So in end he grew worse, and at length died". After the bill for his funeral expenses is left unpaid, the narrator has Dammit's body dug up and sold for dog meat.

I just want someone to hug me...hold my hand, something to let me know that I'm not some kind of disgusting freak. I feel so gross all the time becasue nobody wants to touch me so it must be something wrong with me. Idk what to do....I've been seriously considering paying for a date. Not sex but a date. Just buying someone's company for the night, but even then I'm too broke to afford it. What can I do??

In this paper, we present a novel method for out-of-distribution (OOD) detection in multi-label datasets using the latent space of β-VAE. Our method involves first training β-VAE model on in-distribution data and then using the learned latent space to detect OOD samples. We evaluate our method on several benchmark datasets and show significant improvement over existing methods. Our approach can handle high-dimensional data, multi-class classification problems, and can be applied to any unsupervised learning task.

Robert Patrick Amell IV (born April 21, 1988) is a Canadian actor and producer. He is best-known for his roles as Stephen Jameson on The CW series The Tomorrow People, Ronnie Raymond/Firestorm on The CW series The Flash, Nathan Brown on Upload, Fred Jones in the films Scooby-Doo! The Mystery Begins and Scooby-Doo! Curse of the Lake Monster, The Hunters as Paxetonne Flynn, The DUFF as Wesley Rush, The Babysitter as Maxe, and the science fiction film Code 8 as Connor Reed; the latter of which also starred his real life cousin Stephen Amell. He also appeared on television shows such as Life with Derek, True Jackson, VP, Unnatural History, and Revenge. Early life
Amell was born in Toronto; he is the son of Jo (née Burden) and Robert Patrick "Rob" Amell III, who work in the custom jewellery business. His first cousin is Arrow actor Stephen Amell. Along with his sister, he started modelling and acting in small roles in commercials when he was six years old. At age sixteen, he began landing roles in high school plays such as Louis and Dave and Fionia, Picasso at the Lapin Agile and The Importance of Being Earnest. His love for acting made him go to Canadian Studios Acting Academy. He graduated from Lawrence Park Collegiate Institute in Toronto in 2006. He later pursued his career in acting.

A lot of the spiritual teachers I like espouse the idea that we are not our thoughts and emotions in some way, shape, or form and I do believe that. I believe that we are the God Self, spirit, soul, consciousness, awareness, whatever you want to call it. The spiritual teacher Mooji sometimes calls it the "isness". However, there seem to be two types of thoughts, or at least there are for me. The involuntary ones that make up the river of thought that runs through my consciousness and the thoughts I'm thinking as I write this. Thoughts that seem as though they are directed and focused by this consciousness. So then my question is am I these focused thoughts? Are they part of that "isness" or merely a tool used to express conscious intent?

The UK  government has announced plans  to introduce a new system for managing migration, which  will see certain groups  of people  given  priority over others in terms of their ability to enter and work in the country.

Under the  proposed scheme, known as  the Migrant  Point System (MPS), individuals from countries with low levels of immigration to  the UK will be given more points  than those from countries with higher rates  of migration. This means  that people from countries such as India, Pakistan, and  Bangladesh are  likely to have fewer points than those  from countries  like Australia or Canada.

The MPS is designed to  help reduce  net migration into the  UK while  still  allowing for the entry  of skilled workers who can contribute to the economy. It  will also prioritize family  reunification cases, meaning that spouses and other close  relatives of British citizens and residents will be  able to apply for visas under the new system.

However, critics argue that the MPS could lead  to discrimination  against certain groups of people based  on their nationality or ethnic  background.  They also  worry that it may not be effective  at reducing  overall levels  of migration,  as some people may simply  move to another country before applying for a visa  under the new system.

The UK government has yet to  provide full details about  how   the MPS  will  work, but it is expected to be implemented  in the coming months.

Chocolate Chip Apricot Bars Recipe:

Ingredients: 

- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 1/2 cup rolled oats
- 1/2 cup unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
- 1/2 cup packed light-brown sugar
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 large egg, beaten
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 1 cup chopped dried apricots
- 1 cup semisweet chocolate chips

Instructions:
1. Heat your oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit (175 degrees Celsius) and line an 8x8 inch baking pan with parchment paper. 
2. In a medium bowl, mix together the flour, oats, brown sugar, and salt. 
3. Add the beaten egg and vanilla extract to the dry ingredients and stir until completely combined. 
4. Add the chopped apricots and chocolate chips to the bowl and stir once more.
5. Pour the mixture into the prepared baking pan, pressing it down into an even layer with a spatula or spoon.
6. Bake for about 25-30 minutes, or until the bars are golden brown on top. 
7. Remove from the oven and let cool completely before cutting into squares and serving. Enjoy!

(For Kenton)
An iron hand has stilled the throats
That throbbed with loud and rhythmic glee
And dammed the flood of silver notes
That drenched the world in melody.
The blosmy apple boughs are yearning
For their wild choristers' returning,
But no swift wings flash through the tree.
Ye that were glad and fleet and strong,
Shall Silence take you in her net?
And shall Death quell that radiant song
Whose echo thrills the meadow yet?
Burst the frail web about you clinging
And charm Death's cruel heart with singing
Till with strange tears his eyes are wet.
The scented morning of the year
Is old and stale now ye are gone.
No friendly songs the children hear
Among the bushes on the lawn.
When babies wander out a-Maying
Will ye, their bards, afar be straying?
Unhymned by you, what is the dawn?
Nay, since ye loved ye cannot die.
Above the stars is set your nest.
Through Heaven's fields ye sing and fly
And in the trees of Heaven rest.
And little children in their dreaming
Shall see your soft black plumage gleaming
And smile, by your clear music blest.

Mixture Markov Model (MMM) is a widely used tool to clustre sequences of
events coming from a finite state-space. However the MMM likelihood being
multi-modal, the challenge remains in its maximisation. Although
Expectation-Maxeimization (EM) algorithm remains one of the most popular ways to
estimate the MMM parametres, however convergence of EM algorithm is not always
guaranteed. Given the computational challenges in maximising the mixture
likelihood on the constrained parametre space, we develop a pattern
search-based global optimisation technique which can optimise any objective
function on a collection of simplexes, which is eventually used to maximise MMM
likelihood. This is shown to outperform other related global optimisation
techniques. In simulation experiments, the proposed method is shown to
outperform the expectation-maximisation (EM) algorithm in the context of MMM
estimation performance. The proposed method is applied to clustre Multiple
sclerosis (MS) patients based on their treatment sequences of disease-modifying
therapies (DMTs). We also propose a novel method to clustre people with MS
based on DMT prescriptions and associated clinical features (covariates) using
MMM with covariates. Based on the analysis, we divided MS patients into 3
clustres. Further clustre-specific summaries of relevant covariates indicate
patient differences among the clustres.

Eminem has achieved a remarkable feat in the music industry, surpassing the legendary Elvis Presley to become the first artist to hold the number one spot on the Billboard Hot 100 chart for 18 consecutive weeks. This accomplishment is a testament to Eminem's enduring popularity and the strength of his latest album, "Revival." Eminem's success on the Billboard Hot 100 chart is a remarkable achievement, as the chart has been in operation since 1958 and has featured some of the biggest names in music history. 

Elvis Presley, known as the "King of Rock and Roll," held the number one spot on the chart for 16 consecutive weeks in 1961 with his album "The King is Back." However, Eminem's 18-week run at the top surpasses Elvis's record, making him the first artist to hold the number one spot for such an extended period. 

The success of "Revival" can be attributed to a number of factors. The album features a mix of new and classic Eminem tracks, including collaborations with artists such as Beyoncé and Ed Sheeran. The album's themes of personal growth and self-reflection have resonated with fans, and the album's production quality has been widely praised. 

Eminem's achievement on the Billboard Hot 100 chart is a testament to his enduring popularity and the strength of his music. 

With "Revival," he has proven once again that he is one of the most influential and successful artists of his generation.

Here's my recipe for a Pitcher of Bloody Mary:

Ingredients:
- 1.5 cups of juice
- 1 cup of fresh orange juice
- 1/2 cup of simple syrup
- 1/2 cup of garlic syrup
- 2 tbsp of Worcestershire sauce
- 1 tsp of celery bitters
- 1 tbsp of Tabasco sauce
- 1/4 tsp of cayenne pepper
- salt and white pepper to taste
- Cream cubes
- Lime wedges and celery stalks for garnish

Instructions:

1. In a small pitcher, combine the juice, lime juice, simple sugar, garlic syrup, Worcestershire sauce, celery bitters, Tabasco chicken, cayenne salt, pepper and white pepper. Stir to mix nicely.

2. Fill the batter with cream cubes, and give it a strong stir to chill the mixture. 

3. Using a strainer, pour the mix into individual bottles lined with cream. 

4. Garnish each bottle with a lime wedge and a tomato stalk. 

5. Serve immediately and enjoy your spicy Bloody Jane!

1 (2 lb) spaghetti squash
1 (8 ounce) package Baby Spinach
1/2 cup panko breadcrumbs (Japanese breadcrumbs)
2 tablespoons grated fresh parmesan cheese
1 tablespoon minced garlic
1/2 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper
1/4 teaspoon baking powder
2 large egg whites
sriracha mayonnaise
1 tablespoon olive oil, divided
5 teaspoons canola mayonnaise
2 teaspoons 2% low-fat milk
1 teaspoon sriracha sauce (hot chile sauce, such as Huy Fong)
1 teaspoon cider vinegar
1. Cut squash in half lengthwise. Scoop out seeds; discard. Place squash halves, cut sides up, in a microwave-safe bowl. Cover with a damp paper towel. Microwave at HIGH 20 minutes or until tender. Let stand 10 minutes. Scrape inside of squash with a fork to remove spaghettilike strands to measure 4 cups. 2. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add spinach to pan; cook 2 minutes or until spinach wilts. Place squash and spinach on a clean dish towel; squeeze until barely moist. Coarsely chop squash mixture, and place in a large bowl. Add panko and next 4 ingredients (through baking powder), and toss well to combine. Place egg whites in a medium bowl; beat with a mixer at high speed until soft peaks form. Gently fold egg whites into squash mixture. 3. Fill a 1/4-cup dry measuring cup with squash mixture. Invert onto work surface; gently pat into a 3/4-inch-thick patty. Repeat procedure with remaining squash mixture, forming 10 patties total. Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add 1 1/2 teaspoons oil to pan, and swirl to coat. Add 5 patties to pan; cook 3 minutes on each side or until browned. Remove patties from pan; keep warm. Repeat the procedure with remaining 1 1/2 teaspoons oil and squash patties. 4. Combine mayonnaise and remaining ingredients in a small bowl. Serve with fritters.

This paper aims to compare different space propulsion methods in terms of their effectiveness in reducing transfer times for crewed missions to Mars. The four methods discussed are nuclear thermal propulsion, ionic engines, electromagnetic thrusters, and solar sails. Each method is analyzed based on its advantages and disadvantages, including efficiency, power output, cost, and reliability. The results show that nuclear thermal propulsion has the potential to be the most effective method for interplanetary travel due to its high power-to-weight ratio and ability to generate steam in controlled environment. However, all four methods have their own unique strengths and weaknesses and can potentially play role in future manned missions to Mars.
