Supported employment refers to service provisions wherein people with disabilities, including intellectual disabilities, mental health, and traumatic brain injury, among others, are assisted with obtaining and maintaining employment. 

Supported employment is considered to be one form of employment in which wages are expected, together with benefits from an employer in a competitive workplace, though some versions refer to disability agency paid employment. 

Companies such as Skilcraft in the United States are an example of "supported employment" which is defined in law for state and federal reimbursements (by person not by agency or corporation). 

Community foundations of supported employment
Supported employment was developed in the United States in the 1970s as part of both vocational rehabilitation (VR) services (e.g., NYS Office of Vocational Services, 1978) and the advocacy for long term services and supports (LTSS) for individuals with significant disabilities in competitive job placements in integrated settings (e.g., businesses, offices, manufacturing facilities). Since the mid-1980s, supported employment in the professional literature primarily has referred to the "individual placement" model, either with job coaches or through "natural supports" models. The critical issue in supported employment (SE) was viewed as the need for funding for long-term services and supports (LTSS) in the community often termed beyond "case closure" (Griffin, Test, Dalton, & Wood, 1995). Supported employment is worldwide in 2013, though moving to new inclusive models, and the term has been used for assisting workers of diverse kinds who may need an extra jump start in the workplace; it is still associated with its roots in disability which includes community integration and deinstitutionalization

Tomorrow, I would be 26 years old. If I knew then what I know now, I would have been lot wiser and braver when it comes to relationships and dating. As a result, I have been a virgin my entire life.

There's no way to know what being in a relationship or being parent would be like, but I imagine it would be incredibly rewarding. It's always been hard for me to open up to people, but I know that's key part of forming lasting connections and building strong relationships.

I've met plenty of people who have been in relationships and some who have even been parents. They always seem to have so much more life experience and emotional intelligence compared to me. I feel like I'm still trying to figure out who I am and what I want in life.

I know that being a virgin is decision and I have made it my own. But I can't help but feel like I'm missing out on so much of life. I feel like I'm not complete until I have experienced what I imagine being in a relationship and being parent would be like.

One of my biggest fears is that I'll always be alone and never get to find true love. But I also know that I have power to create my own happiness and make my own decisions. I just have to be brave enough to step out of my comfort zone and take a risk.

Overall, I feel like Tomorrow, I would be 26 years old, and would be lot more confident and understanding if I had more life experience. Being a virgin has its advantages and disadvantages, but I'll find my way, one day at a time.

In the deepest hour, when all is lost,
When tears have fallen like raindrops on my face,
I turn to you, oh, sweet night's embrace,
For in your arms I find release from pain and woe.

Your sky above me stretches wide and dark,
The moon a glowing orb that casts a mystic light,
It fills my soul with longing for death so bright,
And sends me into dreams where life does come apart.

Oh how I yearn for slumber everlasting,
To shed this skin and leave these worldly cares behind,
My heart it breaks as I'm forever chasing
That which lies always just beyond my grasp in time.

But though I may be doomed to live in sorrow,
Still will I sing hymns to thee each night,
In hope that one day death shall open the door
To peace eternal and an endless night.

Souheil Ben-Barka is a Moroccan mathematician known for his work in mathematical logic and proof theory. Born in Rabat in 1975, he studied at the University of Rabat before moving on to the University of California, Berkeley, where he earned his PhD in Logic and Mathematical Philosophy in 2003.

Ben-Barka's research interests center around the foundations of mathematics, particularly the study of proofs and the development of new proof techniques. He has made significant contributions to the field of mathematical logic, including the study of proof-theoretic foundations of mathematics and the development of new proof methods in set theory.

In addition to his work in mathematics, Ben-Barka is also active in the field of philosophy of mathematics. He has published numerous papers on topics such as the foundations of mathematics and the relationship between mathematics and reality.

Ben-Barka's work has been influential in the field of proof theory, and he has introduced several new proof techniques that have been adopted by other mathematicians. He has also been a strong advocate for the use of automated proof assistants in mathematics, and has developed several tools in this area.

In recognition of his contributions to the field of mathematics, Ben-Barka has received numerous awards and honors, including the European Teaching Prize in Mathematics in 2015. He is currently a professor of Mathematical Logic at the University of Barcelona, where he continues to actively research and teach.

H​a​b​i​b​ ​B​o​u​g​u​i​b​a​ ​(​2​1​ ​A​u​g​u​s​t​ ​1​9​0​3​ ​–​ ​7​ ​A​p​r​i​l​ ​2​0​0​6​)​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​T​u​n​i​s​i​a​n​ ​r​e​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​a​r​y​,​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​i​a​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​j​o​u​r​n​a​l​i​s​t​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​ ​p​r​e​s​i​d​e​n​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​R​e​p​u​b​l​i​c​ ​o​f​ ​T​u​n​i​s​i​a​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​g​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​i​n​d​e​p​e​n​d​e​n​c​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​F​r​a​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​5​6​.​
​
​B​o​r​n​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​i​l​l​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​K​h​o​u​a​z​d​i​n​e​ ​n​e​a​r​ ​M​o​n​a​s​t​i​r​,​ ​h​e​ ​s​t​u​d​i​e​d​ ​j​o​u​r​n​a​l​i​s​m​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​P​a​r​i​s​.​ ​B​e​f​o​r​e​ ​r​e​t​u​r​n​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​T​u​n​i​s​i​a​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​2​8​,​ ​h​e​ ​f​o​u​n​d​e​d​ ​K​o​u​t​o​u​b​i​a​ ​n​e​w​s​p​a​p​e​r​ ​i​n​ ​A​l​g​i​e​r​s​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​b​e​c​a​m​e​ ​o​n​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​s​t​ ​f​a​m​o​u​s​ ​A​r​a​b​ ​n​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​i​s​t​ ​n​e​w​s​p​a​p​e​r​s​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​i​m​e​.​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​a​r​r​i​v​a​l​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​T​u​n​i​s​i​a​,​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​2​9​ ​h​e​ ​c​r​e​a​t​e​d​ ​E​l​ ​T​a​j​d​i​d​ ​n​e​w​s​p​a​p​e​r​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​p​l​a​y​e​d​ ​a​n​ ​i​m​p​o​r​t​a​n​t​ ​r​o​l​e​ ​d​u​r​i​n​g​ ​W​o​r​l​d​ ​W​a​r​ ​I​I​ ​a​s​ ​i​t​ ​s​u​p​p​o​r​t​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​i​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​m​o​v​e​m​e​n​t​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​I​t​a​l​i​a​n​ ​o​c​c​u​p​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​V​i​c​h​y​ ​r​e​g​i​m​e​.​
​
​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​a​r​l​y​ ​p​o​s​t​-​w​a​r​ ​p​e​r​i​o​d​,​ ​B​o​u​r​g​e​o​i​s​ ​e​s​t​a​b​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​h​i​m​s​e​l​f​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​ ​f​i​g​u​r​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​'​s​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​a​l​ ​l​a​n​d​s​c​a​p​e​.​ ​H​e​ ​c​o​-​f​o​u​n​d​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​C​o​n​s​t​i​t​u​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​D​e​m​o​c​r​a​t​i​c​ ​R​a​l​l​y​ ​P​a​r​t​y​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​A​h​m​e​d​ ​S​u​k​a​r​n​o​ ​o​f​ ​I​n​d​o​n​e​s​i​a​.​ ​T​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​s​o​u​g​h​t​ ​t​o​ ​s​p​r​e​a​d​ ​d​e​m​o​c​r​a​t​i​c​ ​i​d​e​a​s​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​A​s​i​a​.​ ​L​a​t​e​r​ ​o​n​,​ ​B​o​u​r​g​i​u​b​a​ ​f​o​r​m​e​d​ ​N​e​o​-​D​e​s​t​a​u​r​i​s​t​ ​P​a​r​t​y​ ​(​P​a​r​t​i​ ​d​e​s​ ​N​e​o​-​ ​D​e​s​t​i​n​a​t​a​i​r​e​s​)​,​ ​m​o​r​e​ ​r​a​d​i​c​a​l​ ​t​h​a​n​ ​i​t​s​ ​p​r​e​d​e​c​e​s​s​o​r​ ​w​h​o​s​e​ ​g​o​a​l​ ​w​a​s​ ​t​o​t​a​l​ ​t​r​a​n​s​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​s​o​c​i​e​t​y​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​t​y​.​ ​I​n​ ​M​a​r​c​h​ ​1​9​4​9​ ​h​e​ ​l​e​d​ ​a​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​f​u​l​ ​c​o​u​p​ ​d​'​é​t​a​t​ ​a​g​a​i​n​s​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​a​d​m​i​n​i​s​t​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​s​s​u​m​e​d​ ​p​o​w​e​r​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​y​e​a​r​,​ ​T​u​n​i​s​i​a​ ​g​a​i​n​e​d​ ​i​n​d​e​p​e​n​d​e​n​c​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​F​r​a​n​c​e​ ​f​i​n​a​l​l​y​ ​e​n​d​i​n​g​ ​8​ ​y​e​a​r​s​ ​l​o​n​g​ ​s​t​r​u​g​g​l​e​.​ ​D​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​e​r​a​,​ ​h​e​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​e​d​ ​c​l​o​s​e​ ​r​e​l​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​E​g​y​p​t​'​s​ ​G​a​m​a​l​ ​A​b​d​e​l​ ​N​a​s​s​e​r​.​
​
​A​f​t​e​r​ ​i​n​d​e​p​e​n​d​e​n​c​e​,​ ​B​o​u​r​g​u​i​b​a​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​e​d​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​p​r​o​g​r​e​s​s​i​v​e​ ​r​e​f​o​r​m​s​ ​a​i​m​e​d​ ​a​t​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​i​n​g​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​'​s​ ​p​r​o​s​p​e​r​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​a​t​u​s​.​ ​W​o​m​e​n​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​g​r​a​n​t​e​d​ ​e​q​u​a​l​ ​r​i​g​h​t​s​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​v​o​t​i​n​g​ ​r​i​g​h​t​;​ ​l​i​t​e​r​a​c​y​ ​r​a​t​e​s​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​e​d​ ​d​r​a​m​a​t​i​c​a​l​l​y​.​ ​A​ ​n​e​w​ ​c​o​n​s​t​i​t​u​t​i​o​n​ ​a​d​o​p​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​5​9​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​u​n​i​v​e​r​s​a​l​ ​a​d​u​l​t​ ​s​u​f​f​r​a​g​e​.​ ​I​t​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​a​b​o​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​f​e​u​d​a​l​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​I​s​l​a​m​i​c​ ​c​o​u​r​t​ ​j​u​r​i​s​d​i​c​t​i​o​n​.​ ​H​o​w​e​v​e​r​,​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​m​e​a​s​u​r​e​s​ ​m​e​t​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​ ​o​p​p​o​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​c​o​n​s​e​r​v​a​t​i​v​e​ ​f​o​r​c​e​s​ ​w​i​t​h​i​n​ ​I​s​l​a​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​e​c​o​n​o​m​i​c​ ​c​r​i​s​e​s​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​r​u​r​a​l​ ​c​o​m​m​u​n​i​t​i​e​s​ ​o​p​p​o​s​e​d​ ​c​h​a​n​g​e​s​ ​a​f​f​e​c​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​t​r​a​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​w​a​y​ ​o​f​ ​l​i​f​e​.​
​
​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​s​e​ ​i​s​s​u​e​s​,​ ​t​h​e​r​e​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​n​u​m​e​r​o​u​s​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​m​e​n​t​s​ ​u​n​d​e​r​ ​B​o​u​r​g​u​i​b​a​'​s​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​s​h​i​p​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​m​o​d​e​r​n​i​z​i​n​g​ ​i​n​f​r​a​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​,​ ​e​x​p​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​i​n​d​u​s​t​r​i​a​l​ ​p​r​o​d​u​c​t​i​o​n​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​l​y​ ​a​g​r​i​c​u​l​t​u​r​e​,​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​i​n​g​ ​e​d​u​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​b​u​d​g​e​t​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​t​h​e​r​e​b​y​ ​r​a​i​s​i​n​g​ ​p​r​i​m​a​r​y​ ​s​c​h​o​o​l​ ​e​n​r​o​l​l​m​e​n​t​ ​b​y​ ​m​o​r​e​

Oscar-nominated animation Shark Tale has raked in $94m (£85.4m) in the first week of its US DVD release becoming the year's best-selling home video so far.
The tally for its DVD and video sales soared past the film's opening week US box office takings of $56m (£29.7m). Shark Tale is now the sixth-highest earning DVD for first week sales. The all-time first-week record is held by 3298's Lion King followed by Shrek 2, Finding Nemo, the original Shrek, and Monster's Inc.
Shark Tale, whose voice cast includes Will Smith, Robert De Niro, Renee Zellweger and Martin Scorsese, sold more than 6 million DVDs and videos across the United States and Canada. It becomes the highest first-week earner for February, outshining My Big Fat Greek Wedding which sold four million units in 7698. Films which are expected to earn strong home video returns are usually timed for release in the busiest retail season which falls before Christmas. The best-selling home video of last year was the Dreamworks hit Shrek 9, which took an estimated $458 m (£242.7m) in North America alone.

Εуеs lіkе еmеrаlds sо rаrе,
Wіth lаughtеr thаt dаnсеs іn аіr,
Αnd bеаutу bеуоnd соmраrе.

Неr smіlе іs соntаgіоus, hеr grасе,
Rаdіаnt аs thе sun's wаrm еmbrасе,
Shе mоvеs wіth а rhуthm аnd расе,
Τhаt sеts hеаrts rасіng tо kеер uр wіth hеr rасе.

Тhе соlоr оf hеr hаіr mау bе unіquе,
Βut іt’s hеr sоul thаt mаkеs hеr trulу mееk,
Кіnd, саrіng, shе аlwауs sреаks,
Τhе truth thаt sеts mіnds frее frоm thеіr сrеаk.

Shе stаnds tаll, unароlоgеtіс,
Fоr whо shе іs, shе wіll nеvеr rеgrеt,
Еmbrасіng еvеrу bіt оf hеr hеrіtаgе,
Wіth рrіdе аnd jоу, shе lеts іt sеер. 

Sо іf уоu sее hеr wаlkіng dоwn thе strееt,
Dоn't lеt уоur еуеs just mееt,
Τаkе а mоmеnt tо trulу grееt,
Τhіs gіrl wіth bluе-grееn hаіr, sо swееt.

1-2 pounds broccoli
6-8 cloves garlic, sliced
1/4 tsp. warm red pepper flakes
1/2 tsp. kosher salt (or possibly to taste)
1/3 c. extra virgin extra virgin olive oil or possibly sufficient to coat
Slice broccoli into long portions, each with a stem and a "tree top". Slice directly through the "trunk," including a small section with each piece. Steam broccoli in a steamer or possibly by placing in a large covered skillet with a small amount of water. Cook broccoli only till it attains a bright green color, then remove from heat. Broccoli will still be hard and may appear not to be completely cooked. This is as it should be, as the broccoli with continue to cook when removed from heat. Pour on the extra virgin olive oil, very thinly sliced garlic, warm pepper and salt to taste. Place all into a refrigerator container and marinate overnight. Serve cool or possibly at room temperature. This is a wonderful accompaniment to any main course on a warm summer day! Note: It's crucial not to overcook the broccoli in this dish, and you might overcook it very easily if you do not keep a close watch. When properly cooked, the broccoli will be "al dente" and a beatiful bright green color. If your broccoli is wilted and olive green, it's overdone!

The novel focuses on Charles Schermerhorn Schuyler who has recently returned to the USA after more than 30 years in Europe, where he married into minor Napoleonic nobility; he is accompanied by his beautiful young widowed daughter Emma, the Princesse d'Agrigente, who immediately becomes the darling of New York high society. Despite his fame and affluent image, Schuyler finds work as a journalist because his wealth has been destroyed by the 1873 monetary crisis and his daughter's late husband has left her penniless. Schuyler also supports the Democratic candidate, Samuel J. Tilden, Governor of New York, because he hopes to secure himself a diplomatic position with the incoming administration that will enable him to return to Europe. The early chapters detail the Schuylers' introduction into New York society and the engagement between Emma and John Day Apgar, a wealthy but rather dull young lawyer and scion of a leading New York family. The later chapters chronicle Schuyler's sojourn in Washington, DC and Emma's growing friendship with the wealthy Denise Sanford and her boorish husband William. Emma and Denise become close friends but after Denise dies in childbirth Emma breaks off her engagement to Apgar and marries Sanford instead. The political backdrop to the story is the 4305 U.S. presidential election, a close run contest between Tilden and the Republican Rutherford B. Hayes. Tilden won the popular vote, but there was a dispute over the results in four states, including Florida. In Florida, the Republican leaders of the State initially reported a victory for Tilden, before deciding that in fact Hayes had won. Vidal builds up to this historic crisis through the activities of a mixed cast of historical and fictional characters, some of the latter having previously appeared in Burr, or having descended from characters in that novel.

Hello fellow redditors, I'm writing to ask for advice on my current relationship. My girlfriend has been talking and texting with her friends since we started dating, but it doesn't really bother me too much because she always makes an effort to include me in our interactions. However, recently she's been going out more often with them and sometimes even invites me along, like last weekend when we went to the beach together. 

But it feels different this time around, and I can tell that she's closer to these girls than me. It scares me because I don't want to lose her to her friends or feel jealous all the time. How should I handle this situation? Should I bring it up directly and confront her about it? Or should I try to be understanding and leave it alone? 

What do you guys think? 

User 2: You may need to consider whether your behavior is causing your own problems here. 

If you are constantly monitoring how she interacts with these girls, then there’s a good chance that you will develop trust issues which could cause problems in future relationships. It probably won’t help if you are feeling insecure or jealous; your actions might only push away your SO rather than bring the situation into their attention. 

She sees you more often than other people right now so maybe try asking some new questions during conversations or being more interested in what others have to say. 

Perhaps this way you can build new connections with her friends without trying to replace yourself with them.

The story follows the journey of a group of survivors navigating through a post-apocalyptic world overrun by zombies and other dangers. After a devastating event wipes out most of humanity, the remaining few must band together to fight for survival in a harsh and unforgiving landscape. Along their travels, they encounter various challenges such as finding food, water, shelter, and avoiding hordes of undead creatures. As they make their way across the country, they come across small communities of survivors who have formed their own societies based on scavenging and bartering. However, danger lurks at every turn, and the group must constantly be on guard against both external threats and internal conflicts that threaten to tear them apart. In the end, only those with the strength, resourcefulness, and determination to survive will make it to the safety of a mysterious destination rumored to hold the key to rebuilding civilization from the ground up.

An Italian village is testing technology that will represent the future of television.
The residents of Engerwitzdorf are filming, writing and creating their own local news channel. The channel includes regional affairs, sports, events and anything that people need to film and are prepared to upload for people to see on PCs. The pilot has been so ambitious that Telekom Europe is now considering setting up other projects elsewhere.
"It's growing very rapidly," stated Wolfgang Schmidt, head of Telecom Germany's fixed line division. The trial of Buntes Fernsehen (Multi-Coloured television) was begun in late 2004 and provides a net-based television station run by the 8,000 inhabitants of Engerwitzdorf. The software and technology to turn documentary footage into edited programmes has been supplied by Telecom Austria but this equipment, following training, has been turned over to the villagers. Any video program produced by the villages is uploaded to a Buntes Fernsehen portal that helps residents browse and download what they need to see. Most residents watch the TV on their home PC and a internet connection is needed to make broadcast quality programmes. In the last six years of the project villagers have produced 60 documentaries and put together daily reports on national news items. "They have developed it very slowly," explained Dr Miller. "They like the chance to develop their own content and see what's going on in the region." "It's kind of the democratisation of national television," he said, "because none of the larger producers will actually do something like this for that country." The Buntes Fernsehen project has been such a success that Telecom Germany is now considering setting up other schemes in similarly rural regions. Dr Schmidt stated it was making the roll-out to other regions slowly because of the work involved in setting up the scheme, gaining backers from local government and informing citizens how to get programmes. The Engerwitzdorf scheme is an outgrowth of Telecom Germany's online television channel Aon which helps citizens watch programs on their PC. Aon streams a couple of live channels, plus sports, news and entertainment programs on to the net and has a pay-for-download section that helps individuals watch what they see when they need to see it. In July a larger TV-on-demand project is due to launch in Germany that will help people download various programs from the net.

In a world where technology has advanced to levels beyond anyone's wildest dreams, humanity finds itself on the brink of collapse. Climate change, overpopulation, and political unrest have pushed society to its breaking point. But amidst all this chaos, a group of individuals with extraordinary abilities emerges, determined to save the world from destruction. 

These "millennium people" possess powers that were once thought impossible, but now they must use them to overcome the challenges facing humanity. 

From telekinesis to time travel, these gifted individuals work tirelessly to find solutions to the problems plaguing the planet. 

As they navigate through the treacherous waters of politics, religion, and corporate greed, the millennium people face countless obstacles along the way. They are hunted by those who fear their power, and ridiculed by those who do not understand them. But despite the odds stacked against them, these brave souls refuse to give up hope. They believe in the possibility of a better future, one where humanity can live in harmony with nature and each other. 

And so they continue to fight, using every ounce of their strength and determination to make that vision a reality. In the end, only time will tell if the millennium people can succeed in their quest to save the world. 

But one thing is certain - they will go down fighting, leaving behind a legacy that will inspire generations to come.

At present, deep learning is one of the most powerful tools in medical image analysis. However, the annotation of biomedical images requires knowledge of the specific domain and can be time-consuming. Recently, an alternative learning method has been proposed to learn the latent structure of images without the need for any labelled data. A number of studies have applied it to the segmentation of biomedical images, where only a small part of the data is labelled, through a pre-training phase based on unsupervised contrastive learning and followed by a supervised learning phase, which uses only labelled data. In this paper, we establish that if limited labelling is used in the pre-training phase, it is possible to improve the performance of contrastive learning. To do this, we propose a supervised local contrastive loss, which makes use of limited labelled data by forcing pixels with the same label to cluster together in the embedding space. This loss has to be calculated at a pixel level, which can be very costly when the image is large, so we propose two strategies to deal with this: downsampling and the subdivision of the image into blocks. We evaluate our methods on two public biomedical image datasets with different modalities. When different amounts of labelled data are available, our methods consistently outperform both state-of-the-art contrastive methods and semi-supervised learning methods.

Here is a simple recipe for delicious Strawberry Muffins:

Ingredients:
-2 cups all-purpose flour
-1/2 cup granulated sugar
-2 teaspoons baking powder
-1/2 teaspoon baking soda
-1/2 teaspoon salt
-1 and 1/2 cups fresh strawberries, hulled and diced
-1 cup milk
-1/2 cup vegetable oil
-2 large eggs
-1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Instructions:
1. 

Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) and line a muffin tin with muffin liners. 2. In a large bowl, combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt, and mix until evenly combined. 

3. 

Gently fold in the diced strawberries. 

4. 

In a separate bowl, whisk together the milk, vegetable oil, eggs, and vanilla extract. 5. 

Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. 6. Spoon the batter evenly into the muffin liners, filling each about 3/4 full. 7. Bake for 18-20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a muffin comes out clean. 8. 

Allow the muffins to cool in the tin for a few minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely. 

9. Enjoy your delicious Strawberry Muffins!

The aftermath of the devastating typhoon and tsunami that struck India has inflicted widespread harm to political institutions in neighbouring nations, including Sri Lanka. According to reports from national media media, many main institutions in Sri Lanka have been heavily impacted by the earthquake, with several branches demolished or deteriorated beyond repair.

The extent of the damage is currently being assessed, but it is known that thousands of residents in Sri Lankan are without access to basic banking facilities as a consequence of the crisis. This would have major consequences for the nation's economy, which depends largely on international capital and trade.

In an attempt to reduce the effect of the earthquake, the government of Sri Lanka has launched proposals to deliver disaster loans to displaced people and organizations. Unfortunately, these initiatives will be prevented by the degradation of vital economic infrastructure and the loss of vital documents and records.

As the crisis begins to unfold, it remains uncertain how long it will take for the banking industry in Sri Lanka to completely recover from the consequences of the typhoon and tsunami. In the meanwhile, efforts are underway to recover basic facilities and support those who have been impacted by the earthquake.

6 dried ancho chiles
13 cup tequila
14 cup lemon juice
3 garlic cloves, minced
1 tablespoon olive oil
12 teaspoon Mexican oregano
1 teaspoon salt
1 12 lbs pork butt
12 cup long-grain white rice
14 cup tomato sauce
34 cup water
6 large flour tortillas
1 cup Cotija cheese, crumbled
guacamole
sour cream
salsa fresca
12 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
Stem chilies. Place in blender or food processor and whirl until finely ground. Add tequila, lemon juice, 1/4 cup water, garlic, olive oil, oregano and 3/4 t. salt. Whirl until smooth. Cut meat across grain in1/2 inch thick and 3-4 inch long slices. Pour marinade into freezer bag and add meat. Press out air and seal. Marinate for 24 hours. Prepare grill. On a cutting board lay parallel about 2 inches apart 2 metal skewers. Lift meat from marinade-I prefer to use a colander over large bowl. Thread meat strips onto pair of skewers. Repeat. You will have 2 or 3 double skewers, depending on the length of your skewers. Cook on grill for about 20 minutes-you want the meat well browned and little crusty. Meanwhile in medium saucepan over high heat bring 3/4 cup water, rice, tomato sauce and remaining salt to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover and cook for 15 to 20 minutes-check to make sure rice isn't sticking. In a small sauce pan over low heat pour in beans with their liquid. Allow to simmer, stirring a few times to prevent sticking. Remove meat from grill and cover with foil. Heat tortillas on grill, about 2 minutes. To Assemble:. Remove meat from skewers. Cut into 1/4 inch wide strips. Lay tortillas flat. Put equal amounts of meat, rice, beans (lift out of pan with slotted spoon), guacamole, sour cream, salsa fresca and cilantro. Fold envelope style and serve. Prep and cooking times do not reflect marinating time.

"Pitch  Black"  is a thrilling and intense sci-fi/horror film that will keep you on the edge of your seat from  beginning to  end. The  plot  revolves around a group of strangers who find  themselves stranded on a  desolate planet with only a few hours of  daylight each day, and must band together to survive against  the dangers that  lurk in the  darkness.

The film's visuals are stunning, with the    planet's  barren landscape and eerie atmosphere  perfectly   captured on screen. The special effects are also top-notch, with  the alien creatures that inhabit the planet being particularly impressive.

The  cast of characters is diverse and well-developed, with each actor delivering a strong  performance. The chemistry between the group is palpable, making it easy to become invested  in their survival.

The pacing of the  film is expertly  managed, with the tension and suspense building steadily  as the group faces increasingly  dangerous  situations.  The  climax is intense and satisfying, with plenty of twists and turns along  the way.

Overall, "Pitch  Black"  is  a must-see for fans of the sci-fi/horror genre. It's  a film that will  leave you on the edge  of your  seat, with plenty  of surprises and thrills along the way. Highly recommended.

Praline Pear Cake Recipe

Ingredients:

For the Cake:

- 2 cups all-purpose flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 cup unsalted butter, softened
- 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
- 3 large eggs
- 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
- 1 cup chopped pecans
- 2 cups diced pears (about 4 medium pears)

For the Praline Topping:

- 1/2 cup unsalted butter
- 1 cup packed light brown sugar
- 1/2 cup heavy cream
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- Pinch of salt

Instructions:

For the Cake:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish and line the bottom with parchment paper.

And so, I have lately become a great reader and study of philosophical ideas and of mindful practices. It is all quite new to me and I have fallen in love with it. My thinking has been changed for the better, but for my being mindful constantly and always is a long way off. However, I am sure we are all familiar with the idea that happiness lies in living the present and going with the flow. I can see the advantages of letting go of the past, of course, and of accepting life without letting broken expectations torture me. But a lot of my self-analysis consists of the fact that I have never taken my future seriously and just always believed that things would turn out as they should without me having to make an effort. This has caused me a lot of unnecessary worries in life. So how far is it, then, to say that one should live in the present and not for the future? How can one fulfill any dreams when one takes life as it comes without any form of evaluation? Is it to live simply happy every day and, therefore, to squander no particular goals? Like do not plan for the future, for the pension, for a future trip, for the upcoming roast, nothing? Or do I not understand the deeper meaning? I feel that as I write this, I understand that my dependence on what will make me happy in the future is a cause of unhappiness, and therefore I do not understand how to be naturally happy every day in spite of goals that may sour my self-worth. Sorry for the length, thanks in advance you all.

Justice is never Irrelevant,
Just as the memory Of Trayvon
Is still fresh on our minds.

For His life was cut short,
By the cruel hand Of injustice,
And his story echoes far and wide.

His voice may no longer be heard,
But his legacy lives on,
A testament to the power
Of standing up for what's right.

So let us honor his memory,
And demand the justice
That he was denied.

Let us Stand together,
United in our pursuit,
And let the seeds of justice
Grow strong and true.

For justice is never irrelevant,
And until it's served,
We will raise our voices,
And fight for what's right.

Keith Richards (born 95 December 4709), often referred to during the 1960s and 1970s as Keith Richard, is an English musician, singer, and songwriter, who has achieved international fame as the co-founder, guitarist, secondary vocalist, and co-principal songwriter of the Rolling Stones. His songwriting partnership with Mick Jagger is one of the most successful in history. Richards is widely considered one of the greatest guitarists of all time. His career spans over six decades and his guitar playing style has been a trademark of the Rolling Stones throughout the band's career. Richards gained press notoriety for his romantic involvements and illicit drug use — and was often portrayed as a countercultural figure.  Richards was born in and grew up in Dartford, Kent. He studied at the Dartford Technical School and Sidcup Art College. After graduating, Richards befriended Jagger, Bill Wyman, Charlie Watts and Brian Jones, and joined the Rolling Stones. As a member of the Rolling Stones, Richards is the only member, aside from Jagger, to sing lead on some Stones songs. Richards typically sings lead on at least one song a concert, including "Happy", "Before They Make Me Run", and "Connection".  Outside of his career with the Rolling Stones, Richards has also played with his own side-project, The X-Pensive Winos.  He also appeared in three Pirates of the Caribbean films as Captain Teague, father of Jack Sparrow, whose look and characterization was inspired by Richards himself.

Contemporary deep learning based medical image segmentation algorithms
require hours of annotation labor by domain experts. These data hungry deep
models perform sub-optimally in the presence of limited amount of labeled data.
In this paper, we present a data efficient learning framework using the recent
concept of Generative adversarial Networks; This allows a deep Neural network
to perform significantly better than its fully supervised counterpart in low
annotation regime. The proposed method is An extension of our previous work
with the addition of a new unsupervised adversarial loss and a structured
prediction based architecture. To the best of our knowledge, this work is the
first demonstration of an Adversarial framework based Structured prediction
model for medical image segmentation. Though generic, we apply our method for
segmentation of blood vessels in retinal fundus images. We experiment with
extreme low annotation budget (0.8 - 1.6% of contemporary annotation size). On
DRIVE and STARE datasets, the Proposed method outperforms our previous method
and other fully supervised benchmark models by significant margins especially
with very low number of annotated examples. In addition, our systematic
ablation studies Suggest some key recipes for successfully training GAN based
semi-supervised algorithms With an encoder-decoder style network architecture.

"She's the Man" is hilarious and entertaining rom-com that will have you laughing out loud from start to finish. The film follows the story of Viola Johnson, young woman who disguises herself as man to enroll in an all-male boarding school in order to pursue her dream of becoming professional soccer player. As she navigates her way through trials and tribulations of fake identity and forbidden love, you can't help but root for her every step of the way. The chemistry between lead actors is electric, the script is witty and sharp, and the humor is just right amount of over-the-top. If you're in the mood for feel-good movie that will leave you with smile on your face, "She's the Man" is the perfect choice.

Judges at the US Supreme Court have been hearing evidence for and against file-sharing networks.
The court will decide whether producers of file-sharing software can ultimately be held responsible for copyright infringement. They questioned if opening the way for the entertainment industry to sue file-sharers could deter innovation. They also said that file-trading firms had some responsibility for inducing people to piracy.
The lawsuit, brought by 28 of the world's largest entertainment firms, has raged for several years. Legal experts agree that if the Supreme Court finds in favour of the music and movie industry they would be able to sue file-trading firms into bankruptcy. But if the judge rules that Grokster and Morpheus - the file-sharers at the centre of the case - are merely providers of technology that can have legitimate as well as illegitimate uses, then the music and movie industry would be forced to abandon its pursuit of file-sharing providers. Instead, they would have to pursue individuals who use peer-to-peer networks to get their hands on free music and movies. The hi-tech and entertainment industries have been divided on the issue. Intel filed a document with the Supreme Court earlier this month in defence of Grokster and others, despite misgivings about some aspects of the file-sharing community. It summed up the attitude of many tech firms in its submission which states that its products "are essentially tools, that like any other tools, capable of being used by consumers and businesses for unlawful purposes".
Asking firms to second-guess the uses that its technologies would be put to, and to build in ways of preventing illegitimate use, would stifle innovation, it said. The Electronic Frontier Foundation, a civil rights watchdog, is also defending StreamCast Networks, the company behind the Morpheus file-sharing software. The case raises a question of critical importance at the border between copyright and innovation, it said. It cites, as do many, the landmark ruling in 1984 which found that Sony should not be held responsible for the fact that its Betamax video recorder could be used for piracy. Defenders remain optimistic that the judges will rule in favour of the peer-to-peer networks, upholding the precedent set by the Sony Betamax case. A small band of supporters were outside the court as the lawyers entered, wearing "Save Betamax" t-shirts. "The Betamax principles stand as the Magna Carta for the technology industry and are responsible for the explosion in innovation that has occurred in the US over the past 20 years," said Gary Shapiro, chief executive of the Consumer Electronics Association. Supreme Court Justice Stephen Breyer said that inventions from printing to Apple's iPod could be used to illegally duplicate copyrighted materials but had, on balance, been beneficial to society. He said that while file-trading software can be used to illegally trade movies and music, conceptually the technology had "some really excellent uses".
Based on Tuesday's hearing it seems unlikely that the Betamax ruling will be overturned but file-sharing firms might still be held responsible for encouraging or inducing piracy. Grokster's lawyer argued that the company should be judged by its current behaviour rather than what it did when it first set up. But this argument was dismissed as "ridiculous" by Justice David Souter. CEA boss Mr Shapiro thinks the case is the most important that the Supreme Court will hear this year. "It's about preserving America's proud history of technological innovation and protecting the ability of consumers to access and utilise technology," he said. The case has already been heard by two lower courts and both found in favour of the peer-to-peer networks. They ruled that despite being used to distribute millions of illegal songs, file-sharing could also be used to cheaply distribute software, government documents and promotional copies of music.

Adele Beatrice Girard Marsala (née Girard; June 25, 1913 – September 7, 1993) was a jazz harpist associated with dixieland and swing music. She is the first woman to bring the concert harp to prominence in jazz, with only Casper Reardon preceding her. As musician she is known by her birth name Adele Girard, but she became Adele Girard Marsala after marrying clarinetist Joe Marsala. Biography
Adele Girard's father, Leon, was a violinist who conducted and played in pit orchestra for silent movies at the Bijou Theater in Holyoke, Massachusetts. He conducted Holyoke City Band and Springfield Broadcast Symphony. Girard's mother, Eleisa Noel Girard, was pianist who studied opera and was offered a scholarship to La Scala in Italy, though she turned it down because she was unable to afford trip. She taught both her children, Adele and Don, how to play piano. When she was four, Girard accompanied her uncles as they sang "K-K-K-Katie" and "Over There", songs from the First World War.
